HSAM 3016.507-6 
JUSTIFICATION FOR EXCEPTION TO FAIR OPPORTUNITY 
EXCEEDING THE SIMPLIFIED ACQUISITION THRESHOLD 
JEFO No.  FY26-00026  - 1 - 
 
 
JEFO No.: FY26-0049 
PR Number: PCCS-26-40001 
  
 
1. Agency and Contracting Activity.   
 
The Department of Homeland Security (DHS), Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency 
(CISA), Chief of Contracting Office (COCO) prepared this justification for an exception to fair 
opportunity. 
 
2. Nature and/or description of the action being approved. 
 
This Justification for an Exception to Fair Opportunity is prepared in accordance with Federal 
Acquisition Regulation (FAR) 16.507-6(d)(2), FAR Class Deviation 26-04, Revision 1 for FAR 
Part 16.
1  
 
3. A description of the supplies or services required to meet the agency’s need (including the 
estimated value). 
 
The CISA Cybersecurity Division, Vulnerability Management sub-division requires continued 
Cyber Hygiene (CyHy) services that aid in strengthening the cybersecurity posture, limiting 
exposure, and reducing exploitation rates for over 12,500 customers across the Federal Civilian 
Executive Branch, State, Local, Tribal, and Territorial Governments, and Critical Infrastructure 
entities, and which provide multiple types of vulnerability scanning. 
 
These services are highly specialized cybersecurity services aligned to modernized and 
automated scanning services and assessments, which enable CISA to defend the nation against 
rapidly evolving threats.  Scanning services are scalable products where the Government delivers 
proactive products that evaluate an organization’s cybersecurity posture as well as provide 
support on improving exposed vulnerabilities.  The first type of scanning service includes 
Vulnerability Scanning (VS) which is where CyHy Services scans for and reports on 
vulnerabilities that are detectable from outside an organization's network (IP-based).  The second 
kind of scanning service provided is Web Application Scanning (WAS).  This scanning service 
assesses the “health” of publicly available accessible web applications by checking for known 
vulnerabilities and weak configurations.  Finally, Posture and Exposure services are provided.  
Posture and Exposure services use open-source data from both freely available and/or vendor 
supplied data feeds to provide actionable reports to CyHy stakeholders. 
 
 
 
 
1 FAR Class Deviation 26-04, Revision 1 for FAR Part 16 was issued as part of the Revolutionary FAR Overhaul 
(RFO), which was implemented in response to Executive Order 14275, Restoring Common Sense to Federal 
Procurement, signed April 15, 2025. Any subsequent citations to FAR Part 16 in this document will be cited as 
“RFO FAR” for brevity, and are referring specifically to FAR Class Deviation 26-04, Revision 1 for FAR Part 16.  

 
HSAM 3016.507-6 
JUSTIFICATION FOR EXCEPTION TO FAIR OPPORTUNITY 
EXCEEDING THE SIMPLIFIED ACQUISITION THRESHOLD 
JEFO No.  FY26-00026  - 2 - 
 
 
The period of performance will be March 30, 2026 through March 29, 2027. The total estimated 
value is . 
 
4. Identify the exception to fair opportunity and supporting rationale, including a demonstration 
that the proposed contractor’s unique qualifications or the nature of the acquisition requires use 
of the exception cited. 
 
Pursuant to RFO FAR 16.507-6(b)(3), The order must be issued on a sole-source basis in the 
interest of economy and efficiency because it is a logical follow-on to an order already issued 
under the contract, where all awardees were given a fair opportunity to be considered for the 
original order. 
 
Rationale: 
 
The incumbent Contractor, NTT Data Services Federal Government, LLC (NTT Data) is 
currently performing services under task order 70RCSA20FR0000133, which is scheduled to end 
on March 29, 2026. The task order was competitively awarded to the incumbent on September 
30, 2020 against the General Services Administration (GSA) Alliant 2 Unrestricted procurement 
vehicle.  
 
NTT Data is the only vendor that can ensure continued critical services to prevent disruption of 
services to CISA and its customers until the competitive award in FY27 and transition of 
services to a new Contractor
2. NTT Data currently: (1) provides monitoring and response 
capabilities to the nation’s most critical entities; (2) delivers services to the ~12,500 CISA 
customers enrolled in Vulnerability Scanning, Web Application Scanning, and Posture & 
Exposure; (3) onboards new customers into these services and supports modernization and 
efficiency efforts; and (4) supports CISA’s ability to support the following direction: National 
Vulnerability Incident Response; National Cybersecurity Strategy’s Strategic Objective 1.5; 
Supporting the Federal Civilian Executive Branch’s compliance with mission Binding 
Operational Directives, including: 22-01 (replaced 18-01) - Reducing the Significant Risk of 
Known Exploited Vulnerabilities, 23-01--- Improving Asset Visibility and Vulnerability 
Detection on Federal Networks, and 23-02--- Mitigating the Risk from Internet-Exposed 
Management Interface. 
 
Continued support is essential. To solicit other sources at this time would result in schedule 
delays, performance disruption of critical security services, and unnecessary cost inefficiencies 
to the Government. A new competitive procurement could take up to 12 months before an award 
is made. This would delay performance, disrupt mission critical services and increase 
administrative costs to the Government. If a new vendor is awarded this effort there could be 
further disruption of services up to 4 months long while CISA clears the new contractor staff to  
 
2 Market research is being conducted in anticipation of a competitive award in FY27. Early findings demonstrate 
that there are multiple companies on the GSA Alliant 2 Unrestricted Government Wide Acquisition Contract that 
can support the requirement. GSA Alliant 2 is a mandatory strategic sourcing vehicle (SSV).  
 


 
HSAM 3016.507-6 
JUSTIFICATION FOR EXCEPTION TO FAIR OPPORTUNITY 
EXCEEDING THE SIMPLIFIED ACQUISITION THRESHOLD 
JEFO No.  FY26-00026  - 3 - 
 
 
on-board. For example, the average security entry on duty (EOD) timelines for CISA are 
between 69 and 120 days for the types of support needed on the current task order.  The follow-
on work must be performed immediately upon completion of the current task order to ensure 
continuity of operations and avoid a disruption of critical security services. NTT Data has unique 
knowledge and expertise with the current phase of the program that no other vendor possesses. A 
different vendor would have to work to catch up in order to become familiar with the services 
and perform the follow-on which would result in a substantial increase to program costs. The 
added administrative and performance cost as well as performance risk at this time dimmish 
economy and efficiency to the Government, making the short-term logical follow-on necessary.  
 
5. Determination by the Contracting Officer that  the anticipated cost to the Government  
will be fair and reasonable.  
 
Pursuant to RFO FAR 16.506(f), if the service prices were not established at the IDIQ level, then 
the Contracting Officer must establish prices for the order using the policies and methods in 
RFO FAR subpart 15.4. The Contracting Officer anticipates a fair and reasonable price will be 
achieved based on using the rates established by the GSA Alliant 2 GWAC, consideration of 
historical pricing, and comparison of the labor mix and level of effort to the Independent 
Government Cost Estimate (IGCE).  
 
6.  Any other facts supporting the justification. 
 
None at this time.  
 
7.  A statement of the actions, if any, the agency may take to remove or overcome any barriers 
that led to the exception to fair opportunity before any subsequent acquisition for the supplies or 
services is made.  
 
CISA continues to monitor the commercial marketplace and conduct market research. Award of 
a short-term task order to NTT Data will allow continued CyHy support while a competitive 
procurement is underway and will prevent the cessation of these highly specialized services in 
support of mission critical activities. The estimated award date is in Q2 Fiscal Year 2027. CISA 
intends to post the approved justification on www.sam.gov pursuant to RFO FAR 16.507-6(f)(1).   
 
8. Technical/Requirements Personnel Certification. 
 
Pursuant to RFO FAR 16.507-6(d)(2)(ix), I certify that this requirement meets the Government’s 
minimum need and that the supporting data, which form a basis for the justification, are accurate 
and complete. 
   
Contracting Officer’s Representative

search_text:
OVERVIEW 
This Commercial Solutions Opening (CSO) solicitation is issued in accordance with RFO 
DFARS 212.70, Defense Commercial Solutions Opening (FA445226SC001) 
 
Air Mobility Command A6 (AMC) requires innovative methodologies and solutions to support 
secure, scalable, multi- and cross-domain IT transformation, encompassing the core, edge, and 
cloud components of Air Force IT infrastructure. 
 
North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) Code and Associated Description: 
Specified in each Area of Interest (AOI) 
 
Federal Agency Name and Contracting Officer Address: United States Air Force, 763rd 
Enterprise Sourcing Squadron located at 510 POW- MIA Drive, Scott AFB, IL 62225. 
 
Commercial Solutions Opening Title: This is a combined synopsis/solicitation for AMC/A6 
Information Technology Needs 
 
Commercial Solutions Opening Number: FA4452-26-S-C001 
 
Commercial Solutions Opening Type: This is the initial announcement. This will be a 
Hybrid Two-Step Solicitation and is Open until 26 February 2031. Submissions will only be 
accepted following specific AOI submission due dates and times. The Government reserves 
the right to extend this CSO beyond the closed date by amendment if the authority is 
extended. 
 
WHITE PAPER DUE DATE AND TIME: Will be specified in AOI. 
 
Submission POC: White Papers shall be submitted to the Contracting Points of Contact 
(POCs): 
Agreements/Contracting Officer 
Name: Lisa Pendragon 
Phone: 505-225-9752 
Email: lisa.pendragon@us.af.mil  
 
Agreements/Contract Specialist 
Name: Elijah Dodd 
Phone: 618-256-9962 
Email: elijah.dodd@us.af.mil  
 
Solicitation Request: The 763rd Enterprise Sourcing Squadron at Scott AFB will solicit White 
Papers for AMC Information Technology solutions.  
 
Type of Contract: The Air Force anticipates the award of a Firm-Fixed Price type contract 
in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR). Offerors are encouraged to review 
RFO FAR 52.212-4 Contract Terms and Conditions – Commercial Products and 
Commercial Services and RFO F AR 12.205 Solicitation provisions and contract clauses for 

standard terms and conditions in a commercial contract. In addition, the Air Force reserves 
the right to award an Other Transaction for Prototype Project in accordance with 10 U.S.C. § 
3458 and 10 U.S.C. § 4022. 
 
The Air Force reserves the right to award one or more, or no, contracts/agreements for all, 
part, or none of the White Papers and detailed approach submissions received in response to 
the AOI(s) posted. 
 
CLASSIFIED INFORMATION. Offerors shall submit unclassified White Papers to 
simplify and expedite reviews and responses by the Government.  
 
PURPOSE: This CSO is a competitive solicitation focused on technologies to meet the 
mission of the Department of the Air Force. The Government seeks to obtain innovative 
solutions or potential new capabilities to fulfill requirements, close capability gaps, or provide 
potential technological advancements in the capability areas listed in the AOI(s). 
 
SECURITY CLEARANCE:  All respondees require a Secret Clearance.  If a Facilities Clearance is 
required, the level of clearance will be specified in the AOI. 
 
Innovative means: 
1. Any technology, process, or method, including research and development that is 
new as of the date of submission of a White Paper; 
2. Any application of an existing technology, process, or method that is new as of the 
date of submission of submission of a White Paper; or 
3. A new application or adaptation of an existing technology, process, or method as of 
the date of submission of a White Paper. 
 
All items, technologies, and services acquired using a CSO will be treated as commercial 
items if they meet above definition of innovative. The Government encourages White 
Papers that span a wide spectrum of possible technical and business solutions in response 
to the specific AOI(s). 
 
Awards resulting from this CSO will be made based on the evaluation results of a Two-
Step closed process. The Government reserves the right to fund only part of a submitted 
White Paper and detailed approach submission; and may incrementally fund any or all 
awards under this CSO. All awards are subject to the availability of funds. All 
contracts/agreements entered into under this CSO will be Firm Fixed Price type contracts 
or agreements. 
 
White Papers may be submitted at any time prior to the due dates and times specified in the 
AOI(s). Offerors are responsible for monitoring SAM.gov for any amendments to this 
solicitation that may have updated information. All responses to the AOI(s) shall be valid for 
180 calendar days after the submission date.  
 
Submission Limit: White Papers shall not exceed the page limits specified in the AOI(not 
including title page or table of contents) using 12-point Times New Roman font, 1 inch margins 
(on all sides) or, alternatively, White Papers may take the form of briefing slides which shall not 

exceed  the slide limit specified in the AOI (not including title slide). Any pages or briefing slides 
exceeding the limit will be removed and not evaluated.  
 
Required Content: 
1. Section A: Company Information 
 
a. Title of Project 
b. Name of Company 
c. Business Size 
d. Company’s Commercial and Government Entity (CAGE) number 
e. Unique Entity Identifier (UEI) number 
f. Company Point of Contact (POC) [Name, Phone, Email] 
g. Technical POC [Name, Phone, Email] 
h. Facility Clearance (if required, will be specified in AOI) 
 
2. Section B: Technical Solution 
a. Specified in posted AOI(s). 
 
3. Additional Sections may be posted in individual AOI(s) 
 
 
White Paper Review, Evaluation and Selection Process: Each White Paper will be 
evaluated by the Government, and the White Paper shall stand on its own technical 
merit. White Papers will be evaluated in accordance with the following evaluation 
factors: 
1. Technical 
2. Importance to agency 
3. Price 
 
Evaluation factor importance will be determined in the applicable AoI(s).  
 
Method of Evaluation: Scientific, technological, and/or other subject-matter experts peer 
review process. 
 
The selection process will be specified in the posted AOI(s). 
 
Unsuccessful offerors will be notified by the Government, either via email or letter, 
to inform them that the proposed effort is not of interest to the Government at this 
time. 
 
Those Companies whose White Papers are of interest to the Government will be 
notified and communications will include a submission due date for Step Two: 
Detailed Approach at that time. 

 
STEP TWO: DETAILED APPROACH 
 
SUBMISSION INFORMATION: 
 
The Company will be invited to develop and submit a full written Detailed Approach and 
negotiate appropriate terms and conditions governing the prototype project. Companies may 
discuss ideas and details of the approach during the writing process with the Government. 
 
1. Section A shall provide the technical approach;  
a. Title Page  
i. Company Name 
ii. Title 
iii. Point of Contact (Name, Title, Date, E-Mail Address, Phone, and Address) 
iv. Any subcontractors or teaming members 
v. An abstract which provides a concise description of the approach.  
b. Technical Approach  
i. Describe the company’s proposed technical approach, deliverables, and 
the resources needed to execute it.  
ii. Include the nature and extent of the anticipated results. 
iii. Include any potential ancillary and operational issues (i.e. certifications, 
algorithms, and any engineering/software development methodologies) to 
be used.  
iv. This approach shall include a Performance Work Statement (PWS) 
identifying the work to be performed.. Provide a detailed project schedule 
that outlines the various phases of work to be accomplished within the 
proposed period of performance. You may refer to the White Paper that 
prompted this step, but do not duplicate it. 
c. Government Support Required  
i. Identify the type of support, if any, the Company requests of the 
Government in general such as facilities, equipment, data, and 
information, and/ or materials.  
 
2. Section B shall address the price schedule portions of the approach.  
a. The Company shall propose a total price to complete the project and provide any 
other data or supporting information that is necessary for the determination of a 
fair and reasonable price. This can include, but is not limited to, commercial price 
catalog or other proprietary information to help the Government assess project 
price. 
 
These submissions will be assessed by the Government team subject matter experts. Companies 
may propose their own internal terms and conditions (e.g., Service License Agreements (SLA) 
and/or User License Agreements (ULA)) to be considered during deliberations with the 
Government. Companies should note that there are certain terms and conditions the Government 
is unable to accept. However, projects awarded through the CSO are flexible to adopt customary 
industry standards where it is otherwise legal and where it meets the Government’s needs.  

 
 
DEBRIEFINGS: 
The Government will conduct written, post-award debriefings (only applicable to Two Step 
Awards) upon request only in accordance with RFO FAR 52.212-1(e) and will address the 
evaluation factors contained herein only. It will not include a comparison of submissions 
against a common statement of work (SOW) as the CSO provides no common statement of 
work that interested parties must respond to and instead represents opportunity for submission 
of a variety of innovative commercial items, technologies, and/or services that represent 
meaningful submissions of diverse scope and price. 
 
ADDITIONAL INFORMATION: 
1. Deliverables/Schedule: Specified in each AOI. 
 
Other Information: 
• Program Security Classification: If a DD254 is applicable, offerors shall verify their 
Cognizant Security Office information is current with the Defense 
Counterintelligence and Security Agency, DCSA. 
• Export Control: Information involved in this effort may be subject to Export 
Control, International Traffic in Arms (ITAR) 22 CFR 120-131, or Export 
Administration Regulations (EAR) 15 CFR 710-774. If effort may be subject to 
export control, then a Certified DD Form 2345, Militarily Critical Technical Data 
Agreement, will be required to be submitted with White Paper. (See RFO DFARS 
225.7901-4, RFO DFARS 252.225- 7048, Export-Controlled Item). 
• Government Furnished Property (GFP) Availability: GFP is not anticipated to be 
made available under any resulting contract, or agreement but will be determined in 
each AOI. In accordance with RFO FAR 45.201(b), the offeror is responsible for all 
costs related to making the property available for use, such as payment of all 
transportation, installation or rehabilitation costs. 
• Base Support/Network Access: Base support will be specified in each AOI. 
 
2. Eligible Offeror: This is an unrestricted solicitation. Small businesses are encouraged 
to propose. Offerors are encouraged to complete the Annual Representations and 
Certification in the System for Award Management (SAM.gov) as soon as possible as active 
registration is required to receive an award.   
 
3. Foreign Participation: Participation at the prime level is prohibited. Subcontracting 
arrangements will be handled in accordance with the International Traffic and Arms Regulation. 
In addition, foreign national participation will be limited to public domain information only. 
 
4. You may be ineligible for award if all requirements of this CSO and the applicable AOI 
are not met on the White Paper due date or Step Two submission due date when applicable. 
 
5. 763d ESS policy is to treat all submissions as confidential information, and to disclose 
their contents only for the purpose of evaluation. The Government understands that 
information provided in response to this CSO is presented in confidence and may contain 

trade secret or commercial or financial information, and it agrees to protect such 
information from unauthorized disclosure to the maximum extent permitted or required by 
Law, to include:  
a. 18 U.S.C. 1905 (Trade Secrets Act);
b. 18 U.S.C. 1831 et seq. (Economic Espionage Act);
c. 5 U.S.C. 552(b)(4) (Freedom of Information Act);
d. Executive Order 12600 (Pre -disclosure Notification Procedures for Confidential
Commercial Information); and,
e. Any other statute, regulation, or requirement applicable to Government employees.

Introduction & Mission Objective 
Air Mobility Command (AMC) seeks innovative partners to lead a comprehensive 
transformation of its Information Technology (IT) infrastructure. The current legacy environment 
is outdated, inefficient, and hinders the "Global Reach" mission by compromising operational 
speed, data sharing, and overall warfighter effectiveness. 
The objective is to overhaul this system by assessing existing architecture, developing a forward-
looking roadmap, and integrating modern technologies across core, cloud, and edge 
environments. The goal is to field a secure, scalable, and purpose-built IT infrastructure that 
enables data-driven, globally maneuverable airpower for modern joint warfare. 
The Challenge: Critical Infrastructure Vulnerabilities 
The IT infrastructure currently supporting AMC bases suffers from several critical vulnerabilities 
that directly impact mission readiness: 
• Operational Inefficiency: Aging hardware and antiquated software result in slow

title: TFSC AI Modernization Initiative
search_text:
REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI)
/ MARKET RESEARCH NOTICE

TFSC AI Modernization Initiative

Air Force Personnel Center (AFPC)

Draft Planning Document for Industry Engagement and Acquisition Planning

Release and distribution of this package should be coordinated with the cognizant contracting office prior to external dissemination.

1. Notice to Industry

This Request for Information (RFI) is issued solely for market research and acquisition planning purposes.

This notice is not a solicitation, not a Request for Proposal (RFP), not a Request for Quotation (RFQ), and not a commitment by the Government to award a contract or otherwise pay for information received in response to this notice.

The Government seeks information from industry to better understand commercially available capabilities, implementation considerations, security and compliance approaches, integration patterns, sustainment models, and rough-order-of-magnitude planning factors relevant to the modernization of customer service operations supporting the Total Force Service Center (TFSC).

Responses to this RFI are for informational and planning purposes only. A response to this notice will not be considered an offer and will not be evaluated for contract award. The Government will not pay respondents for any information submitted or for any costs incurred in preparing a response. Any information submitted may be used by the Government in developing or refining requirements, acquisition strategy, and any future pre-solicitation or solicitation documentation.

Participation in this RFI is voluntary. The Government reserves the right to use or not use any information provided and is under no obligation to acknowledge receipt of responses, provide feedback to respondents, or release the results of this market research effort.

2. Background

The Air Force Personnel Center (AFPC), located at Joint Base San Antonio–Randolph, Texas, provides personnel support and human resource services affecting a large Total Force population worldwide. One of AFPC’s critical service organizations is the Total Force Service Center (TFSC), which provides customer support across a range of personnel, benefits, and human resource-related inquiries for military members, civilians, retirees, veterans, and eligible family members.

AFPC is exploring options to modernize TFSC customer service capabilities through the use of advanced automation and artificial intelligence-enabled solutions. Areas of interest include, but are not limited to, intelligent virtual agents, conversational AI, voice and chat automation, knowledge-based assistance, workflow automation, agent-assist capabilities, analytics, secure integration with enterprise systems, and scalable contact center operations suitable for government environments.

The Government is conducting market research to better understand existing industry capabilities, deployment models, security posture, implementation approaches, and associated risks or dependencies relevant to a potential future requirement.

3. Purpose

The purpose of this initiative is to refine and validate operational and technical requirements while assessing commercially available and emerging solution capabilities. It also aims to facilitate an understanding of hosting, integration, security, and compliance considerations. Furthermore, the objective is to identify implementation dependencies, risks, and best practices to inform the acquisition strategy and potential contract structure, and to develop a more accurate understanding of pricing and scaling considerations.

4. Scope of Interest

• Conversational AI for voice and digital channels.

• Natural language understanding and intent recognition.

• Intelligent virtual agents and chatbots.

• Voice automation and IVR modernization.

• Human-agent escalation and assisted service.

• Ticketing and workflow automation.

• Knowledge retrieval and response generation.

• Analytics, reporting, and performance monitoring.

• Integration with case management, CRM, telephony, and enterprise systems.

• Secure deployment in government or DAF-relevant environments.

• Scalability to support enterprise customer service operations.

5. Requested Information from Industry

Interested vendors are requested to provide written responses to the questionnaire in Attachment A and, where applicable, comments on the draft requirements contained in Attachment B.

Respondents are encouraged to describe currently available capabilities, clearly identify any functionality that remains on a future product roadmap, and distinguish between native product capabilities and partner-delivered or integrator-delivered services. Respondents should also identify assumptions, dependencies, implementation prerequisites, and any material limitations that may affect cost, schedule, security posture, or operational feasibility.

Where appropriate, respondents are encouraged to recommend clarifications, alternative approaches, or tradeoffs the Government should consider before finalizing operational or technical requirements.

6. Response Instructions

Interested vendors should submit a written response containing, at a minimum, the items listed below. Responses should be clear, concise, and tailored to the subject matter of this RFI. Proprietary or competition-sensitive information should be clearly marked.

• Company name.

• Primary point of contact.

• Email address and telephone number.

• CAGE code and DUNS number.

• Completed questionnaire responses.

• Comments on the draft requirements list / compliance matrix.

• Optional supporting materials, such as capability briefs, product sheets, architecture overviews, or relevant case studies.

Suggested formatting and administrative information:

.

Responses should be submitted electronically via email to the points of contact listed below no later than the response due date.

Primary POC: Shiri Avnet, shiri.avnet.1@us.af.mil

Alternate POC: Corey Collins, corey.collins.12@us.af.mil

Please format your response in Microsoft Word or Adobe PDF and limit it to 20 pages.

The subject line of the email should read: "Response to RFI-FA701426-TFSC - [Your Company Name]".

7. Important Disclaimer

This RFI is issued for market research only. The Government is not requesting proposals, quotations, or bids at this time. No contract will be awarded as a result of this notice. The Government may use information received in response to this notice to support planning, requirement development, acquisition strategy development, and any future acquisition documentation.

If a future solicitation is issued, it will be announced through the appropriate contracting channels. External distribution of this market research package should be coordinated with the cognizant contracting office to ensure fair and consistent dissemination practices.

8. Attachments

This package includes the following attachments:

• Attachment A – Market Research Questionnaire

• Attachment B – Draft Vendor Requirements / Compliance Matrix

ATTACHMENT A

Market Research Questionnaire

TFSC AI Modernization Initiative

I. General Vendor Information

1.	Please provide a brief overview of your company, including company background, core lines of business, and relevant experience delivering AI-enabled customer service or contact center solutions.

2.	Please provide examples of similar implementations, particularly for federal, Department of the Air Force, other public sector, or large-scale enterprise environments.

3.	Please describe your company’s current roadmap for future development and innovation relevant to AI-powered customer engagement, automation, or contact center modernization.

4.	Please identify whether your company would participate as a product provider, system integrator, managed service provider, prime contractor, subcontractor, or through a partnered delivery model.

II. AI and Natural Language Processing Capabilities

5.	Please describe the AI, natural language processing, speech recognition, and/or language model technologies that power your solution.

6.	How does your system determine user or caller intent from natural language input, and what performance measures do you typically use to evaluate accuracy?

7.	What languages, dialects, and input/output modalities are supported across voice and digital channels?

8.	Does your solution improve over time based on interactions, feedback, tuning, or model updates? If so, please describe how this occurs and what level of customer control is available.

9.	Please describe your digital engagement capabilities, including supported channels such as web, mobile, chat, SMS, email, and social platforms, where applicable.

10. Please describe your voice capabilities, including IVR modernization, speech-to-text, text-to-speech, call routing, agent escalation, and real-time transcription, where applicable.

11. Please describe any agent-assist features, such as recommended responses, knowledge suggestions, summarization, disposition support, or next-best-action capabilities.

12. Please explain how your solution addresses hallucination risk, answer grounding, confidence scoring, human review, and escalation for low-confidence responses.

III. Ticketing, Workflow, and Integration

13. How does your solution integrate with service management, case management, CRM, and ticketing systems for automated case creation, update, routing, and closure?

14. What data elements can be captured and populated automatically during ticket or case creation?

15. Can workflows, routing rules, data fields, business rules, and escalation paths be configured or customized to align with customer-specific needs?

16. Please identify any pre-built integrations or commonly supported interfaces with CRM, case management, telephony, identity, knowledge management, or workflow platforms.

17. Please describe your API, event, webhook, middleware, or integration architecture, including common approaches for connecting to enterprise systems.

18. Please describe how your solution integrates with telephony or contact center platforms, including voice routing, handoff to live agents, and screen-pop or context transfer capabilities.

IV. Security, Compliance, and Data Handling

19. Please describe your security architecture and the security controls relevant to government use cases.

20. Please describe your solution’s ability to support Department of War Impact Level 4 (IL4) and/or Impact Level 5 (IL5) environments, including current status, limitations, or planned path. 

21. Is your solution currently authorized, assessed, or aligned for use in federal or Department of the Air Force environments? Please provide details regarding FedRAMP, DoW SRG, DISA, agency ATOs, or similar certifications or authorizations, as applicable.

22. Please describe whether your proposed solution and all resulting deliverables can be deployed, operated, and sustained on an Air Force-approved platform or hosting environment. In your response, identify any external dependencies, third-party hosting requirements, or architectural limitations that would prevent the solution from operating fully within Air Force systems. 

23. Please describe whether your solution can operate using data that resides solely within Air Force-owned or Air Force-approved systems, without requiring replication to vendor-managed environments, and explain how transferable or generalizable the solution’s capabilities are for reuse across other Air Force use cases, including voice-enabled applications beyond the TFSC mission.

24. How does your solution protect Personally Identifiable Information (PII), sensitive data, and customer interaction records?

25. Please describe encryption methods used for data in transit and at rest, identity and access control mechanisms, logging, auditability, and monitoring capabilities.

26. Please describe your data residency, data sovereignty, retention, and deletion policies, including where customer data is logically and physically stored.

27. Please describe how customer data is used for training, fine-tuning, analytics, or service improvement, and what controls are available to restrict such use.

28. Please identify any subcontractors, cloud service providers, or third-party components that are materially involved in hosting, processing, or securing customer data.

V. Technical Specifications and Scalability

29. Please describe the underlying software stack, architecture, deployment model, and major technical components of your solution.

30. Please describe the infrastructure, networking, and implementation prerequisites required to deploy and operate your solution.

31. What is the maximum number of concurrent users, calls, sessions, or transactions your system can support, and how does it scale to meet demand?

32. Please describe your approach to high availability, resilience, failover, continuity of operations, and disaster recovery.

33. Please describe the level of customization available for user interfaces, workflows, branding, and customer experience design.

34. Please identify whether your offering is multi-tenant, single-tenant, dedicated, or otherwise configurable for isolated government deployments.

35. Please describe your solution’s architecture and capabilities related to containerization. Specify your experience with technologies such as Docker and Kubernetes and describe how your solution can be delivered as a containerized product for deployment in a government-managed environment.

VI. Government Experience and Delivery Approach

36. Please describe your experience supporting federal agencies, Department of the Air Force organizations, or similarly regulated environments.

37. Please describe your implementation approach, including typical phases, timeline ranges, government dependencies, and required customer participation.

38. Please identify the types of government-furnished data, subject matter expert support, integration access, or infrastructure dependencies normally required for implementation.

39. Please describe your sustainment model, including operations support, service-level support, model updates, content updates, tuning, and administration responsibilities.

40. Please provide a rough-order-of-magnitude description of your pricing structure, such as licensing, implementation, integration, hosting, support, usage-based pricing, or seat-based pricing, if available for planning purposes.

41. Please provide any recommended acquisition, implementation, or deployment considerations the Government should take into account based on your experience.

VII. Feedback on Draft Government Requirements

42. Please review the draft requirements in Attachment B and identify: (a) requirements that are well aligned to current market capabilities; (b) requirements that may need clarification; (c) requirements that may significantly affect cost, schedule, or feasibility; (d) recommended alternative appro

REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI): Session Border Controller (SBC) Network Solution


DESCRIPTION: This Request for Information (RFI) is for informational and market research purposes only. This is not a Request for Proposal (RFP) and does not constitute a solicitation. This RFI shall not be construed as a commitment by the U.S. Government. Responses in any form are not offers, and the Government is under no obligation to award a contract as a result of this announcement. No funds are available to pay for the preparation of responses to this announcement. Any information submitted by respondents is strictly voluntary.

The Directorate of Contracting, Test Range and Specialized Contracting Division at the Air Force Test Center (AFTC), Edwards Air Force Base, CA, 93524, is conducting market research. The purpose is to identify available and mature information system network or on-premise network solutions that include comprehensive life-cycle support. The AFTC would acquire the solution to issue as Government-Furnished Equipment (GFE) to small business contractors supporting the various service and research and development efforts for the AFTC.

BACKGROUND: The AFTC is seeking information on available information system network or on-premises solutions that include comprehensive life-cycle support. Such a solution, acquired by AFTC, would ensure that all Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI)—whether provided by the government or generated by AFTC contractors and their subcontractors—resides on a government-owned platform. The express purpose is to maintain AFTC control over all contractor-related CUI.

This RFI explores a new paradigm where the responsibility for protecting Department of Defense (DoD) CUI remains with the DoD, rather than being distributed across multiple small business networks. Consequently, small business contractors using this AFTC-owned and furnished solution would not be subject to CMMC Level 2 assessment, as the CUI would reside on a government network, not a contractor network. This model is similar to existing networks, such as the one used by PEO Carriers, which centralizes all shipbuilding CUI on the PEO’s network instead of distributing it across contractor and subcontractor systems. Other similar networks are currently used to protect sensitive information in various fields.

The ideal solution would be comparable to the federal NIPRnet but offer greater flexibility to accommodate the diverse needs of small business contractors supporting the AFTC. The solution must provide life-cycle support compliant with NIST SP 800-53 and all other government requirements, encompassing hardware, firmware, software, provisioning, training, help-desk support, monitoring, and governance. Furthermore, the solution must meet all requirements for protecting ITAR and EAR data and be capable of qualifying for a DoD Authority to Operate (ATO).





SOLUTION REQUIREMENTS: Potential contractors with a comprehensive solution to the challenge described are requested to submit an entry in the attached spreadsheet, detailing how their solution meets the requirements below. These characteristics are intentionally open-ended to encourage unique and innovative proposals:

Scalable from a pilot program to tens of thousands of contractors.

Acquirable by the government in its entirety.

The network must contain contractor-generated and government-provided CUI while enabling small business contractors and their subcontractors to perform work on a wide range of contract deliverables for the AFTC, including manufacturing, software development/testing, R&D, and other services.

The solution must comply with NIST SP 800-53 and other government network requirements to ensure it is acceptable for acquisition and can achieve an ATO from the AFTC.

The solution must be a stand-alone, comprehensive solution that includes life-cycle services/support, training, governance, a helpdesk, monitoring, and all other related functions.

The solution must provide life-cycle support in the form of personnel and hardware/firmware/software. It should also include the knowledge and expertise to adapt the network to the needs of AFTC contractors and to remain compliant with AF IT acquisition and life-cycle requirements.

The stand-alone solution must comprehensively include all management, personnel, equipment, supplies, facilities, transportation, tools, materials, supervision, and other items and non-personal services necessary to sustain the network or on-premises solution.

If a proposed solution has challenges in meeting the full requirements detailed in this RFI, respondents should provide suggested methods for overcoming those challenges.

INSTRUCTIONS:

INDUSTRY DAY (PENDING RESPONDENT INTEREST): Based on interest generated by this RFI, the AFTC may hold an industry pitch day. This event would serve to present the AFTC's mission and objectives for the anticipated services and to receive comments from industry. The government will determine whether to schedule an industry day after reviewing RFI responses.
WHERE: Virtual
WHEN: To Be Determined (TBD)

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION: This is a Request for Information announcement only and does not constitute a Request for Proposal (RFP) or a solicitation for bids. No contract will be awarded from this notice, and it is not to be construed as a commitment by the Government. All submissions are voluntary, and the Government assumes no financial responsibility for any costs incurred in responding to this market survey. The Government is not obligated to acknowledge receipt of any information or to provide feedback to respondents. Telephone inquiries will not be accepted or acknowledged.

Interested parties are requested to submit a capability statement demonstrating their experience in meeting all or part of the Solution Requirements. These statements should not be proposals, but rather a summary of the entity’s existing technical and management capabilities to perform the work as outlined. Respondents should clearly indicate which requirements their solution can meet. All submitted information must be in writing and contain sufficient detail to allow the government's technical representative to evaluate the state of the industry. All information is for market research purposes only and is provided on a voluntary basis.

Scope and Objective: The critical objective is to determine if secure network or on-premises information system solutions are available for procurement by the AFTC. These solutions would be for use by small business contractors and their subcontractors who provide a wide array of services and research and development efforts to the AFTC.

Purpose: This RFI supports market research being conducted by AFTC to identify the current state of the industry for network or on-premises solutions designed to protect DoD Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) that is provided to or generated by small business contractors and their subcontractors.

Requirements: See SOLUTION REQUIREMENTS.

Requested Information: The Government requests capability statements from interested parties demonstrating their experience in meeting all or a majority of the SOLUTION REQUIREMENTS. Submissions should include:
a. Company name, UEI Number and CAGE Code, address, points of contact with phone number and email address.
b. Responses should be provided by completing Column B of the attached spreadsheet to illustrate the state of the industry for this type of solution. The information package must be clear, concise, and complete. The Air Force is under no obligation to provide feedback or contact any company for clarification.

Inquiries: Requests for further information will not be honored.

Submission Instructions: Please submit responses via email to the Points of Contact listed below. If you need to submit proprietary information or large files, please request a DoD SAFE link from the POCs.

POINTS OF CONTACT:

Contracting Officer
1st Lt Austen Siegler, AFTC/PZZF
Austen.siegler.1@us.af.mil

Contracting Officer
Mr. Tylan J. McBride, AFTC/PZZ
Tylan.mcbride.2@us.af.mil

### Sheet: Sheet1
 | Question | Response
1 | Describe the solution. | 
2 | Is the solution on-premises or cloud-based? | 
3 | Describe compliance with NIST SP 800-53 and other government network requirements--ensuring the network or on-prem solution is  acceptable for acquisition/ATO by the AFTC. | 
4 | Describe how the solution would become GFE for AFTC. | 
5 | Describe the life-cycle support that would be offered.  For example, life-cycle services/support, training, governance, helpdesk, monitoring, etc. | 
6 | Describe how the solution will  include all management, personnel, equipment, supplies, facilities, transportation, tools, materials, supervision,  other items, and non-personal services necessary to sustain the network or on-prem network solution. | 
7 | Describe why this solution is superior to others. | 
8 | Describe how this solution would allow for small business contractors and their subcontractors to work various contract deliverables, such as manufacturing, software development/testing, R&D, and other small business contractor services. | 
9 | Describe how this solution would scale to thousands of contractors and subcontractors. | 
10 | Describe how CUI including ITAR and EAR data will be protected per their unique requirements and remain on the network solution.  Include how you will ensure the contract CUI remains on the government-owned solution. | 
11 | Should the proposed solution encounter challenges in meeting the full requirements enunciated in the RFI, provide  ways those challneges might be met. |

252.232-7006 Wide Area WorkFlow Payment Instructions. (Jan 2023)
WIDE AREA WORKFLOW PAYMENT INSTRUCTIONS (JAN 2023) 
 (a) Deﬁnitions. As used in this clause- 
 "Department of Defense Activity Address Code (DoDAAC)" is a six position code that uniquely identiﬁes a unit, activity, or organization. 
 "Document type" means the type of payment request or receiving report available for creation in Wide Area WorkFlow (WAWF). 
 "Local processing ofﬁce (LPO)" is the ofﬁce responsible for payment certiﬁcation when payment certiﬁcation is done external to the entitlement system. 
 "Payment request" and "receiving report" are deﬁned in the clause at 252.232-7003, Electronic Submission of Payment Requests and Receiving Reports. 
 (b) Electronic invoicing. The WAWF system provides the method to electronically process vendor payment requests and receiving reports, as authorized by Defense 
Federal Acquisition Regulation Supplement (DFARS) 252.232-7003, Electronic Submission of Payment Requests and Receiving Reports. 
 (c) WAWF access. To access WAWF, the Contractor shall- 
 (1) Have a designated electronic business point of contact in the System for Award Management at https://www.sam.gov; and 
 (2) Be registered to use WAWF at https://wawf.eb.mil/ following the step-by-step procedures for self-registration available at this web site. 
 (d) WAWF training. The Contractor should follow the training instructions of the WAWF Web-Based Training Course and use the Practice Training Site before submitting 
payment requests through WAWF. Both can be accessed by selecting the "Web Based Training" link on the WAWF home page at https://wawf.eb.mil/ 
 (e) WAWF methods of document submission. Document submissions may be via web entry, Electronic Data Interchange, or File Transfer Protocol. 
 (f) WAWF payment instructions. The Contractor shall use the following information when submitting payment requests and receiving reports in WAWF for this contract or 
task or delivery order: 
 (1) Document type. The Contractor shall submit payment requests using the following document type(s): 
 (i) For cost-type line items, including labor-hour or time-and-materials, submit a cost voucher. 
 (ii) For ﬁxed price line items- 
 (A) That require shipment of a deliverable, submit the invoice and receiving report speciﬁed by the Contracting Ofﬁcer. 
____ 
(Contracting Ofﬁcer: Insert applicable invoice and receiving report document type(s) for ﬁxed price line items that require shipment of a deliverable.) 
 (B) For services that do not require shipment of a deliverable, submit either the Invoice 2in1, which meets the requirements for the invoice and receiving report, or the 
applicable invoice and receiving report, as speciﬁed by the Contracting Ofﬁcer.

 (iv) For performance based payments, submit a performance based payment request. 
 (v) For commercial ﬁnancing, submit a commercial ﬁnancing request.

title: Notice of Intent to Award a Sole Source Modification to Lockheed Martin to Increase Contract Ceiling
search_text:
DEPARTMENT OF THE NAVY 
NAVAL INFORMATION WARFARE CENTER PACIFIC 
53560 HULL STREET 
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92152-5001 
J&A No. 26-225 
 
JUSTIFICATION AND APPROVAL 
 FOR USE OF OTHER THAN FULL AND OPEN COMPETITION 
 
1. Contracting Activity.  
 
Department of the Navy, Naval Information Warfare Center Pacific (NIWC PAC). 
 
2. Description of the Action Being Approved.  
 
The proposed action is to increase the ceiling for contract N00039-19-D-0006 awarded to 
Lockheed Martin (LM) by $30,000,000 from $104,242,344 to $134,242,344 in support of 
Radiant Mercury (RADMERC). The cost-plus-fixed-fee (CPFF), Indefinite Delivery, Indefinite 
Quantity (IDIQ) contract was awarded on a sole source basis on 01 September 2019 with a five-
year base period and one five-year option period. Based on the higher than expected burn rate, 
the option period was exercised on 27 October 2021, roughly three years earlier than initially 
planned. While the current contract’s ordering period ends on 30 September 2029, the remaining 
contract ceiling has been allocated and is expected to be obligated by Third Quarter, Fiscal Year 
2026. The ceiling increase is necessary to avoid a gap in services critical to the Department of 
War (DoW).  
 
3. Description of Supplies/Services.  
 
The ceiling increase to contract N00039-19-D-0006 will provide continued support for the 
Department of the Navy and other Agencies’ RADMERC operations  
The current contract provides for 
installation, program management, maintenance, modernization, and sustainment of RADMERC 
systems and will provide system security authorization support, and logistics and training 
services. The work scope also includes sustainment of existing  
 
 
 
 
Due to a software baseline compromise identified around March 2022, the National Cross 
Domain Strategy and Management office and NCDSMO and DoW Chief Information Office 
(CIO) directed the CDS developer, Lockheed Martin (LM), to ramp up efforts to deliver a new 
version. This directive,  
 have resulted in an increase in demand on 
current requirements, unplanned costs, and a higher demand for RADMERC services. To 
support the substantial unanticipated demand on the current contract’s requirements, a ceiling 
increase of $12,028,735 was awarded on 13 February 2025, to avoid a gap in capability while the 
follow-on contract was pending.  


 
 
 
 
   
 
Despite the February 2025 ceiling increase, the ceiling on N00039-19-D-0006 contract was 
nearly exhausted within seven (7) months, as NIWC Pacific  
Customers not being prepared, changing/increasing their 
RADMERC order allocation, and updating their baseline, also resulted in a significant increase 
in changes to the RADMERC schedule, which further caused LM to bid for more support hours.   
 
Contractually, multiple customers (new and existing), have asked for additional support with the 
current baseline, from upgrading existing systems, to requiring additional CDS installations. 
Since the transition of the RADMERC contract to NIWC Pacific, seventy-six (76) task orders 
have been awarded and the customer base has grown from  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
  
 
 
 
 
 
  
 
4. Identification of Statutory Authority:  
 
The statutory authority is 10 U.S.C. 3204(a)(1), as implemented by Federal Acquisition 
Regulation (FAR) Subpart 6.302-1: Only one responsible source and no other supplies or 
services will satisfy agency requirements. 
 
5. Rationale Justifying Use of Cited Statutory Authority. 
 
 


 
  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
   
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 




 
6. Description of Efforts Made to Solicit Offers from as Many Offerors as Practicable.   
 
The proposed contract requirement will be synopsized on SAM.gov and any responses received 
will be considered prior to award. No additional market research was conducted because it is not 
practical, for the reasons discussed in paragraph 5 above, for any company other than LM to 
provide the required supplies and services. The USG did synopsize the previous procurement on 
the FEDBIZOPS via the NIWC PAC E-Commerce Central website on 03 December 2018. This 
announcement was posted for fifteen (15) days and resulted in only one question requesting the 
name of the incumbent contractor.  No other questions were received before the end date of 18 
December 2018. To date, no other potential sources have expressed interest in this requirement.  
 
7. Determination of Fair and Reasonable Cost.   
 
The Contracting Officer has determined that the anticipated cost to the Government for the 
supplies/services covered by this J&A will be fair and reasonable.   
 
8. Actions to Remove Barriers to Future Competition. 
 
Market research will be conducted for future contract requirements of this nature to determine 
the most advantageous method of fulfilling the Government’s need. As part of any future market 
research, the Government will monitor the NSA certification activity to determine if any other 
CDS can meet the Navy’s CDS certification requirements. 
 
The Government has established a CDS Working Group specifically to identify a viable long-
term solution that meets the requirements of all customers. The future solution is anticipated to 
be implemented incrementally over the next ten (10) years due to the complexity of the 
capability and the cost and time it takes to obtain NSA accreditation.

title: Enterprise Architecture, Data, and Engineering Support
search_text:
CUI 
 
SOURCE SELECTION INFORMATION – See FAR 2.101 and 3.104 
 
 
 
CUI  
JUSTIFICATION AND APPROVAL (J&A) 
FOR OTHER THAN FULL AND OPEN COMPETITION 
 
ENTERPRISE ARCHITECTURE, DATA, AND ENGINEERING (EADE) BRIDGE 
CONTROL NUMBER 26-22 
 
 
1. CONTRACTING ACTIVITY: 
 
United States Transportation Command, Directorate of Acquisition, IT Programs & Support Services 
Division (USTRANSCOM/TCAQ-D), 508 Scott Drive, Scott AFB, IL 62225. 
 
2. NATURE AND/OR DESCRIPTION OF THE ACTION BEING APPROVED: 
 
The proposed action is award of a sole-source Indefinite Delivery, Indefinite Quantity (IDIQ) contract to 
UNCOMN, LLC to provide mission-essential enterprise architecture, enterprise data, and enterprise 
engineering (EADE) technical support services to USTRANSCOM, Air Force Life Cycle Management 
Center (AFLCMC), and the U.S. Army Transportation Command (ARTRANS). The IDIQ will include 
Firm Fixed Price (FFP), Labor Hour (LH), and Time & Material (T&M) contract line items (CLINs). 
Funding will be applied at the task order level. 
 
These services were competed in CY2025, and a contract (HTC71126DE116) was awarded; however, a 
small business size protest is currently pending with the Small Business Administration (SBA). This 
action will support critical EADE services until the protest is resolved and the Government is able to 
acquire support under the follow-on contract.  
 
3. DESCRIPTION OF SERVICES REQUIRED TO MEET THE AGENCY’S NEEDS: 
 
The EADE contract will provide professional services, enterprise-wide support, and technical guidance 
related to mission needs and issues concerning the above commands and the Joint Deployment and 
Distribution Enterprise (JDDE). These services include operational and system perspectives of 
Command and Control (C2), planning, transportation, logistics, and business support system domains. 
Additionally, this requirement will continue support for prototyping, information support plan 
development, software development, and system administration allowing enterprise architecture data to 
reside in integrated architecture tool suites. These services allow USTRANSCOM and its component 
commands to share information and data across the enterprise, maintaining a true enterprise view.  
 
Since 2020, the EADE contract has been supported by the incumbent contractor, UNCOMN, LLC under 
HTC71120DD002. This was a competitive, small-business set-aside, single award IDIQ. 11 task orders 
were issued against this IDIQ. The awarded period of performance under all task orders ended on 30 
September 2025. After utilizing the full six months of FAR 52.217-8, Option to Extend Services, for 
each task order, the periods of performance (PoPs) are now set to expire on 31 March 2026.
  
 
 
Controlled by: USTRANSCOM  
Controlled by: Directorate of Acquisition 
CUI Category/ies: PROCURE 
Limited Dissemination Control: FED ONLY 
        POC:  
 
 








 
CUI 
 
SOURCE SELECTION INFORMATION – See FAR 2.101 and 3.104 
 
 
 
CUI  
6. DESCRIPTION OF EFFORTS MADE TO ENSURE THAT OFFERS ARE SOLICITED 
FROM AS MANY POTENTIAL SOURCES AS POSSIBLE. 
 
The market research report determined the level of collaboration required to continue services performed 
under the current EADE contract could pose a challenge to non-incumbents. To address this, a transition 
period was required for the follow-on acquisition. In accordance with FAR Part 5, the Government has 
determined a notice for the bridge requirement will not be publicized, as this action falls under the 
exception outlined in FAR 5.101(b)(2). The unusual and compelling urgency of the requirement does not 
allow the Government to comply with the publicizing timelines outlined in FAR Part 5. Publicizing this 
action would delay the award of the bridge contract, resulting in unacceptable interruptions to mission-
critical services.  
 
7. DETERMINATION BY THE CONTRACTING OFFICER THAT THE ANTICIPATED 
COST TO THE GOVERNMENT WILL BE FAIR AND REASONABLE. 
 
The Government will ensure the final price is fair and reasonable by conducting a detailed price analysis 
using one or more techniques outlined in FAR 15.404-1(b)(2). The Government has historical pricing 
from the predecessor contract (HTC71120DD002) and will also compare the proposed pricing to the 
pricing evaluated for HTC71126DE116, the protested action, which was determined fair and reasonable 
on 19 September 2025.  
  
8. THE REASON MARKET RESEARCH WAS NOT CONDUCTED. 
 
Due to the unusual and compelling urgency, market research was not conducted. As detailed above, the 
agency reviewed and adopted the market research report from Fiscal Year 2024 for EADE solicitation 
HTC71125RD002, which provided the basis for the Government’s decision to award a bridge contract to 
UNCOMN LLC.  The circumstances surrounding this requirement necessitate immediate action to 
ensure continuity of critical services and prevent unacceptable delays that would jeopardize mission 
success. The urgency of this acquisition leaves no time to conduct meaningful stand-alone market 
research without compromising the Government’s ability to have mission support. This action will 
support critical EADE services until the protest is resolved and the Government is able to acquire 
support under the follow-on contract. 
 
9. ANY OTHER FACTS SUPPORTING THE USE OF OTHER THAN FULL AND OPEN 
COMPETITION. 
 
Transition to a new contractor at this critical juncture would introduce substantial risks to operational 
continuity, jeopardizing the coverage required to continued support. In addition, the time required to 
conduct a new competitive procurement, resolve potential post-award protests, and transition would 
drastically exceed the available timeframe, making it impossible to award a competitive interim contract 
before 1 April 2026.  
 
10. LISTING OF THE SOURCES, IF ANY, THAT EXPRESSED AN INTEREST IN THE 
ACQUISITION. 
 
Offers from other sources were not solicited for this bridge extension; however, the follow-on 
competition remains active. The follow-on acquisition has been impacted by a protest  
 
 
11. ACTIONS, IF ANY, THE AGENCY MAY TAKE TO REMOVE OR OVERCOME ANY 
BARRIERS TO COMPETITION BEFORE ANY SUBSEQUENT ACQUISITION FOR

CONTINUATION PAGE

36C26226Q0506 Question and Answers

1) Can you provide photographs of the installation locations?

ANSWER: We can mark the locations on the plan drawing. 

2) Can you describe the types of walls the screens will be mounted to? I.E. CMU, drywall with metal studs, drywall with wood studs, brick?

ANSWER: Mostly dry walls and some locations with tiled walls. 

3) Will the screens be mounted in Landscape or Portrait format?

ANSWER:  The VA San Diego Healthcare System will rely on the contractors' expertise to assess and provide professional recommendations to determine the optimal orientation. As noted in the Statement of Work (SOW), the design must comply with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) requirements. 

4) You mention a range for screen size of 47"-65". Will you require different sized screens for different locations? Should screen size be standardized for all 16 installation locations?

ANSWER: The VA San Diego Healthcare System will rely on the contractors' expertise to assess the space and provide professional recommendation to help the facility make an informed decision. 65” are most likely going near the main entrances and 47” for other locations.

5) Is there any branding, color pallet, or logo placement that should be incorporated into the design of the screens? 

ANSWER: Yes. Similar navy blue color and logo as seen on the main VA website: https://www.va.gov/ shall be used as a starting point for design. 

The contractor shall have design discussion sessions with VASD for interface design. VASD will be relying on the contractor's design expertise. 

6) Will power and data connections be available at each installation location?

ANSWER: Power will be VASD’s responsibility. Cabling and network set up will be the contractor’s responsibility. 

7) Will the installer be able to utilize the dumpster, compactors, or recycling facilities at each installation location? Or will they be responsible for hauling away all packaging material? 

ANSWER: No. Contractors are responsible for their housekeeping. Note that at the main La Jolla Medical Center contractors shall only use the service elevators. 

8) Can the screens be received at each facility prior to installation? Or can they be received at a central location? Or will the installer be responsible for bringing the screens to each installation location? 

ANSWER: The contractor is responsible for safely storing their materials and tools. No materials, screens, or tools shall be left or stored on-site unsupervised at any time. VASDHS is not responsible for the storage, damage, or loss of any items left at VASDHS's medical center or clinics.

9) Can you clarify the expected time frame for issuing a PO and the desired installation date for the 16 screens? 

ANSWER: The PO will be issued once the contract has an award.  Dates for the installation will be June 30-July 31.

AMENDMENT 1 RESPONDING TO QUESTIONS RECEIVED REGARDING THE DIGITAL WAYFINDING SYSTEM

CONTINUATION PAGE

36C26226Q0506 Question and Answers

1) Can you provide photographs of the installation locations?

ANSWER: We can mark the locations on the plan drawing. 

2) Can you describe the types of walls the screens will be mounted to? I.E. CMU, drywall with metal studs, drywall with wood studs, brick?

ANSWER: Mostly dry walls and some locations with tiled walls. 

3) Will the screens be mounted in Landscape or Portrait format?

ANSWER:  The VA San Diego Healthcare System will rely on the contractors' expertise to assess and provide professional recommendations to determine the optimal orientation. As noted in the Statement of Work (SOW), the design must comply with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) requirements. 

4) You mention a range for screen size of 47"-65". Will you require different sized screens for different locations? Should screen size be standardized for all 16 installation locations?

ANSWER: The VA San Diego Healthcare System will rely on the contractors' expertise to assess the space and provide professional recommendation to help the facility make an informed decision. 65” are most likely going near the main entrances and 47” for other locations.

5) Is there any branding, color pallet, or logo placement that should be incorporated into the design of the screens? 

ANSWER: Yes. Similar navy blue color and logo as seen on the main VA website: https://www.va.gov/ shall be used as a starting point for design. 

The contractor shall have design discussion sessions with VASD for interface design. VASD will be relying on the contractor's design expertise. 

6) Will power and data connections be available at each installation location?

ANSWER: Power will be VASD’s responsibility. Cabling and network set up will be the contractor’s responsibility. 

7) Will the installer be able to utilize the dumpster, compactors, or recycling facilities at each installation location? Or will they be responsible for hauling away all packaging material? 

ANSWER: No. Contractors are responsible for their housekeeping. Note that at the main La Jolla Medical Center contractors shall only use the service elevators. 

8) Can the screens be received at each facility prior to installation? Or can they be received at a central location? Or will the installer be responsible for bringing the screens to each installation location? 

ANSWER: The contractor is responsible for safely storing their materials and tools. No materials, screens, or tools shall be left or stored on-site unsupervised at any time. VASDHS is not responsible for the storage, damage, or loss of any items left at VASDHS's medical center or clinics.

9) Can you clarify the expected time frame for issuing a PO and the desired installation date for the 16 screens? 

ANSWER: The PO will be issued once the contract has an award.  Installation shall begin before June 30th. 

Will floor plans, CAD drawings, or other layout documentation be provided for these locations?

ANSWER: VASD can provide archived floor plan drawings (CAD/PDF). Locations are still being finalized by end users. Generally expected areas include main entrances and lobbies. The VASD will also rely on contractors' expertise to assess the space and provide professional recommendations on this matter.  

Question 6 of the initial RFI response notes that power will be the responsibility of VASD, while cabling and network setup fall to the contractor.

Will both power and data be available at the installation locations? 

Or will the contractor be responsible for extending power and data to each location? 

ANSWER: Network cabling set up will be the contractor’s responsibility. Power will be VASD’s responsibility. 

Are there any active construction projects underway at these facilities? If so, who is serving as the General Contractor?

ANSWER: There are multiple active construction projects under different contractors. Pathfinding screen locations are not affected by these constructions. 

Pg 39 of the SOW requires all items to be delivered by 5-01-2026, however, per RFI response #9 the work is to be performed between 6-30-2026 and 7-31-2026. Further RFI response #8 states that the contractor is responsible for all tools and materials at all times. What is to be delivered on 5-01-2026? 

ANSWER: The PO will be issued once the contract has an award.  Installation shall begin before June 30th.

Request for Information  
 
Oracle Retail and Store Inventory Management (SIM) SaaS Migration 
 
36C10B26Q0323 (VA-26-00059598) 
 
Amendment One 
  
 
 
1. Introduction 
 
This Request for Information (RFI) is for planning purposes only and shall not be 
considered an Invitation for Bid, Request for Task Execution Plan, Request for 
Quotation, or a Request for Proposal.  Additionally, there is no obligation on the part of 
the Government to acquire any products or services described in this RFI.  Your 
response to this RFI will be treated only as information for the Government to consider.  
You will not be entitled to payment for direct or indirect costs that you incur in 
responding to this RFI. This request does not constitute a solicitation for proposals or 
the authority to enter into negotiations to award a contract. No funds have been 
authorized, appropriated or received for this effort. Interested parties are responsible for 
adequately marking proprietary, restricted or competition sensitive information 
contained in their response.  The Government does not intend to pay for the information 
submitted in response to this RFI. 
 
 
The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) for this requirement is 
541512- Computer Systems Design Services with a size standard of $34 million.   
 
2. Submittal Information: 
 
All responsible sources may submit a response in accordance with the below 
information.   
 
There is a page limitation for this RFI of (25) pages.  The Government will not review 
any other information or attachments included, that are in excess of the (25) page limit. 
NO MARKETING MATERIALS ARE ALLOWED AS PART OF THIS RFI.  Generic 
capability statements will not be accepted or reviewed.  Your response must address 
capabilities specific to the services required in the attached PWS and must include the 
following: 
 
a. Interested Vendors shall at a minimum, provide the following information in the 
initial paragraph of the submission: 
 
Name of Company 
Address 
Point of Contact 

Phone Number 
Fax Number 
Email address 
Company Business Size and Status 
                     For VOSB and SDVOSBs, proof of verification in SBA VetCert.  
NAICS code(s) 
Socioeconomic data 
Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) Number 
Existing Contractual Vehicles (GWAC, FSS, or MAC) 
 
b. Provide a summary of your capability to meet the requirements contained 
within the Draft PWS. 
 
c. Please review Attachment A titled “RFI Questions for Industry Responses 
Oracle upgrade” and provide responses to Government questions to 
Industry with your capability statements. 
 
d. Corporate experience or expertise in performing these services and specific 
examples or references.  Specific examples or references provided must include 
the agency, point of contact, dollar value, and contract number. 
 
e. Your company’s intent and ability to meet the set aside requirement in 
accordance with VAAR 852.219-73 (JAN 2023) (DEVIATION) VA Notice of Total 
Set-Aside For Certified SDVOSBs and 13 CFR §125.6, which states the 
contractor will not pay more than 50% of the amount paid by the government to 
the prime for contract performance to firms that are not certified SDVOSBs listed 
in the SBA certification database (excluding direct costs to the extent they are not 
the principal purpose of the acquisition and the SDVOSB/VOSB does not provide 
the service, such as airline travel, cloud computing services, or mass media 
purchases). When a contract includes both services and supplies, the 50 percent 
limitation shall apply only to the service portion of the contract.  
 
f. If you are an authorized Oracle distributor, or re-seller please provide 
authorization letter from the OEM (this letter will not count towards your page 
limit). 
 
g. Has the draft PWS provided sufficient detail to describe the technical 
requirements that encompass the software development and production 
operations support services to be performed under this effort.  
 
______ YES _______ NO (if No, answer question g.) 
 
h. If “NO”, please provide your technical comments/recommendations on elements 
of the draft PWS that may contribute to a more accurate proposal submission 
and efficient, cost-effective effort. 
  

3. Response information 
Responses are due no later than (13:00 EST), (4/22/2026) via email to Contract 
Specialists Justin Daniel at Justin.Daniel@va.gov  and Sean Doyle, at 
Sean.Doyle@va.gov and Evan Schlisserman, Contracting Officer at 
Evan.Schlisserman@va.gov. Please note “Oracle Retail and Store Inventory 
Management (SIM) SaaS Migration” in the subject line of your response. Mark your 
response as “Proprietary Information” if the information is considered business 
sensitive.  The email file size shall not exceed 5 MB.   
**Please note this RFI will be posted at: System For Award Management 
(SAM.gov). Interested vendors only need to reply to one of these postings.

RFI VA-26-00059598 – Questions for Vendor Responses 
The Department of Veterans AƯairs (VA), Veterans Canteen Service (VCS), requests vendor 
feedback regarding the modernization and migration of the Oracle Retail Merchandising 
(RMS) and Store Inventory Management (SIM) platforms to Oracle Retail Merchandising 
Foundation Cloud Service (MFCS) and Store Inventory and Operations Cloud Service 
(SIOCS). 
Please provide concise responses to the following critical questions aligned with Section 5 
of the Performance Work Statement (PWS). 
1. Pricing & SaaS Subscriptions 
1. Subscription Bill of Materials (BOM):   
Provide a line-item, tabular price list for all proposed Oracle Retail SaaS 
subscriptions (MFCS, SIOCS, RPCS, ReIM, Allocation, RICS). For each product, 
include the unit of measure (tenant, user, store, message volume, environment), 
quantities, and pricing for the 12-month base period and each of the four 
12-month option years. Identify any environment-speciﬁc premiums (Dev, Test, 
Prod), overage charges, and price protections.   
Please present the BOM in the following table format: Product, UOM, Quantity 
(Base & Option Years), Unit Price, Extended Price, Overage. 
2. What’s Included vs. Customer Managed:   
Using Oracle’s SaaS shared responsibility model, clearly delineate which 
services are covered by Oracle SLAs versus contractor managed tenant 
responsibilities (e.g., integrations, workﬂows, KPI refreshes, IAM, regression 
testing). Map your delineation to the PWS SLA assignments (Incident Response, 
Integrations, Batch/Orchestration, Reporting/KPIs, IAM). 
3. Assumptions & Dependencies AƯecting Price:   
List any pricing assumptions (e.g. store count, message volume, batch windows, 
mobile devices/peripherals, training seats, third party connectors) and 
dependencies on Oracle environment provisioning & release cycles. State any 
price change triggers.  
4. Discounts & True Up:   
Describe tiered or enterprise discounts, true up/true down mechanisms for 
user/store counts, and how you handle mid-term adds (e.g., new canteens). 
2. Program Approach & Implementation Strategy 

1. Implementation methodology:  
Describe your recommended implementation approach (e.g., Agile, iterative, 
hybrid). How do you manage scope, risks, dependencies, and schedule for SaaS 
modernization programs of similar size and complexity?  
2. Migration feasibility:  
Based on your experience, what major risks or blockers do you anticipate when 
migrating from RMS v13/SIM to MFCS/SIOCS, and how do you mitigate them? 
3. Timeline:  
How long does your assessment phase typically take for comparable 
MFCS/SIOCS migrations? 
3. Assessment, Fit/Gap & Solution Design 
1. Assessment approach:  
Describe your approach to conducting a SaaS migration assessment, including 
discovery of current state integrations, customizations, data structures, and 
store workﬂows.  
2. Fit/Gap minimization:  
How do you ensure the solution maximizes use of standard Oracle cloud 
capabilities and reduces or eliminates the need for extensions? 
4. Data Migration & Data Quality 
1. Migration tools and methods:  
Provide your methodology for conducting data proﬁling, cleansing, 
transformation, and migration into MFCS/SIOCS.  
2. Mock conversions:  
How many mock conversions do you recommend, and what criteria do you use 
to determine readiness for cutover? 
3. Limitations:  
What limitations should VCS expect when migrating legacy RMS/SIM reports to 
MFCS/SIOCS? 
5. Integrations & Technical Architecture 
1. Integration experience:  

Summarize your experience integrating MFCS/SIOCS with ERP , POS, EDI, data 
warehouse, and reporting systems. Identify any recommended redesign 
considerations for VCS’ existing interfaces.  
2. Standards & tools: 
Identify which Oracle integration technologies (e.g., BDI, RIB-Cloud, OIC) you 
typically use for Retail SaaS implementations and why. 
3. Existing tool integration:   
Identify any issues you Forsee with integrating MFCS/SIOCS with existing VCS 
systems.  How do you evaluate whether existing integrations must be rebuilt, 
redesigned, or retired? 
6. Conﬁguration & Store Operations 
1. MFCS/SIOCS conﬁguration:  
Describe your approach to conﬁguring roles, hierarchies, store workﬂows, and 
SIOCS mobile operations.  
2. Environment strategy:  
How do you ensure conﬁguration consistency and traceability across 
Development, Test, and Production environments? 
7. Reporting & Custom-Built Components 
1. Reporting modernization:  
Provide your recommended approach for modernizing or replacing existing 
custom reports and legacy OBIEE/Analytics artifacts within the Oracle Retail 
Cloud ecosystem. 
2. Limitations:  
What limitations should VCS expect when migrating legacy RMS/SIM reports to 
MFCS/SIOCS? 
8. Testing & Quality Assurance 
1. Testing strategy:  
Summarize your testing approach across unit, system, integration, end-to-end, 
performance, and UAT. Identify what automation tools, if any, you use.  
2. Defect management:  

Explain how you manage defects and ensure timely resolution during SIT and 
UAT phases. 
9
