Justification and Approval
For Other than Full and Open Competition
Plan ID Number: 2026-LGD-0076
Form Updated 12/10/2025 
Part 2 – Program Office Justification & Approval
4. Authority for Contracting Without Competition Over $350,000
 
Up to and including $350,000
For this requirement up to $350,000, using simplified acquisition procedures under FAR part 13, the basis for contracting without 
providing for full and open competition is:  
Only one responsible source is reasonably available to meet Library requirements. 41 U.S.C. § 6101(b); FAR 13.106-1.  
 Product or service is reasonably available from only one source.
Source controls copyrights, patents or other exclusive licensing arrangements.
 Urgent circumstances; only one source can reasonably deliver by required date. Option years are not permitted.  
Source is expressly authorized or required by statute: Identify the authority
Library’s needs can only be satisfied by a brand-name product  
The Product is listed the Library of Congress Enterprise Architecture Repository (LCEAR) database (Attach applicable  
IT Standard Data Sheet)
 
5. Rationale for Contracting Without Competition 
We are authorized to limit competition on the basis of the citation in section 4 because:  
 
The Library of Congress’s BIBFRAME Linked Data System (BFLDS) previously operated in tandem with Voyager and, as of 
June 2025, now interfaces with FOLIO as the Library’s system of record. BFLDS consists of the BIBFRAME database 
(BFDB), Linked Data Service (LDS ID), and the Marva editor, with LDS ID and BFDB built on the MarkLogic platform, 
which incorporates triplestore functionality. BFLDS and FOLIO were developed using distinct and not fully compatible 
architectures, requiring specialized middleware to enable efficient interoperability. 
Under contract LCLGD25C0002, IndexData conducted a comprehensive analysis, working directly with the original 
FOLIO developers, of how BFLDS and FOLIO can be integrated. This effort produced detailed, implementation-ready 
recommendations for middleware design, workflow optimization, and data exchange. 
 
IndexData is uniquely qualified to perform this work due to its direct role in both the design and development of FOLIO 
and the development of core components of BFLDS. As a result, IndexData possesses institutional and technical 
knowledge of system architecture, design decisions, and integration constraints that is not documented to a level 
sufficient for transfer to another contractor without additional time and cost. 
 
Awarding this requirement to another contractor would require the new contractor to independently reconstruct 
IndexData’s prior analysis, reverse-engineer design decisions across both systems, and develop equivalent expertise. 
This would result in significant duplication of effort, increased cost to the Government, and schedule delays. 
 
Accordingly, IndexData is the only responsible source capable of meeting the Library’s requirement within the required 
timeframe, without duplication of costs. 
6. Actions to Overcome Barriers to Competition Before Subsequent Contract Is Required  
To increase competition, we will: continue to survey the environment for possible expertise. 
 
7. Program Office Certification  
I certify that the description of the Government’s minimum needs, schedule requirements, and technical information that provide 
the basis for this justification are accurate and complete.  
  Procurement Analyst   1/2 0/26 
________________________ 
Name
___________________________
Title Signature 
______________
Date 


Justification and Approval

Plan ID Number: 2026-LGD-0076

0BPART 3 – CONTRACTING OFFICER DETERMINATIONS & CERTIFICATION
8. Efforts to Solicit from As Many Potential Sources As Practicable 
Unless a justification is based on FAR 6.302-2, Unusual and compelling urgency, contracting officers shall post justifications for other than 
full and open competition, including brand name justifications, to the Contract Opportunities website on sam.gov (or eBuy for Brand Name 
Justifications under FAR Part 8) prior to requesting a proposal or otherwise entering into negotiations.
 
Advance notice will be published in sam.gov for 15 days before a contract is awarded. FAR 5.203(a). 
Advance or concurrent notice will be published in sam.gov for a reasonable time for commercial items FAR 5.203(a) and 12.204(b).  
Advance notice will not be published because the action meets the following FAR exception:
Notice would compromise national security. FAR 5.202(a)(1).  
Unusual and compelling urgency; notice period would seriously injure Government. FAR 5.202(a)(2). 
Expressly authorized or required to be made through another Government agency. FAR 5.202(a)(4).
Order will be placed against an IDIQ contract awarded under FAR 16.5. FAR 5.202(a)(6).
Order will be placed against GSA schedule or other competitively-awarded contract. FAR 5.202(a)(11). 
Other:
Advance notice will not be published per 41 U.S.C. § 6101; see attached deviation from the FAR per LCR 7-210 § 3.A.
We made the following efforts to interest potential sources in this award: a justification for other than full and open competition 
will be posted to SAM.gov as a pre-solicitation notice prior to requesting a quote or otherwise entering into negotiations, in 
accordance with LC06.305 Availability of the Justification.
9. Listing of Sources That Expressed Interest in Writing 
The following sources expressed interest in this award: None  
 
 
10. Additional Information 
The following additional information is relevant to this justification and approval: None 
 
 
11. Contracting Officer Determination that Price Is Fair and Reasonable  
As explained in detail below, I have determined that the prices for this contracting action are fair and reasonable based on 
comparison of the prices to the following (see FAR 15.404-1(b)(2)):  
Historical prices paid by the Government     Published competitive price lists 
Library’s Independent Government Estimate    Prices obtained through market research 
Other:              Prices obtained through competitive proposals
The following analysis (based on attached pricing information) supports my determination: This action is valued at approximately 
 as documented in the Library’s IGCE. Once a quote has been submitted by IndexData, their pricing data, such as level of 
effort and unit prices, will be compared to the Library’s IGCE, in addition to the pricing provided in their prior contract 
LCLGD25C0002. It is anticipated that similar labor categories and levels of expertise will be required for this work, providing 
an accurate comparison for price analysis. Award will only be made after a price reasonableness determination has been 
made. 
 
12. Contracting Officer Determination of Responsibility  
Checked System for Award Management (or will check in the case of competitive proposals for a brand name product) 
     
13. Contracting Officer Determination of Best Value
I have determined that this action represents the best value to the Government because: As previously stated, IndexData is 
uniquely qualified to perform this work due to its direct role in both the design and development of FOLIO and the 
development of core components of BFLDS. As a result, IndexData possesses institutional and technical knowledge of 
system architecture, design decisions, and integration constraints that is not documented to a level sufficient for 
transfer to another contractor without additional time and cost. Therefore, awarding to IndexData therefore provides the

limited budget.

approve contracting without competition for this requirement.

search_text:
SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE 

622673622


The Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) is seeking sources for Defense Enterprise Office Solution (DEOS) Department of Defense (DoD) Office 365 Operations and Maintenance. This requirement will support DISA Portfolio Acquisition Executive Services (PAE Services) (SD), Enterprise Collaboration Services Division (SD3). 

CONTRACTING OFFICE ADDRESS:  

DISA/DITCO-NCR 

6910 COOPER AVE, 

FT. MEADE, MD 20755

INTRODUCTION:

This is a SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE to determine the availability and technical capability of large and small businesses (including the following subsets: Small Disadvantaged Businesses, Certified 8(a), Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Businesses, HUBZone Small Businesses and Woman-Owned Small Businesses) to provide the required products and/or services. 

DISA SD / SD3 is seeking information from potential sources for: providing engineering, operations and cybersecurity support for the sustainment of and engineering of the DEOS DoD Office 365 Non-classified Internet Protocol Router Network (NIPRNet) and Secret Internet Protocol Router Network (SIPRNet) Tenants and potentially a Top-Secret Office-365 Tenant. 

DoD selected Microsoft Office 365 (M365) as the solution to provide common communication, collaboration, and productivity capabilities that are mission-effective, efficient, widely accessible, and facilitate DoD operations worldwide. DISA, in support of the Deputy Secretary of Defense’s direction to accelerate DoD’s adoption of cloud computing technology, plans to continue to build on its seamlessly integrated enterprise M365 as a replacement for disparate DoD legacy enterprise information technology services, such as individual voice, video, collaboration, email, content management, information management, and productivity suites. 

DEOS PMO operates the DoD365-Joint tenant (IL5) with approximately 137k users across Defense Agencies and Field Activities (DAFA) and Combatant Commands (CCMD) organizations; and the DoD365-Sec tenant (IL6) with approximately 350k users across the Department’s DAFAs, CCMDs, and MILDEPs. Additionally, at IL6, DEOS PMO operates two Five Eyes (FVEY) tenants for United Kingdom and Australia with the potential to add additional FVEY partner nations. Planning estimates for an IL7 environment are at approximately 150k users across DoD. DEOS PMO continuously evolves DoD365 capabilities as Microsoft 365 service offerings are enhanced and mission requirements emerge.  

The anticipated period of performance is a one-year base period and four one-year optional periods.

The anticipated period of performance is:

Base Year		April 3, 2027 – April 2, 2028 

Option Year 1		April 3, 2028 – April 2, 2029 

Option Year 2		April 3, 2029 – April 2, 2030 

Option Year 3		April 3, 2030 – April 2, 2031 

Option Year 4		April 3, 2031 – April 2, 2032

The anticipated place of performance is at DISA Headquarters (6910 Cooper Ave, Fort Meade, MD, 20755), DISA Columbus, Ohio (3990 E. Broad St., Bldg. 23 Columbus, OH 43218-1152), and any DISA-approved contractor Secure Facility at the convenience of the contractor.

DISCLAIMER: 

THIS SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE IS FOR INFORMATIONAL PURPOSES ONLY. THIS IS NOT A REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL. IT DOES NOT CONSTITUTE A SOLICITATION AND SHALL NOT BE CONSTRUED AS A COMMITMENT BY THE GOVERNMENT. RESPONSES IN ANY FORM ARE NOT OFFERS AND THE GOVERNMENT IS UNDER NO OBLIGATION TO AWARD A CONTRACT AS A RESULT OF THIS NOTICE. NO FUNDS ARE AVAILABLE TO PAY FOR PREPARATION OF RESPONSES TO THIS NOTICE. ANY INFORMATION SUBMITTED BY RESPONDENTS IS STRICTLY VOLUNTARY.

CONTRACT/PROGRAM BACKGROUND: 

The DEOS DoD365 Operations and Maintenance requirement is a recurring requirement currently being executed under the following contract:

Contract Number: 47QTCA21A0001 / 47QFSA21F0023

Contract Vehicle: General Services Administration (GSA) Single-Award Blanket Purchase Agreement (BPA)

Incumbent: General Dynamics Information Technology, Large Business

Method of previous acquisition: Task Order under Single Award Blanket Purchase Agreement 

Period of performance: 05 Mar 2021 - 4 May 2026 

The DISA DEOS also requires optional tasks to be incorporated into the follow-on contract for an IL7 (Top-Secret) solution. Therefore, in addition to the recurring requirements being performed in the existing Task Order above, the IL7 optional task requirements will also be included.

Additionally, the DISA anticipates awarding an additional one-year task order under the above single-award BPA, Contract Number 47QTCA21A0001, for a period of performance of 05 May 2026 - 04 May 2027.

REQUIRED CAPABILITIES: 

The DISA DEOS DoD 365 team requires a contractor to sustain, operate, engineer, and provide common communication, collaboration, and productivity capabilities to facilitate DoD operations worldwide. The Government is seeking contractors capable of providing subject matter expertise in support of this effort. 

Microsoft 365 (M365) Engineering and Operational Expertise (IL5, IL6, & IL7): Responses shall demonstrate experience and technical expertise in providing engineering, operations, and cybersecurity support for M365 environments at Impact Level 5 (IL5 – NIPRNet), Impact Level 6 (IL6 - SIPRNet), and Impact Level 7 (IL7 – Top-Secret) including integrated third-party products.  Describe and provide an example of your company’s experience, successful M365 deployments, troubleshooting complex issues, and optimizing configurations for large-scale DoD environments. Specific experience with Azure Government Unclass (IL5) and Secret (IL6) infrastructure is highly desirable. 


Cybersecurity and Risk Management: Responses shall demonstrate experience providing comprehensive cybersecurity services within DoD cloud environments (Azure preferred), including experience with Risk Management Framework (RMF) implementation, security control assessments, incident response, cyber threat hunting, and compliance with DISA Security Technical Implementation Guides, Information Assurance Vulnerability Management, and other DoD security policies and procedures. The ability to contribute to and maintain an Authority to Operate is critical. Describe and provide an example of your company’s experience providing comprehensive Cybersecurity and RMF Compliance in Cloud Environments (specifically M365, IL5 at a minimum) and DoD cloud environments.  


Knowledge Management and Training Capabilities: Given the need to create and maintain knowledge repositories and potentially develop training materials, responses shall demonstrate capabilities in knowledge management best practices, content creation, and online and virtual instructor-led training development. Describe and provide an example of improving user adoption and satisfaction through effective knowledge sharing and user engagement.

Staffing and Transition Management: Requires a smooth transition-in demanding rapid staffing. Responses shall showcase the ability to rapidly mobilize qualified personnel, manage staffing plans effectively, and execute seamless transitions. Describe and provide an example of your company’s experience managing staffing and transition for large-scale efforts (75-100 people) within 15-30 days of period of performance start date.

SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS: 

Facility Clearance Level. The work to be performed under this contract is up to the Top-Secret level. Therefore, responses shall demonstrate an interim or final Top Secret Facility Clearance from the Defense Security Service Facility Clearance Branch, which will be required at time of proposal submission.  Please include your company’s current facility clearance level in your reply to this notice.

Personnel Security. The work to be performed under this contract is up to the Top-Secret level.  All contractor staff are required to have a minimum of a Secret clearance and a Top Secret clearance will be required as needed to support specific DEOS projects. Describe and provide an example of your company’s experience managing security clearance requirements.    

Key contractor personnel shall be Microsoft 365 Administrator Expert Certified and the vendor shall be authorized to provide Enterprise Cloud Service Offering certified staff support.

Capability Maturity Model Integration (CMMI) Certification: Because of the complexity of the integration and operational management of the DEOS effort and ongoing requirements to develop and integrate new collaboration capabilities, prime contractors shall be at least CMMI Services V3.0 Maturity Level 5 for all contract work and supporting subcontractors shall be at least CMMI Services V3.0 Maturity Level 3 for the life of the contract. Please include your company’s current CMMI level in your reply to this notice and demonstrate the ability to have this certification at time of proposal submission.  

SOURCES SOUGHT:  

The North American Industry Classification System Code (NAICS) for this requirement is 541511 (https://www.census.gov/naics/), with the corresponding size standard of $34 million. In order to make a determination for a small business set-aside, two or more qualified and capable small businesses shall submit responses that demonstrate their qualifications. Responses shall demonstrate the company’s ability to perform in accordance with FAR clause 52.219-14, Limitations on Subcontracting.

To assist DISA in determining the level of participation by small business in any subsequent procurement that may result from this Sources Sought Notice, provide information regarding any plans to use joint ventures (JV) or partnering. Please outline the company's areas of expertise and those of any proposed JV/partner who combined can meet the specific requirements contained in this notice.

SUBMISSION DETAILS:

Responses should include:

Responses to the Required Capabilities and Special Requirements above;

Business name and address; 

Name of company representative and their business title; 

Small Business Socio-economic status (if small); 

Commercial and Government Entity (CAGE) Code;

Prime contract vehicles; to include ENCORE III, SETI, NIH CIO-SP4, NASA SEWP V, GSA: OASIS, ALLIANT II, VETS II, STARS III, MAS (including applicable SIN(s), groups or pools), or any other Government Agency contract vehicle that allows for decentralized ordering. (This information is for market research only and businesses with a valid CAGE code that lack prime contract vehicles are still encouraged to respond to this notice.) 

Businesses who wish to respond shall send a response via email no later than 4:00 PM Eastern Standard Time on  to elizabeth.a.bono.civ@mail.mil and michelle.l.rand.civ@mail.mil . Interested businesses should submit a brief capabilities statement package addressing the specific questions above (no more than seven pages), demonstrating the ability to perform the services listed under Required Capabilities.

Proprietary information and trade secrets, if any, shall be clearly marked on all materials. All information received that is marked Proprietary will be handled accordingly. All submissions become Government property and will not be returned. All Government and contractor personnel reviewing submitted responses will have signed non-disclosure agreements and understand their responsibility for proper use and protection from unauthorized disclosure of proprietary information as pursuant to 41 USC 423. The Government shall not be held liable for any damages incurred if proprietary information is not properly identified.

For information on DISA’s requirements, you can find current information on our home page: www.disa.mil – that includes current forecast and the current acquisition strategies. If there is no information regarding the requirement you may be inquiring about, then there is no additional publicly available information. Please continue to review www.disa.mil.

GENERAL INFORMATION

PLACE OF PERFORMANCE

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

DESCRIPTION

DISCLAIMER: THIS IS A SOURCE SOUGHT NOTICE ONLY. THIS SYNOPSIS IS NOT A REQUEST FOR QUOTE, PROPOSAL, OR BID.

This notice is not a solicitation as defined by FAR 2.101, therefore it shall not be construed as a commitment by the Government to enter into a contract, nor does it restrict the Government to an acquisition approach. All information contained in this RFI is preliminary as well as subject to modification and is in no way binding on the Government. Information submitted in response to this notice is voluntary; the Government will not pay for information requested nor will it compensate any respondent for any cost incurred in developing information provided to the Government. The Government is requesting that restrictive or proprietary markings not be used in response to this notice. If a solicitation is released, it will be synopsized on the SAM.gov or GSA. It is the responsibility of the interested parties to monitor these sites for additional information pertaining to this RFI.

STATEMENT OF WORK: 

Investigational Drug System (IDS); automation software to aid in the management of VAPSHCS’s investigational drug service.

Vendor shall provide IDS, a secure, HIPAA and 21 CFR Part 11 compliant, web-based application that has the following features:

Monitors Receive, Transfer, Dispense, Return to Supplier, Destroy, and Adjustment transactions

Manages a comprehensive drug catalog, protocols, patients, suppliers, documents, and pharmacies

Generates accountability logs exportable to NCI DARF, NCI ORAL DRAF, ACTG, De-Identified and other format, significantly facilitating the audit process

Granular inventory tracking down to an individual Bottle, Vial, or Kit #

Patient-specific drug tracking

Double signature verification

Barcoded inventory labels

Physical inventory monitoring

Par level management

Pharmacy billing

Operates as a standalone system but has interfaces available to reduce data entry, including CTMS, EMR, and IRB systems for protocol, patient, and document management; inpatient or outpatient pharmacy systems for deducting inventory and logging dispenses; and carousels for inventory movement between locations

Comprehensive coverage of all inventory transactions performed with investigational drugs

Simplifies preparation for monitor visits and audits

Ensures dispensing accuracy by tracking a patient’s dispensing history, treatment arms, expiration, and patient-specific inventory

Print patient labels and record inventory deduction on logs in a single dispense transaction

Supports multi-site pharmacy systems and allows users to sign into specific dispensing locations

Minimize workflow errors with role-based access

HIPAA and 21 CFR Part 11 compliant (including audit trail logging)

IDS shall be accessible to staff at the Seattle and American Lake Divisions of VAPSHCS.

GENERAL INFORMATION

PLACE OF PERFORMANCE

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

DESCRIPTION

The Department of Veterans Affairs (VA), Office of Procurement Acquisition and Logistics, Technology Acquisition Center (TAC), located at 23 Christopher Way, Eatontown, New Jersey, is conducting market research to identify potential sources capable of providing an Enterprise Mammography Tracking & Reporting System.

Currently, VA is contemplating a potential requirement for requires an Enterprise Mammography Tracking and Reporting System, software licenses, installation and technical services, and training. The MTRS will provide radiologists and clinical staff with enhanced tools to efficiently manage and monitor VHA mammography programs.

The anticipated Period of Performance shall be 12 months from the date of award, with four 12-month option periods, and four Optional Tasks that may be exercised anytime during performance.

The potential North American Industrial Classification (NAICS) for this effort is 6525. 

See specific requirements in the attached DRAFT Performance Work Statement (PWS).

This Sources Sought notice is issued solely for information and planning purposes. Do not submit a proposal. This notice does not constitute an Invitation for Bid, Request for Task Execution Plan, Request for Quotation, or Request for Proposal, and shall not be construed as a commitment by the Government to award a contract or otherwise acquire any products or services. In accordance with Revolutionary Federal Acquisition Regulation Overhaul VA Class Deviation (RFO) 15.101(c), responses are not offers and cannot be accepted by the Government to form a binding contract. The Government will not reimburse any costs incurred in responding to this notice; all participation is strictly voluntary. No funds have been authorized, appropriated, or received for this effort. Respondents are responsible for properly marking proprietary, restricted, or competition-sensitive information. Information marked as proprietary will be handled accordingly.

Any Service-Disabled Veteran-owned small businesses (SDVOSBs) or Veteran-owned small businesses (VOSBs) responding to this RFI must include their intent and ability to meet set-aside requirements for performance of this effort in accordance with the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) Acquisition Regulation (VAAR) 852.219-73, VA Notice of Total Set-Aside for Certified SDVOSB (DEVIATION) or 852.219-74, VA Notice of Total Set-Aside for Certified VOSB; specifically the requirement for at least 50% of the cost of manufacturing or 50% of the services be performed by yourself and/or other eligible/verified SDVOSB/VOSB concerns; and be listed in the Small Business Administration certification database at the time of submission. 

HOW TO RESPOND

Respondents are encouraged to reply if they have the capabilities to meet the VA’s requirements. Provide a clear, concise, and complete capability package. Respondents shall submit a capability statement limited to 10 pages (excluding transmittal page) describing your company’s ability to meet the requirements outlined in this RFI. 

Include the following in your response:

Company Information: 

Provide Company Information name, address, email, and telephone number 

CAGE/UEI Number under which the company is registered in SAM.gov.

Point of contact having the authority and knowledge to clarify responses with VA representatives.

For small business concerns, indicate whether at least 50% of the total amount paid by the Government will be paid to firms that are similarly situated.

Company Business Size and socioeconomic status, including business type(s)/certifications(s) such as SDB, 8(a), HUBZone, SDVOSB, WOSB, etc. 

If a small business, clearly identify what socioeconomic category you are recommending for this effort. 

If a small business, are you able to comply with RFO 52.219-6 and 52.219-14 in execution of this effort? Are you able to comply with subcontracting limitations in 13 CFR 125.6 in execution of this effort?

Identify if your company is the Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) of the solution or a reseller? If you are the OEM, identify whether you have any resellers available.

Identify existing contract vehicle(s) in which you are a current contract holder that can be used to procure the required services (i.e., General Services Administration Multiple Award Schedule, Government Wide Acquisition Contract, NASA Solution for Enterprise-Wide Procurement (SEWP) Government-Wide Acquisition Contract (GWAC), etc.). 

Your company’s intent and ability to meet the set aside requirement in accordance with VAAR 852.219-73 (JAN 2023) (DEVIATION) and 13 CFR §125.6, which states the contractor will not pay more than 50 percent of the amount paid by the Government to it to firms that are not SDVOSBs.

Brief detailed summary describing your company’s approach to meeting the requirements. Responses should be as complete and informative as possible.

Does the draft Performance Work Statement (PWS) provide sufficient detail to describe the technical and functional requirements that encompass the requirement?  If “NO”, please provide your technical and functional comments, recommendations, and or questions on elements of the draft PWS that may contribute to a more accurate proposal submission and efficient, cost-effective effort.

Provide commercially available list pricing for the solution that can meet the Government’s minimum requirements, including recommended quantities and unit pricing. If commercially available list pricing is not available, provide a Rough Order of Magnitude, and any additional anticipated costs to meet the Governments’ minimum requirements

Respondents are requested to identify any additional information considered applicable to this RFI.

INSTRUCTIONS FOR SUBMITTING QUESTIONS AND RESPONSES

Do not wait until the last minute to submit your responses. To avoid submission of late responses, we recommend the transmission of your response file 24 hours prior to the required response due date and time. Please be advised that timeliness is determined by the date and time an Offeror’s response is received by the Government not when an Offeror attempted transmission. Offerors are encouraged to review and ensure that sufficient bandwidth is available on their end of the transmission. 

Questions and response shall be submitted via email to Terricia Lloyd, Terricia.Lloyd@va.gov. Information submitted any other method will not be considered. 

Submit questions no later than 10:00 am EST on April 20, 2026.  

Submit response no later than 10:00 am EST on April 27, 2026.

The VA reserves the right to not respond to any, all, or select responses or materials submitted. All VA current requirements identified herein are subject to change at any time. VA appreciates your time and anticipated response.

See attached document: P02 Draft PWS.

BACKGROUND

The Department of Veterans Affairs (VA), Veterans Health Administration (VHA),

National Radiology Program Office strives to provide high quality, effective, and efficient solutions to those responsible for providing care to the Veterans at the point-of-care as well as throughout all the points of the Veterans’ health care in an effective, timely and compassionate manner. VA depends on Clinical Information Systems (CIS) to meet mission goals. 

Veterans Integrated Service Networks (VISNs) facilities across VHA may utilize various Radiology Information Systems (RIS) and Picture Archiving and Communication Systems (PACS), in addition to the Electronic Health Record (EHR). Therefore, the proposed Mammography Tracking System must be capable of integrating with these systems to ensure interoperability, accurate data exchange, and compliance with VHA standards. This integration will enable seamless workflow, reduce duplication of effort, and support comprehensive reporting and patient care management. Furthermore, VHA has substantial interface requirements between the proposed Mammography Tracking and Reporting System (MTRS), the Veterans Health Information and Systems Technology Architecture (VistA), and Federal Electronic Health Record (FEHR). This integration is critical to enable seamless workflow, reduce duplication of effort, support comprehensive reporting, and enhance patient care management.

The Veterans Health Administration (VHA) requires an Enterprise Mammography Tracking and Reporting System, software licenses, installation and technical services, and training. The MTRS will provide radiologists and clinical staff with enhanced tools to efficiently manage and monitor VHA mammography programs.

search_text:
1. Purpose and Objective

The US Government (Caspian Team at UP) is seeking innovative commercial solutions to manage, standardize, and validate high-volume data streams. The primary objective is to maintain the integrity of the SchemaOne data standard while leveraging automation and dedicated 24/7/365 on-call technical expertise to accelerate the ingestion of time-series logs and cyber threat intelligence (CTI).

2. The Problem

Currently, the Government faces challenges in maintaining consistency across diverse, voluminous datasets that flow at all hours. As data sources evolve, the manual effort required to map and validate data creates bottlenecks. Furthermore, out-of-cycle data breaks and schema drifts occur outside of standard business hours, leading to data loss or corruption. We require a partner who can eliminate configuration drift and data acquisition irregularities via a solution that includes 24/7/365 operational monitoring and immediate on-call remediation to ensure the reliability of mission-critical data.

3. Desired Outcomes & Technical Requirements

The Government is looking for vendors to demonstrate capabilities in the following four pillars:

Lifecycle Leadership & Governance: Provide end-to-end management of the SchemaOne standard. This includes maintaining the framework and facilitating stakeholder alignment through the CCB. Vendors must provide a 24/7/365 on-call support structure capable of addressing critical schema failures or ingestion blockers in real-time.

Intelligent Data Mapping: Deliver a scalable solution to map ingested datasets to the SchemaOne specification. The solution must ensure absolute consistency in field names and formats across disparate sources.

High-Fidelity Transformation: Provide a robust mechanism to transform raw source data into SchemaOne-compliant formats with minimal latency and high-availability (HA) failover.

Continuous Validation & 24/7 Health Monitoring: Implement a solution to continuously validate ingested data. This system must proactively detect irregularities and trigger automated alerts to on-call personnel, ensuring that configuration drift is corrected immediately, regardless of the hour or day.

4. AI Enhancement & Efficiency Criteria

A key differentiator for proposed solutions will be the integration of AI to drive operational efficiencies and support on-call responders. The Government expects the vendor to demonstrate how AI/ML will:

Augment On-Call Responsiveness: Use AI-driven diagnostics to provide on-call engineers with instant root-cause analysis for data failures.

Reduce Time-to-Field: Accelerate the transition of new datasets into production.

Minimize Error & Fatigue: Use automated reasoning to reduce the volume of "false positive" alerts, ensuring that 24/7 on-call resources are focused on genuine mission-critical issues.

1 
 
 
Request for Information (RFI) #140D0426R0032 
Department of the Interior (DOI), Interior Business Center (IBC) – Human 
Resources Directorate (HRD), IBC-HRD – AI Tools and Automations 
 
General Information 
This RFI notice is issued solely for information and planning purposes - it does not constitute an invitation for 
bid, request for quotation (RFQ), request for proposal (RFP), or an obligation on behalf of the Government or 
a promise to issue a solicitation in the future. The Government is not seeking proposals and will not accept 
unsolicited proposals at this time. Vendors responding to this notice are advised that the U.S. Government 
will not pay for any information or administrative costs incurred in response to this RFI; all costs associated 
with responding to this RFI will be solely at the interested party's expense. Not responding to this RFI does 
not preclude participation in any future solicitation, if issued. Interested parties are responsible for CLEARLY 
MARKING proprietary or competition sensitive information contained in their response. No classified, 
confidential, or sensitive information should be included in your response. Please be advised that all 
submissions become Government property and will not be returned. Marketing brochures and/or generic 
company literature will not be considered and shall not be sent in response to this RFI. 
Purpose of RFI 
IBC’s Human Resources Directorate seeks information from industry on AI tools and applications capable of 
securely supporting a mainframe-centric, payroll-focused, PII-regulated environment across the full systems 
software development lifecycle and operational landscape. HRD maintains and enhances an integrated 
personnel and payroll system, along with multiple supporting subsystems, that interface with numerous 
Federal systems and serve more than 300,000 employees across multiple agencies and commissions. 
HRD is seeking solutions that can automate testing (script, unit, regression, load, performance), strengthen 
batch processing and JCL scripting/job-run comparisons, and support comprehensive database management 
(configuration, capacity and space utilization, security recognition, forecasting and drift detection, patching, 
migrations, conversions, and integrity). HRD also requires capabilities tailored to payroll operations, 
including payroll calculations, retroactive processing, W-2 and ACA processing, annual pay adjustments, and 
file generation and transmission. 
Additional areas of interest include AI-enabled code and infrastructure analysis and refactoring (mainframe 
module assessment, call-routine mapping, bug identification, historical change analysis, and LLM-based 
scanning), automated generation of system documentation, training materials, and analytical reports, and 
customer service workflow automation (ticket creation and routing, self-service tools, legal and payroll 
guidance). Solutions must support cross-platform integration with Federal partners such as OPM, IRS, and 
shared service providers; operate in a non-multi-tenant environment; align with FedRAMP controls; meet 

 
 
 
2 
 
 
Section 508 requirements; and provide governance, auditability, scalability, and deployment options suitable 
for IBC HRD. 
Responses to this RFI will help HRD identify viable industry solutions and vendors capable of meeting these 
mission needs and will inform the development of future procurement strategies. 
Background: IBC as a Shared Services Provider 
IBC is an Office of Personnel Management (OPM) authorized Shared Service Provider (SSP) that provides 
systems and services to its parent, DOI, and 54 other federal agencies (customers). HRD fully maintains and 
enhances its integrated personnel/payroll system and its supporting sub systems. HRD’s integrated personnel 
and payroll system and sub systems interface with several Government computer systems which service more 
than 300,000 employees (customers) from numerous Federal agencies and commissions.  
 
The operational environment includes: 
 
• Large-scale mainframe and batch processing ecosystems 
• Mission-critical payroll and personnel systems containing Personally Identifiable Information (PII) 
• Extensive JCL, ADBAS, batch scheduling, and interface dependencies 
 
These sub systems complement HRD’s integrated mainframe personnel/payroll system in various ways to 
include: 
• Payroll/personnel data warehousing 
• Service management ticketing  
• Timekeeping and attendance 
• Workforce tracking 
• Learning, development, and performance management 
• Planning, hiring, tracking 
 
Our systems provide thorough support throughout the entire development and maintenance life cycle. This 
includes the creation and implementation of methodologies and standards, in-depth requirements analysis, and 
comprehensive business process evaluation. Additionally, we conduct extensive technology research, manage 
communications effectively, and design robust system architecture. Our expertise extends to system 
development and maintenance, software development, application re-hosting, and the conversion of legacy 
systems. By integrating these diverse elements, we ensure that our solutions are both innovative and reliable, 
while meeting the highest standards of quality and performance. 
 
Objectives 
  
IBC-HRD is seeking a Vendor who provides an AI-Tool or Application which will compliment HRD systems 
in their ability to:  
 
• Automate and Produce 
o Script test software 
o Unit test software 
o Regression test 
o Load test 
o Performance test 
o Batch processing capabilities 
o Database configuration – preserving integrity and infrastructures 

 
 
 
3 
 
 
o Database space utilization 
o Database security recognition 
o Patch upgrades 
o New client migrations   
 
• Online/Production – Automated job run and compares 
o QA (Quality Assurance) processing and testing 
o Production script case – data input 
o W2s 
o Annual pay raise implementation 
o ACA (Affordable Care Act) processing 
o Software version release cadence 
o File creation, generation. Transmission (internal and external) 
o Payroll calculation and retroactive processing  
o Workflow generation 
 
• Assess Production and Test Databases  
o Conversions of software 
o Data transfer and conversion 
o Integration 
o Database performance 
o Capacity 
o Usage logs 
o Security 
o JCL scripting  
 
• Analyze code and infrastructures 
o Ability to scan various LLMs (large language models) in its entirety 
o Mainframe 
o Refactoring code 
o Module assessment 
o Call routines  
o Bug fixes 
o Historical pinpoints of software modifications 
o Mapping 
 
• Create system documentation and training materials 
 
• Generate data analysis reports among systems 
 
• Customer Service Ticket Generation 
o Situational customer ticket creation  
o Assigning of ticket to corresponding branch or working group 
o Associated and relevant resources applicable for usage with ticket generation 
 Legal documents 
 Payroll or personnel guides 
o Job aids 
o Bots 
 
• Self Help Feature 
o Situational customer inquiry for payroll or personnel processing 

 
 
 
4 
 
 
o Associated and relevant resources applicable for payroll or personnel processing 
o Bots  
 
• Cross platform compatibility 
o Assess HRD’s systems and ability to work in partnership supporting HRD functions 
o Integrate with non HRD systems and Federal Agency partnering  
 OPM – Office of Personnel Management 
 IRS – Internal Revenue Service 
 SSPs – Shared Service Providers 
 AQD – Acquisitions Services Directorate (IBC) 
 FMD – Financial Management Directorate (IBC) 
 OMB – Office Management Budget  
 
IBC-HRD Essentials    
 
• Non-Sole Source methods for IBC-HRD’s determination of partnering with an AI-
Tool/Application or Vendor. 
  
• Non-multi-tenant environments 
o The AI-Tool/Application will not be a multi-tenant environment for users outside of IBC-
HRD. 
 
• IBC Policies and Security  
o Support the enforcement of IBC IT and Security Policies and Procedures. 
o FedRAMP-aligned controls  
o 508 Compliance 
o System security plans and policies provided 
 
Submission Requirements 
Vendors interested in participating in this market research activity are requested to submit the following 
(Note: if vendors are proposing more than one solution, please provide a separate response for each proposed 
solution): 
1. Corporate Information requested in Section 1. 
2. Narrative capability statements addressing the business requirements in Section 2. Please limit your 
response to each business requirement to 2 pages (font no smaller than 11pt). General marketing 
information or reference to vendor web site as a response to any of the business requirements will not 
be considered responsive to this notice.  (7-page maximum) 
3. Rough Order of Magnitude in Section 3.  
Vendors must identify any proprietary information submitted in response to this RFI. The proprietary 
information must be clearly marked as proprietary. The government will not treat any other information as 
proprietary. 
The Government reserves the right to contact, or not contact, any party responding to this RFI to obtain 
further information for market research purposes. The government will not notify respondents of the results of 
the information received and will not return submittals to the sender.  
All interested sources must respond to any potential future solicitation announcements separately from 
responses to this RFI.  

 
 
 
5 
 
 
Interested sources that believe they have the ability to provide the solution to the requested functionality listed 
herein should submit responses electronically via email to Christina Lene (christina_lene@ibc.doi.gov) and 
Lilian Huyen (lhuyen@ibc.doi.gov). Please reference “HRD AI Tools and Automations RFI # 
140D0426R0032 ”  in the email subject line. No telephone, mail, or fax responses will be accepted. Other 
than the opportunity to ask questions as detailed below, inquiries for additional information and/or a request 
for a meeting to discuss this RFI/sources sought notice will not be accepted. Responses must be received by 
5:00pm EST, April 17th, 2026.  Please be advised that it is the responders’ responsibility to ensure the 
Government receives your RFI submission on or before the specified due date. 
Questions 
Written questions are to be submitted via email to the contact information listed below, by no later than 
March 17
th, 2026, 3:00pm EST. 
Christina Lene at christina_lene@ibc.doi.gov  and Lilian Huyen at lhuyen@ibc.doi.gov   
All questions must be submitted by the date and time listed above or they may not be addressed before the 
RFI closing date. All questions received by the due date will be answered via an amendment to the RFI.

11) Describe any third-party alliances,

solution(s) 
 

 
 
 
7 
 
 
Section 2 – HRD AI Tools and Automations Requirements 
Vendors shall provide written responses describing how their AI tools or applications meet the objectives and 
essentials defined by IBC-HRD. Responses must address both the objective perspective (how the solution 
advances HRD’s mission) and the essentials perspective (how the solution meets required technical, security, 
and operational standards). Responses should be detailed enough for IBC-HRD to determine solution 
suitability, while not exceeding seven total pages. 
 
IBC-HRD welcomes a wide range of submission formats to help the organization understand the capabilities, 
maturity, and applicability of AI tools and applications that may support its mission. Vendors may include 
any combination of the following categories to demonstrate the breadth and depth of their solutions. 
 
• Written Submissions 
o Demonstrating your technical approach and methodology 
o User story papers showing real-world applications  
o Case studies highlighting implementation success 
o Capability statements  
 
• Visual Demonstrations 
o Product videos

o Interface screenshots

• Access to sandbox environment for hands-on exploration

o Zero Trust alignment description

(a)  For other than firm fixed priced contract line item numbers (CLINs), the Contractor agrees to segregate costs incurred under

CLIN level, rather than at the total contract/TO level, and to submit invoices reflecting costs incurred at that level.  Supporting 
documentation in Wide Area Workflow (WAWF) for invoices shall include summaries of work charged during the period 
covered as well as overall cumulative summaries by individual labor categories, rates, and hours (both straight time and 
overtime) invoiced; as well as, a cost break down of other direct costs (ODCs), materials, and travel, by technical instruction 
(TI), SLIN, or CLIN level.  For other than firm fixed price subcontracts, subcontractors are also required to provide labor 
categories, rates, and hours (both straight time and overtime) invoiced; as well as, a cost breakdown of ODCs, materials, and 
travel invoiced.  Supporting documentation may be encrypted before submission to the prime contractor for WAWF invoice 
submittal.  Subcontractors may email encryption code information directly to the Contracting Officer and Contracting Officer

7.6.4-01 - Inspection and Acceptance - July 2008
(a) All goods and services shall be subject to inspection and test by the FDIC Oversight Manager, to the extent
practicable, at all times and places during the term of the award.  All inspections by the FDIC shall be made in such
a manner as not to unduly delay the work.
 
(b) The FDIC shall have fifteen (15) business days from the date of Contractor''s delivery to determine if such goods
and services are in compliance with the requirements of the contract.  If any services performed or goods delivered
hereunder are not in conformity with the requirements of this Award, the FDIC shall have the right to require
Contractor to reperform the services or redeliver the goods in conformity with the requirements of the Award, at no
additional increase in total contract amount.  When the services to be performed are of such a nature that the defect
cannot be corrected by reperformance of the services, the FDIC shall have the right to (1) require Contractor
immediately to take all necessary steps to ensure future performance of the services in conformity with the
requirements of the contract; and (2) reduce the contract price to reflect the reduced value of the services
performed.  In the event Contractor fails promptly to reperform the services or redeliver the goods, or to take
necessary steps to ensure future performance of the services or delivery of the goods in conformity with the
requirements of the Award, the FDIC shall have the right to either (1) by contract or otherwise, have the services
performed or the goods delivered in conformity with the contract requirements and charge to Contractor any cost
occasioned to the FDIC that is directly related to the performance of such services or the delivery of such goods; or
(2) terminate this Award for default as provided in 7.6.6-02, Termination for Default.
 
(c) Contractor shall provide and maintain an inspection system acceptable to the FDIC covering the goods or
services to be delivered or performed hereunder.  Records of all inspection work by Contractor shall be kept
complete and available to the FDIC during the term of this Award and for such longer period as may be specified
elsewhere in this Award.

7.3.2-41 - FDIC Personnel - July 2008
(a)  FDIC Oversight Manager.  The Oversight Manager is the person designated in writing by the Contracting
Officer to represent the FDIC for the purpose of monitoring technical performance and accepting goods or services.
The Oversight Manager is not authorized to issue any instructions or directions which effect any substantive change
in this contract, including, but not limited to, an increase or decrease in the price of this contract, or a change in the
delivery date(s) or Period of Performance.  Specific areas of delegated authority are more particularly defined in the
Oversight Manager Appointment Memorandum. 
 
The Oversight Manager is Duane Bouie. He can be reached at dbouie@fdic.gov.
 
 
(b)  FDIC Contracting Officer.  The Contracting Officer is the person with FDIC-delegated authority to enter into,
modify, administer, and terminate contracts and orders.
 
The Contracting Officer is Diamond Toles. She can be reached at ditoles@fdic.gov.
 
7.5.13-01 - Method of Payment - Electronic Fund Transfer (EFT) - March 2014
(a) Payment methods.  Payments by the FDIC may be made by check or electronic funds transfer (EFT), or by a
third party in lieu of payment directly from the FDIC, at the option of the FDIC. If the FDIC makes payment by EFT,
the FDIC may, at its option, also forward the associated payment information by electronic transfer.  Any third party
payments will be made by the FDIC''s commercial purchase card issuer.  In the event Contractor certifies in writing
to the payment office that Contractor does not have an account with a financial institution or an authorized payment
agent, the FDIC would make payments by other than EFT.
 
(b) Contractor Payment Requests.  If the FDIC elects for third party payments to be made, Contractor shall make
payment requests through a charge to the FDIC purchase card with the third party, at the time and for the amount
due in accordance with the terms of this contract.  Contractor and the third party shall agree that payments due
under this contract shall be made upon submittal of payment requests to the third party in accordance with the
terms and conditions of an agreement between Contractor, the Contractor''s financial agent (if any), and the third
party and its agents (if any).  No payment shall be due the Contractor until such agreement is made.   Payments
made or due by the third party are not subject to the Prompt Payment Act or any implementation thereof in this
contract.  Documentation of each charge against the FDIC''s purchase card shall be provided to the Contracting
Officer upon request.

title: Extension of Sole Source Urgent & Compelling Services to operate and Maintain Metro Energy Generation System (MEGS)
search_text:
DEPARTMENT OF THE NAVY 
NAVAL FACILITIES ENGINEERING SYSTEMS COMMAND SOUTHWEST 
750 PACIFIC HIGHWAY 
SAN DIEGO CA 92132-5190 
 
 
IN REPLY REFER TO 
            
           J&A No. 26-221 
 
JUSTIFICATION AND APPROVAL 
FOR USE OF OTHER THAN FULL AND OPEN COMPETITION 
1. Contracting Activity.  
Department of the Navy, Naval Facilities Engineering Systems Command Southwest (NAVFAC SW), 
Facilities Engineering Acquisition Division (FEAD), San Diego, California. 
2. Description of the Action Being Approved:   
Request authority to award a sole source contract, without the use of full and competition procedures, to 
Energy Technology and Service, Inc. located at 2637 Ariane Drive, San Diego, CA 92117 in support of 
the Metro Energy Generation System (MEGS).    
3. Description of Supplies/Services:   
The proposed contract will provide services to operate and maintain the MEGS for the Navy Medical 
Center San Diego (NMCSD) within the NAVFAC SW area of responsibility (AOR).  The period of 
performance for the proposed contract is an extension for 6-months. 
The MEGS is an Iconics Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition System (SCADA).  MEGS provides a 
control system that continuously monitors the operation and performance of the NMCSD Co-Generation 
(COGEN) utility system.  The COGEN system provides continuous, reliable, on-site utilities (electrical 
power, water, steam, compressed air) for the NMCSD.  The system includes generation units, control 
systems, automatic transfer switching, emissions and compliance equipment, and integration with 
NMCSD’s existing electrical infrastructure.   
          E s t i m a t e d  D o l l a r  V a l u e  
The estimated dollar value of this contract action is approximately $255,00.00 based on the Independent 
Government Estimate (IGE), utilizing historical data, input from subject matter experts, and market 
research. The Government estimate and period of performance are described below: 
 
PERIOD 
PERIOD OF 
PERFORMANCE 
 
LABOR 
                  
ODC 
 
TOTAL 
BASE  3/25/26 – 9/30/2026 $215,000.00 $40,000.00 $255,000.00 
     
TOTAL    $255,000.00 
 
 
 


J&A No. 26-221 
2 
4. Statutory Authority Permitting Other Than Full and Open Competition: 
The statutory authority permitting other than full and open competition is 10 USC § 3204 (a)(2), as 
implemented by the Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) 6.302-2, Unusual and Compelling Urgency, 
which permits contracting without providing for full and open competition in situations where (1) an 
unusual and compelling urgency precludes full and open competition and (2) delay in award of a contract 
would result in serious injury, financial or other, to the Government. 
5. Rationale Justifying Use of Cited Statutory Authority:  
NMCSD is a 24/7 critical medical facility supporting active-duty service members, veterans, and their 
families.  The facility requires an on-site generation system capable of delivering uninterrupted utilities 
(electrical power, water, steam, compressed air) to critical care areas, operating rooms, and life-support. 
The MEGS must seamlessly integrate all utility systems without operational downtime, while providing 
redundant and load-balanced power generation with automated fault detection and correction. The risk of 
failure to meet these mission critical requirements to the support Naval Medical Center San Diego would 
pose unacceptable risk to the health and safety of patients and Government personnel. 
Historically, MEGS support has been provided via contract N6247325P3803, awarded by FEAD San 
Diego on 25 SEP 2025.  That contract is set to expire on 24 MAR 2026.  The prior MEGS support was 
provided via contract W912DY-18-D-0003/P0003, awarded by the USACE on 18 NOV 2015.  That 
contract expired 03 DEC 2020.  Following the USACE contract, MEGS support has been provided as a 
rider to various NMCSD construction and maintenance contracts. For example, the NMCSD Automated 
Transfer Switch (ATS) upgrade and maintenance contract (N62473-22-F-4456) provided MEGS support.   
The period of performance of the proposed contract is six (6) months, which does not exceed the 
maximum time allowed for sole source acquisitions based on the unusual and compelling urgency 
statutory authority.  The proposed contract action will allow sufficient time to conduct a competitive 
acquisition via the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (USACE) existing Utility Monitoring and Control 
System (UMCS) Programmatic Multiple Award Task Order Contract (MATOC) V. 
A site survey is underway to assess the existing MEGS conditions.  The results of the survey will 
determine the best, most cost effective way to define the requirement and implement needed 
improvements.    
The incumbent contractor, Energy Technology and Service, Inc. (ETS), has been the primary provider of 
MEGS support.  Consequently, ETS has unique and specialized knowledge of the system to ensure 
mission critical requirements are met.  No other contractor could provide the localized knowledge and 
immediate services necessary to operate and maintain the existing MEGS System without a significant 
lead time and unacceptable delay.  Such a delay would result in serious injury to the Government by 
creating an undue risk of loss of vital services at the Naval Medical Center San Diego.   
Pursuant to the authority of 10 U.S.C. § 3204(a)(2), as implemented by FAR 6.302-2, this Justification & 
Approval (J&A) requests the authority to limit competition to this contractor and award a one-time 
contract based on the unusual and compelling urgency to provide services without disruption or impact to 
the mission for Navy installations within the NAVFAC SW AOR. The contractor is capable of satisfying 
the agency’s unusual and compelling requirement starting on March 25, 2026. 
 

J&A No. 26-221 
3 
6. Description of Efforts Made to Solicit Offers from as Many Offerors as Practicable:  
 
NAVFAC conducted a limited market survey, including outreach to several energy generation and 
engineering firms, review of GSA Schedule offerings, and analysis of similar government procurements.  
No other vendor or manufacturer demonstrated the ability to meet all NMCSD’s technical, environmental, 
and mission resiliency requirements without significant phase-in/ramp-up time.  Due to the urgency of 
this project, standard market research procedures for other qualified firms could not be conducted in 
accordance with FAR Part 10.  Other than full and open competition is necessitated by an unusual or 
compelling urgency under the authority of 10 U.S.C. § 3204(a)(2) as implemented in FAR 6.302-2 and 
offers were requested from as many potential sources as was practicable under the circumstances in 
accordance with FAR 6.302-2(c)(2). The planned action covered by this J&A meets an exception to the 
synopsis requirements per FAR Part 5.202(a)(2). The approved J&A will be released in accordance with 
FAR 6.305(b).   
 
7. Determination of Fair and Reasonable Cost:  
The Contracting Officer has determined the anticipated cost to the Government of the services covered by 
this J&A will be fair and reasonable. 
 
8. Actions taken to Remove Barriers to Future Competition:  
 
NAVFAC will utilize the pending COGEN Site Survey assessing the existing SCADA system’s 
conditions to the best, most cost effective way to implement any new improvements.   This is a one-time 
extension of the existing contract that is necessary to avoid delay that would cause serious injury, 
financial or other, to the Government, and is necessary to provide immediate, continuous, reliable, and 
compliant on-site electrical power generation for the NMCSD. It is anticipated that the Government will 
utilize US Army Corps of Engineers (USACE) Utility Monitoring and Control System (UMCS) 
Programmatic MATOC V contract vehicle for this requirement, which will be competitively awarded.

Category Question Answer
1 Incumbent Contract Information Are there current contracts and contractors performing any of these tasks? If so, what are the contractor 
names and contract numbers? No, we currently do not have any contracts or contractors performing these tasks. 
2 Architecture & Mainframe Environment What specific mainframe platforms and programming environments are currently in use (e.g., z/OS, 
ADABAS/Natural, COBOL versions)? Most specifically, ADABAS/Natural, JCL
3 Architecture & Mainframe Environment What enterprise scheduler is used for batch orchestration, and how are job dependencies and execution 
comparisons currently managed? Control M and Control O
4 Architecture & Mainframe Environment Is the governent expecting to convert and migrate mainframe applications to cloud platforms? Possibly, although we have not arri ved there yet. It has been in discussions. 
5 Payroll Operational Constraints What is the payroll processing frequency and peak processing window?
Bi-weekly processing. Peak processing is within 4th quarter of calendar year and most likely due
to year-end processing. 
6 Payroll Operational Constraints May AI-enabled capabilities participate in controlled execution workflows under defined safeguards?
In some areas, possibly. We would want to ensure security and privacy standards are honored 
and held intact. 
7 Data & Database Management What database platforms support payroll and personnel systems, and how is schema or configuration change 
management handled across environments? Database platforms which support our payroll/personnel systems include 
8 Data & Database Management What are the approximate database sizes and daily transaction volumes supporting payroll and personnel 
operations? Database size approx. 35 TB, daily transactions approx.  1.2 Million/ 4.8 million per week
9 Data & Database Management Can you clarify the intended meaning of 'ability to scan various LLMs (large language models) in its entirety' 
under the Analyze Code and Infrastructures objective?
Because our codebase is fairly extensive and nearly 30 years old, we would like for the tool to 
assist with identifyng specific areas in the code. Example, timeframes when code was modified 
or seek bugs within the code. Is there potential to translate the code to a different langauge 
base? 
10 AI Model Usage & Data Sensitivity Must AI capabilities operate exclusively within DOI-controlled infrastructure?
For our payroll/personnel system, yes that is the preference. Pertaining to sub and supporting 
systems which do not contain PII data, we may have flexibility. 
11 AI Model Usage & Data Sensitivity Are probabilistic AI outputs expected to be limited to analytical and advisory use cases, or may they support 
operational workflows under defined governance and audit controls? The output will likely support defined operational work flows .
12 AI Model Usage & Data Sensitivity Will the AI solution need to process, access, or be trained on data containing PII? If so, are there data 
residency or data handling requirements beyond FedRAMP alignment?
Yes, data may contain PII. This may also involve standards outlined by FISMA, NIST 800-53 
(for authentication), Privacy Act of 1974, Zero Trust Alignment 
13 Integration Architecture Are integrations with external partners (e.g., OPM, IRS, SSPs) primarily file-based, API-based, message-
queue based, or hybrid? External integrations are primarily file based. 
14 Integration Architecture Are integrations strictly batch-driven, or are near real-time capabilities required in certain domains? Primarily Batch-driven v ia transmission files.
15 Security & Compliance
Does IBC-HRD require an existing FedRAMP Authorization at a specific baseline, or will a Government-
owned, Contractor-operated deployment aligned to the applicable NIST 800-53 baseline within DOI’s 
security boundary be acceptable? If the application will be cloud based, FedRAMP will be required.  
16 Security & Compliance What logging, auditability, and retention requirements apply to AI-generated outputs and automation 
workflows? Audit requirements would be focused on our primary systems and not on AI output.  
17 Scalability & Resilience What peak concurrency and throughput expectations should proposed AI-enabled solutions be architected to 
support? Unknown at this time
18 Scalability & Resilience What Recovery Time Objective (RTO) and Recovery Point Objective (RPO) thresholds must be met for AI-
enabled systems operating in payroll-supporting environments? 24 hour RTO/RPO
19 Scalability & Resilience
Of the 300,000+ employees serviced by HRD systems, how many concurrent users are anticipated for the AI-
enabled tools and applications? Are usage patterns expected to be steady-state or concentrated around 
payroll cycles and peak processing windows? HRD PERS #
20 Governance & Oversight Is there an established AI governance framework that proposed tools must align with? YES - DOI (OCIO) POLICY AND MEMORANDUMS.  U SG (??)
21 Governance & Oversight Which mission-critical workflows require mandatory human review or approval before production execution?
Medium and High Risk enhancments/features/capabilities require System Owner approvals 
before migration/deployment into production environments.
22 Testing & Modular Architecture
Does IBC-HRD require the proposed AI tool to natively provide automated testing capabilities, or will 
integrated testing automation delivered through complementary enterprise platforms be considered 
acceptable? Hybrid - depending on Testing being completed (Role based)
23 Strategic Alignment & Implementation Does IBC-HRD anticipate prioritizing specific capability domains for initial implementation phases? To be determined
24 Strategic Alignment & Implementation Which of the listed objectives represent the greatest operational risk or resource burden within the current 
HRD environment? refer back to RFI objetctives  (mainframe)
25 Strategic Alignment & Implementation Is the primary intent to enhance the existing mainframe-centric architecture, or to support longer-term 
modernization initiatives?
Initial intent would be support for existing systems/architecture. Long term support will likely 
be for modernization initiatives
26 Strategic Alignment & Implementation What measurable performance improvements will define success for AI-enabled automation? Efficiencies in processes for reduces la bor force. 
27 Strategic Alignment & Implementation Will AI-enabled customer service capabilities support internal HRD staff only, or external federal employees 
serviced by HRD?
Currently it will support HRD staff only but could support cutstomer service processes for IBC 
customers as well. 
28 Submission Clarification Given the 7-page maximum for Section 2 and the breadth of objectives listed, should vendors address all 
objectives at a summary level or prioritize a subset in greater depth? Vendor's discretion
29
Scope of Solution Integration
Does HRD anticipate a single integrated AI platform addressing all listed domains (testing automation, 
batch processing, database management, code analysis, documentation, customer service automation, etc.), 
or are modular, multi‑vendor solutions acceptable if they meet interoperability and security requirements? No, open to options.  All options must meet FEDRAMP specifications/certifications.

30 Clarification on “LLM Scanning”
The RFI references the ability to “scan various LLMs in its entirety.” Can HRD clarify whether this refers to 
scanning large language models, legacy language modules, or both? Both
31
Non‑Multi‑Tenant Requirement
For the requirement that the AI tool “will not be a multi tenant environment for users outside of IBC HRD,” 
is a logically isolated single tenant environment within a FedRAMP authorized boundary acceptable, or is 
physical isolation required? FedRamp authorized boundary
32 FedRAMP Alignment vs. Authorization
The RFI states that solutions must “align with FedRAMP controls.” Is FedRAMP authorization required at 
this stage, or is alignment with the controls sufficient for RFI purposes? Controls must be going through FedRamp authorization process; or already approved.
33 Availability of Sample Artifacts
Will HRD provide sample JCL, anonymized payroll scenarios, or representative data structures to help 
vendors accurately scope automation capabilities? Samples maybe provided during Technical Evaluation period.
34 Integration Expectations with Federal Partne
r
For integrations with OPM, IRS, SSPs, AQD, FMD, and OMB, should vendors assume real‑time 
interoperability, batch‑based exchanges, or both? Both
35 ROM Pricing Granularity
Does HRD prefer ROM pricing broken out by major capability area (e.g., testing automation, database 
automation, payroll automation), or at the overall solution level? Yes- broken out by major capability area.
36 Vendor One‑on‑One Sessions
If HRD conducts one‑on‑one sessions, will vendors be notified in advance of the specific capabilities or 
focus areas HRD wishes to review?
Some focal points will be provided but a high level overview of application capabilities will be 
appreciated. 
37 Is there a timeline and/or milestone for mainframe modernization (components) to cloud infrastructure? To be determined
38 Is a hybrid cloud deployment model part of the mainframe modernization strategy?" To be determined
39 What is the current operating system and version for the mainframe? ZOS 2.5 (3.2 scheduled for migration this FY)
40 If legacy, are you willing to upgrade to the latest mainframe operating system? Yes
41 What is the current mainframe database and version? ADABAS 8.6
42 What are the data native formats, record formats, and compatibility requirements for running data 
preprocessing for machine learning on the mainframe? EBCDIC
43 If data moves off mainframe, are there requirements in how data quality, privacy, and security shall be 
managed? Yes, must meet NIST requirements
44 What are the different mainframe tools/software components being used for the DB query, transaction 
processing, configuration management, workload, file, security access, etc. No specific tools
45 Are there interests to expand to Quantum? Would be interested to learn more about
46 What does success look like? What are the specific business objectives and desired results? See RFI 
47 Is real-time data actions needed or will batch processing suffice majority of the requirements? Both
48
Are you prioritizing on-platform inference (mainframe-centric) running machine learning directly on the 
mainframe for the Online/Production jobs or can offloading to a cloud/hybrid environment (using 
AWS/Azure/GCP) be consider? Open to options or consideration
49
For the Customer Service Ticket Generation objectives (customer service workflow), do you consider this 
sub system complement as ancillary in support of the mainframe. Example, support system (e.g. like 
ServiceNow)? Verse this is on-platform sub system mainframe feature running inside the mainframe. Ancillary support system (ie. ServiceNow)
50 For the Self Help Feature objectives in support of customer service do you consider these ancillary in suppor
t
or also seeking support that require features to be on-platform running inside the mainframe. Ancillary
51 Please provide number of end users and administrators in related to training. Range up to 175 personnel.  Depends upon Application / Mainframe
52 Is there a page limit, font requirement for the Rough Order of Magnitude in Section 3? 2 pages limit
53
“IBC’s Human Resources Directorate seeks 
information from industry on AI tools and 
applications capable of securely supporting
…
across the full systems software development 
lifecycle…”
To take full advantage of best‑in‑class AI capabilities, does IBC‑HRD consider a combination of 
complementary AI platforms and tools—working together within a cohesive, secure architecture—to be 
responsive, rather than requiring a single, monolithic AI platform? Combination.
54 “Mainframe‑centric, payroll‑focused, 
PII‑regulated environment…” Should vendors assume direct integration with the existing mainframe payroll environment? Yes
55
“Additional areas of interest include 
AI‑enabled code and infrastructure analysis 
and refactoring (mainframe module 
assessment, call‑routine mapping, bug 
identification, historical change analysis, and 
LLM‑based scanning)…”
Does DOI IBC envision the future of FPPS as a modernization of the existing mainframe platform (e.g., 
incremental enhancement and refactoring), a partial refactor with selective component replacement, or a full 
system replacement? Please describe the desired end‑state and planning horizon, if defined. To be determined
56
Analyze code and infrastructures – Ability to 
scan various LLMs (large language models) 
in its entirety
Can HRD clarify the intent of "ability to scan various LLMs in its entirety"? Is this referring to evaluating 
LLM outputs for accuracy, bias, and security, or describing the use of LLMs to scan HRD’s codebase? Intent is for both

57 “IBC Policies and Security – FedRAMP-
aligned controls”
To take full advantage of emerging commercial products and technologies, does IBC‑HRD consider phased 
FedRAMP authorization approaches—such as government sponsorship, interim deployment models, or 
limited‑scope authorizations—acceptable for solutions that are not yet fully FedRAMP authorized? Yes
58 Please limit your response to each business 
requirement to 2 pages... (7-page maximum)
To accommodate vendors with multiple relevant solutions, would the government revise this requirement to 
a 10-page overall limit for section 2, with no limit per individual requirement? No - prioritize recommended solution
59 “IBC‑HRD welcomes a wide range of 
submission formats…”
Are architecture diagrams, workflow visuals, screenshots, or process flows counted toward the seven‑page 
limit, or may they be included as embedded graphics or appendices? May be included as embedded graphics or appendicies
60 Creative Pricing Expectations Is there a ceiling or budget range HRD can share for planning purposes? To be determined
61 Are there specific performance metrics or KPIs that HRD will use to evaluate the effectiveness of AI tools?
Policy and expectations are being provided by DOI/OCIO and internally to IBC
62 What are the performance and scalability requirements for AI tools (e.g. concurrent batch jobs,  throughput)?
Unknown at this time
63
Could you provide more detail on the types of external systems and integration points that AI solutions must 
support and any associated security or compliance constraints? Ticketing, Time & Attendance, workforce tracking, learning management, data warehouse, 
change management systems must all maintain standards outlined by FISMA, NIST 800-53 (for 
authentication), Privacy Act of 1974, Zero Trust Alignment
