NONDISCLOSURE AGREEMENT AND AGREEMENT FOR RECEPTION AND CONTROL OF CONTROLLED UNCLASSIFIED INFORMATION

FOR INTERESTED CONTRACTORS FOR UNITED STATES TRANSPORTATION COMMAND (USTRANSCOM) CONTRACTS

NOTE: This Agreement is a standard agreement designed for use by contractor (including sub-contractor) employees preparing proposals for USTRANSCOM contracts.  Its use is designed to protect non-public Government information from disclosure, identify potential conflicts of interest, and prevent violations of federal statutes/regulations.  The restrictions contained in this agreement also serve contractors by promoting compliant behavior that keeps contractors eligible to compete for Government contracts.  In addition to the potential impact on future business opportunities, failure to abide by this agreement could result in administrative, civil, or criminal penalties specified by statute or regulation.

I, 	, currently an employee of 	, hereby agree to the terms and conditions set forth below. I further agree that any company or entity by whom I am employed, represent, or act of its behalf, is also bound by this agreement.

2.  I understand that I will have access to controlled unclassified information provided as a result of my request for the Technical Data Package for the HTC71125QD039 Transportation Command "Medical" Regulating and Command & Control Evacuation System (TRAC2ES) Request for Quote. I fully understand that such information is sensitive and must be protected in accordance with 41 U.S.C. §2101-§2107 and FAR 3.1.  

3. As used in this agreement and as defined in 32 CFR Part 2002, Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) is information the Government creates or possesses, or that an entity creates or possesses for or on behalf of the Government, that a law, regulation, or Government-wide policy (LRGWP) requires or permits an agency to handle using safeguarding or dissemination controls. However, CUI does not include classified information [Information that Executive Order 13526, Classified National Security Information, December 29, 2009 (3 CFR, 2010 Comp., p. 298), any predecessor or successor order, or the Atomic Energy Act of 1954, as amended, requires agencies to mark with classified markings and protect against unauthorized disclosure] or information a non-executive branch entity possesses and maintains in its own systems that did not come from, or was not created or possessed by or for, an executive branch agency or an entity acting for an agency. 

The government may require or permit safeguarding or dissemination controls in three ways: (1) Requiring or permitting agencies to control or protect the information but providing no specific controls, which makes the information CUI Basic; (2) Requiring or permitting agencies to control or protect the information and providing specific controls for doing so, which makes the information CUI Specified; or (3) Requiring or permitting agencies to control the information and specifying only some of those controls, which makes the information CUI Specified, but with CUI Basic controls where the authority does not specify. 

3.	I agree to the following:

Non-disclosure: I agree to not disclose any CUI (in whole or in part, alone or in combination with other information, directly, indirectly, or derivatively)  to which I am granted access to anyone who, or anything that, is not granted such access pursuant to an applicable lawful Government purpose. I understand my responsibilities to protect and not disclose CUI except for, and to people with, an appropriate lawful Government purpose and also possess a fully executed NDA. To ascertain whether someone or something is authorized access, pursuant to an applicable lawful Government purpose, to CUI that is under my control or in my possession, I will do so according to 32 CFR Part 2002, Department of Defense guidance, USTRANSCOM Guidance and applicable procedures. 

I agree not to disclose the CUI by any means to any person except to an individual (non-Government person including, but not limited to, a person in my company, affiliated companies, sub-contractors, etc.) who has a need to know related to the immediate proposal, has executed a valid form of this non-disclosure agreement, and I understand that misuse of non-public information is subject to penalties established in applicable laws, regulations, or Government-wide policies.  All distribution of the documents will be controlled with the concurrence of the Contracting Officer.  

Scope of CUI: I understand that the CUI to which I am granted access includes any CUI I create or use on behalf of or for the Government proposal, CUI I receive from the Government to maintain, handle, transmit, or use (for research, in derivative documents, etc.), or CUI originating from another source (such as CUI generated for the Government by a contractor or research entity).

I agree to not use such information for any non-Governmental purposes or government purpose not being TRAC2ES including, but not limited to, the preparation of bids or proposals for work not in support of this specified requirement, or the development or execution of other business or commercial ventures.

Security/control: I am obligated to protect CUI to which I am granted access from unauthorized disclosure, in accordance with the terms of this agreement, 32 CFR Part 2002, Department of Defense guidance, USTRANSCOM Guidance and the applicable procedures to the specific categories of CUI to which I am granted access.

I agree to store the information in such a manner as to prevent inadvertent disclosure or releases to individuals who have not been authorized access to it.

I agree to delete and destroy the information upon notification from the contracting officer and am obligated to provide a response upon completion. 

Reporting breaches: I agree that I shall promptly report to appropriate officials, in accordance with the guidance issued for the applicable category of CUI, any unauthorized disclosure that I have knowledge of whether or not I am personally involved. I also understand that my anonymity will be preserved to the extent possible when reporting such violations.

Time: I understand that all conditions and obligations imposed upon me by this agreement apply during the time I am granted access to CUI, and at all times thereafter while such information remains controlled. These conditions and obligations may no longer apply if I am explicitly released in writing by an authorized representative of USTRANSCOM or the United States Government. 

Breach: If I violate the terms and conditions of this agreement, such violation may result in cancellation of my access to CUI, and may result in civil and criminal sanctions. This may also serve as a basis for denying me access to other types of information under USTRANSCOM’s control or result in the disqualification from submitting an TRAC2ES  offer.

Assignee: I assign to the United States Government all royalties, remunerations, and emoluments that have resulted, will result, or may result from any disclosure, publication, or revelation of CUI to which I am granted access that is inconsistent with the terms of this agreement.

Remedies: This agreement is made and intended for the benefit of the United States Government and may be enforced by the United States Government or the authorized non-Federal entity. By granting me access to CUI in this context, the United States Government may seek any remedy available to it to enforce this agreement, including, but not limited to, application for a court order prohibiting disclosure of CUI in breach of this agreement. I understand that if I violate the terms and conditions of this agreement, I could be subjected to civil, or criminal action, as appropriate, under the procedures applicable to the category of CUI involved, and neither the United States Government nor the non-Federal authorized entity have waived any statutory or common law evidentiary privileges or protections that they may assert in any administrative or court proceeding to protect any CUI to which I have been given access under the terms of this agreement.

Severability: Each provision of this agreement is severable. If a court should find any provision of this agreement to be unenforceable, all other provisions shall remain in full force and effect. 

Other agreements: My execution of this agreement shall not nullify or affect in any manner any other secrecy or non-disclosure agreement(s) that I have executed or may execute with USTRANSCOM, the United States Government, or any of its other departments or agencies.

4.  I understand that this agreement is personal to me and shall survive and remain in full force and effect notwithstanding any change in current employment. I read this agreement carefully and my questions, if any, were answered. I have had an opportunity and/or the benefit of private counsel and hereby knowingly and informatively accept the terms of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.

By signing below, I certify that I have read and understand the terms of this Non-Disclosure Agreement and voluntarily agree to be bound by its terms.

SBU - CONTRACTING AND ACQUISITIONS 
U.S. DEPARTMENT OF STATE 
Enterprise Physical Security Information Management (PSIM) Platform 
Global Integration, Engineering, and Operations Support 
REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI) / SOURCES SOUGHT (SS) 
19AQMM26N0033 
 
 
1. Purpose 
The Government is seeking industry capability information from firms with demonstrated 
experience integrating and sustaining enterprise Physical Security Information 
Management (PSIM) systems within secure government or similarly controlled 
environments. The requirement involves engineering, integration, sustainment, and operational 
support of a globally deployed PSIM platform integrating multiple physical security subsystems 
including enterprise video management systems, access control systems, intrusion detection 
systems, GIS-based facility mapping, 3-D modeling environments, and advanced video analytics 
capabilities including facial recognition and license plate recognition technologies. 
The system operates within segmented network environments supporting mission-critical 
operations, requiring high-availability architecture, controlled software release management, 
and integration of multiple security technologies into a unified operational platform. The 
Government is particularly interested in vendors with demonstrated experience supporting 
large-scale integrated security systems operating within secure or regulated environments 
and capable of providing continuous operational support for mission-critical platforms. 
This notice is for market research purposes only and does not constitute a solicitation. 
2. Unique System Architecture 
The environment supported under this requirement is not a conventional enterprise IT system. 
The PSIM platform operates within a distributed, multi-tier security architecture integrating 
multiple security technologies and data environments into a unified operational platform. 
The architecture integrates: 
• Enterprise Video Management Systems (VMS) 
• Access Control Systems (ACS) 
• Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS) 
• Geographic Information Systems (GIS) 
• 3-D facility modeling and visualization platforms 

 
SBU - CONTRACTING AND ACQUISITIONS 
• Enterprise relational databases and data warehousing environments 
• Event correlation and alarm management engines 
• Secure API integration with external security platforms 
The environment supports global deployments across secure facilities and operates within 
network environments that include: 
• Segmented network enclaves 
• Virtualized server infrastructure 
• High-availability production environments 
• Configuration-controlled software release pipelines 
• Secure enclave cybersecurity requirements 
• Strict configuration baselines and system authorization controls 
The system must maintain the following operational standards: 
• Greater than 99.9% uptime for production server environments 
• Greater than 99% uptime for client environments 
• Real-time alarm ingestion and correlation across integrated security systems 
• Continuous centralized system health monitoring and reporting 
The architecture requires experience integrating physical security subsystems with enterprise 
software platforms operating in controlled security environments. 
3. Advanced Video Analytics Integration 
The PSIM environment incorporates advanced video analytics capabilities used to enhance 
situational awareness and automated security detection functions. 
The architecture integrates both camera-edge analytics and centralized server-based video 
analytic processing platforms supporting technologies including: 
• Facial Recognition (FR) 
• License Plate Recognition (LPR) 
• Object detection and classification 
• Motion-based event analytics 
• Behavioral detection analytics 
The system must support ingestion and correlation of analytic outputs generated from: 
• Edge analytics embedded within IP cameras 
• Server-based analytic engines 
• Video management system analytic modules 
• Third-party analytic platforms 

 
SBU - CONTRACTING AND ACQUISITIONS 
Analytic events are correlated with other security system events and presented within the PSIM 
environment to support operational decision making. 
The architecture must support high-volume analytic event processing while maintaining system 
performance and operational availability. 
4. Required Industry Experience 
The Government seeks vendors with direct and verifiable experience supporting complex 
security system environments with the following technical capabilities. 
Enterprise PSIM Integration 
• Configuration and customization of enterprise PSIM platforms 
• Integration of multiple vendor security systems into unified command environments 
• Alarm/event normalization and correlation 
• Integration of VMS, ACS, IDS, and perimeter detection systems 
• Development and maintenance of PSIM interfaces and integration services 
Mission-Critical System Engineering 
• Secure enterprise application architecture 
• High-availability system engineering 
• Enterprise database architecture and administration 
• DevSecOps practices in controlled environments 
• Enterprise configuration, release, and patch management 
Physical Security System Integration 
• Integration of physical security subsystems with enterprise software platforms 
• Video management system integration 
• Security command center technology and monitoring platforms 
• Alarm monitoring and operational dashboards 
Advanced Video Analytics Integration 
• Camera-edge analytics platforms 
• Server-based video analytic processing engines 
• Facial recognition system integration 
• License plate recognition system integration 
• Analytic event ingestion and alarm correlation 
• Integration of analytic outputs into PSIM operational environments 
• Optimization of analytic processing pipelines for real-time security operations 
 
 

 
SBU - CONTRACTING AND ACQUISITIONS 
 
GIS and Spatial Data Integration 
• GIS-based facility mapping and spatial data management 
• Spatial database architecture 
• Integration of GIS data into operational security systems 
• 3-D facility modeling and visualization platforms 
Cybersecurity and Compliance 
• Support of systems operating within FISMA-aligned environments 
• Information Assurance documentation and compliance support 
• System configuration baselining and security hardening 
• Security authorization and accreditation support 
• Coordination with Information System Security Officers (ISSO) 
Tiered Operational Support Model 
The PSIM system operates under a structured support model including: 
Tier 1 operational support 
Tier 2 technical troubleshooting and escalation 
Tier 3 engineering support and system development 
The environment includes 24/7 Integrated Operations Center (IOC) support, requiring: 
• Continuous system monitoring 
• Rapid response to operational incidents 
• Tiered escalation of system issues 
• Structured incident management and tracking processes 
5. Specialized Government Processes 
The requirement includes operational governance processes that may differ from commercial IT 
environments. 
These processes include: 
• Government-controlled Configuration Control Boards (CCB) 
• Formal change approval and release governance processes 
• Operational Readiness Reviews (ORR) 
• Government Acceptance Reviews prior to global deployment 
• Alpha and Beta testing cycles conducted within designated government facilities 
• Configuration-controlled release pipelines 
• Quality Assurance and Compliance Reporting requirements 
• Formal Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) development and maintenance 

 
SBU - CONTRACTING AND ACQUISITIONS 
V endors unfamiliar with these processes may not be suitable for this requirement. 
 
6. Capability Indicators 
The Government is specifically interested in vendors demonstrating experience supporting 
environments involving: 
PSIM integration 
Enterprise Video Management Systems 
Physical security system integration 
Advanced video analytics integration 
Facial recognition technologies 
License plate recognition systems 
Edge analytics platforms 
Server-based analytic processing engines 
GIS-enabled security systems 
3-D facility modeling environments 
DevSecOps pipelines in controlled environments 
Configuration control governance 
FISMA-aligned operational environments 
Enterprise alarm monitoring platforms 
Mission-critical uptime requirements 
7. Anticipated Contract Characteristics 
The anticipated contract may include: 
• Base period with option years 
• Labor-based service Contract Line Item Numbers (CLINs) 
• Engineering and integration services 
• Global sustainment and support services 
• Software integration milestones 
The final contract structure will be informed by responses received through this market research 
effort. 
8. Response Requirements 
Interested vendors should provide: 
1. Company information and business size classification 
2. Relevant past performance within the past five years 

 
SBU - CONTRACTING AND ACQUISITIONS 
3. Description of experience supporting enterprise PSIM or comparable integrated security 
platforms 
4. Demonstration of experience supporting mission-critical security systems 
5. Description of capability to support 24/7 operational environments 
Responses should focus on direct technical capability and experience supporting complex 
integrated security systems. 
7. Submission Instructions 
All written responses should be submitted in Microsoft-compatible formats (e.g., Word) or PDF.  
Responses must use 12-point font and 1-inch margins, including all text, tables, and graphics.  
Page size shall be 8 ½ X 11-inch.  Page limit is twenty-five (20) single sided.   
Additional attachments such as data sheets providing additional technical details for proposed 
solutions may also be provided, if necessary, in addition to the 20-page limit. General brochures 
or other marketing materials not directly relevant to the Department requirements are not 
requested and will not be considered as part of the response. 
Please submit all responses to Jennifer C. Willis at WillisJC2@state.gov May 7
th, 2026, by 3:00 
PM ET.

1 
Distribution Statement A. Approved for public release: distribution is unlimited. 
 
REQUEST FOR INFORMATION 
OPERATIONAL TEST AND TRAINING INFRASTRUCTURE (OTTI) 
SPACE DATA MANAGEMENT DIVISION 
1.0 Description 
 
1.1 The United States Space Force (USSF) is seeking information on how an interested 
contractor could provide the user interface and automation layer of a military Human Resources 
Information Technology (HRIT) system that is easy to use for both Guardians and Human 
Resource (HR) Practitioners. In  support of this effort, USSF is interested in gathering industry 
feedback on a phased approach to incrementally deliver military HR and Talent Management 
capabilities in accordance with the Personnel Management Act. 
 
1.2 THIS IS A REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI) ONLY. This Request for Information 
(RFI) is issued solely for information and planning purposes (market research), in accordance with 
Revolutionary Federal Acquisition Regulation Overhaul (RFO) 15.101(c) . This RFI does not 
constitute a solicitation (Request for Proposal (RFP) or Request for Quotation (RFQ)) or a 
promise to issue a solicitation in the future. This RFI does not commit the Government to 
contract for any supply or service whatsoever. Responses to this RFI are not offers and cannot be 
accepted by the Government to form a binding contract. Furthermore, the Government is not, at 
this time, seeking proposals and will not accept any unsolicited proposals. Respondents are 
advised that the Government will not pay for any information or administrative costs incurred in 
response to this RFI. All costs associated with responding to this RFI will be solely at the 
responding party’s expense. Not responding to this RFI does not preclude participation in any 
future solicitation, if any is issued. Any information submitted by respondents to this RFI is 
strictly voluntary. All submissions become Government property and will not be returned. 
Proprietary information will be safeguarded in accordance with the applicable Government 
regulations. Proprietary information or trade secrets shall be clearly identified. 
 
2.0 Background 
 
The USSF is at a pivotal moment in its evolution as it works to implement the Space Force 
Personnel Management Act (PMA) by the end of Fiscal Year (FY) 2028, as defined in the FY24 
National Defense Authorization Act. This legislation introduces a groundbreaking approach to 
military personnel management, creating a service model without traditional components and 
offering both full -time and part -time service options instead of a reserve or guard component. 
This shift is designed to enhance flexibility, efficiency, and readiness, ensuring the Space Force 
can attract, recruit, and retain the top talent necessary to maintain superiority in the space domain. 
However, the current military HR systems in use by the USSF are highly fragmented, with over 
300 disparate systems in operation. The goal for this effort is to incrementally deploy capabilities 
that bring together Sustained (full -time) and Non- Sustained (part -time) Guardians  

2 
Distribution Statement A. Approved for public release: distribution is unlimited. 
 
into a single cohesive application, eliminating the need to transition between systems, an action 
that often creates gaps in pay and benefits throughout a member’s career. 
 
The Space Force’s mission to bring together Sustained and Non-sustained Guardians into a single 
system is driven by the need to align its workforce management practices with its unique 
operational demands. As the newest branch of the DOW, the USSF faces distinct challenges in 
managing a growing and diverse force while maintaining compliance with evolving policies and 
regulations. Current systems have created inefficiencies, such as repetitive manual tasks and 
fragmented data management, which negatively impact morale, retention, and operational 
effectiveness. To address these challenges, the USSF is committed to deploying capabilities that 
integrate modern technology, simplify processes, streamline workflows, and enhance the overall 
user experience for both Guardians and HR practitioners. 
 
This modernization effort is critical to ensuring the Space Force can meet Congressional timelines 
outlined in the Personnel Management Act (PMA), improve operational effectiveness, and support 
its mission to prevail in the space domain. By presenting USSF HR and Talent Management 
functions in a unified system, the USSF aims to eliminate inefficiencies, improve data 
transparency, and create a seamless experience for its workforce. This initiative represents a 
significant step forward in aligning the Space Force capabilities with its strategic goals and setting 
a new standard for personnel management within the DOW. 
 
The USSF seeks an incremental, phased approach to deploy military HR capabilities to align with 
PMA and the larger “Federal HR 2.0” modernization effort. This approach seeks to deploy small 
capabilities quickly that provide demonstrated value to the Space Force. 
3.0 Requirements 
The USSF needs the capability to identify, track, and manage Guardians in a single system without 
component. The system must be modern, secure, and adaptable to evolving business processes and 
the unique workforce management needs of a growing force. As the USSF moves forward as a 
service without component, the system must be capable of providing Guardians with flexible 
workflows and processes to manage Active Guardians in both Sustained (full -time) and Non-
Sustained (part-time) duty status which sets the USSF apart from other DOW services branches. 
One potential solution the Space Force is considering for improving the user experience for 
Guardians is the deployment of a COTS/SaaS Guardian Automation Layer (GAL) and Guardian 
Data Layer (GDL). By decoupling front end software experience (GAL) from the back-end system 
interfaces (GDL), the future military HRIT system will be  able to remain flexible and scalable as 
the USSF grows. These capabilities will enable the USSF to respond rapidly to emerging 
requirements, integrate with existing and future platforms, maintain vendor competition, and 
remain sustainable through changes in policy, organization, and scale. 
3.1 Guardian Automation Layer (GAL): The GAL will serve as the Single User Interface for 
Guardians and HR Practitioners to interact with military HR Data. Historically, Guardian data has 
been stored within disparate systems, making process improvements and automation cumbersome. 
With no single source of truth for Guardian records or port-of-call for completing military HR 

3 
Distribution Statement A. Approved for public release: distribution is unlimited. 
 
actions, practitioners and end users rely on manual workarounds to reach data needed for decision 
making. 
The primary purpose of the Space Force GAL is to track and manage Guardians in a service 
without component structure throughout the military HR lifecycle. The desired solution will allow 
the Guardian a continuous experience within the GAL throughout the entirety of the HR lifecycle, 
from onboarding and career development to retirement and beyond. The GAL will be flexible, 
with a positive user experience based in human- centered design practices that are reflective of 
modern commercial capabilities. 
The USSF is pursuing a solution that leverages role-based authorities within a unified interface to 
analyze data and create interactive dashboards. Low code/no code configurability will allow 
practitioners to adapt to the evolving policy and process requirements, such as PMA. 
Automation and AI capabilities are a highly desired function of the GAL. The USSF aims to 
greatly reduce administrative burden by freeing Guardians from routine and repetitive tasks and 
will allow for increased focus on higher level work to deliver results for the USSF. It will also 
improve data quality, timeliness of service delivery, Guardian self -service, and HR data 
transparency to Guardians. 
The available dashboards should provide Guardians with a view of open HR actions and track the 
stage of these actions in the workflows. This will reduce the administrative burden on Guardians, 
Supervisors, Commanders, and HR practitioners by providing transparency and reducing the need 
for follow-up correspondence. 
In line with current USSF and DOW policies, the Government is seeking to minimize custom 
software development and maximize the use of commercial-off-the-shelf (COTS), software-as-a-
service (SaaS), and configurable solutions for the GAL. 
3.2 Guardian Data Layer (GDL): The GDL will be the primary integration point and data 
repository for Guardian Data between the GAL and applicable Department of the Air Force (DAF), 
DOW, and other US Government systems. The GDL will be comprised of tools designed to collect, 
store, process, disseminate, and analyze military HR data including, but not limited to, database 
services, data warehousing, big data, data integration tools, data visualization, business 
intelligence, data lake, extract, transformation, application programming interfaces (APIs), and 
data governance. The Guardian Data Layer will also be capable of hosting other commercial 
applications that may need access to Guardian Data. Any solution chosen for the GAL will need 
to integrate with the GDL. 

4 
Distribution Statement A. Approved for public release: distribution is unlimited. 
 
4.0 Requested Information 
 
Before answering the questions below, please review the Attachment to this RFI, “System 
Requirements Document (SRD) - Space Force Guardian Personnel Management System.” 
 
4.1 : Compliance, Security & Data Sovereignty 
 
4.1.1 Does your product have a current Authority to Operate (ATO)? Specify the Impact 
Level (IL) and whether you have FedRAMP or DISA certification. If not, what is your 
roadmap to achieve certification? 
4.1.2 Is your organization owned or operated, in whole or in part, by a foreign entity? 
Describe the relationship and confirm if your platform ensures full data segregation 
between U.S. and foreign operations. 
4.1.3 Can your solution deploy on a Government Cloud (GovCloud) or provide its own 
equivalent? What is your platform's data storage architecture, preferred deployment 
model, and why? 
4.1.4 How does your product manage secure, role -based access for external users (e.g., 
civilian employees, support contractors) who manage military personnel but may not be 
managed in the HR system? Address licensing implications for these users. 
 
4.2 : Core Platform Capabilities & SRD Compliance 
 
4.2.1 Review the attached System Requirements Document (SRD). For each requirement, 
complete the SRD Spreadsheet by stating if the requirement can be met "Out of the 
Box," "With Configuration," or "With Customization." Provide SKU numbers for all 
"Out of the Box" or "With Configuration" solutions. 
Out of the Box: Capability exists in the system without any changes required to 
implement 
Configuration: Changes are required in the system but the ability to make the changes is 
a part of the system design and capabilities. 
Customization: Changes are required in the system and require coding or development 
against the product line. 
 

5 
Distribution Statement A. Approved for public release: distribution is unlimited. 
 
 
4.2.2 How does your platform manage military -specific constructs, including: rank as a 
dynamic attribute, multiple service dates, and unique personnel cases (e.g., Exceptional 
Family Member Program, Joint Spouse)? 
4.2.3 Does your product support a mixed workforce (full-time military, part-time military, 
civilian) within a single environment, accommodating unique data fields, rules, and 
processes for each population? Are the data fields fixed and require development 
against a product line or are they customizable? 
4.2.4 Describe your product's ability to integrate personnel and financial data to support 
complex "change of station" events. Highlight any native data visualization capabilities 
(e.g., map-based tracking). 
4.2.5 Describe how your system supports multi -step onboarding workflows, from initial 
recruitment and pre-assignment (training) to the first duty assignment, ensuring data 
accuracy and dashboard visibility. 
4.2.6 Does your product have native capabilities for generating standardized forms with 
electronic signatures? Does your product store documents attached to a personnel 
record? Detail any associated costs and recommended approaches for mobile or 
workflow optimization. 
4.2.7 Does your product have native capabilities to perform data analytics and data 
visualization? 
 
 
4.3 : Implementation, Sustainment & Scalability 
 
4.3.1 Provide a sample implementation plan, including recommended sequencing, testing 
approach, and deployment schedule against the capabilities identified in the draft 
capabilities roadmap. Include a reasonable timeline for a vendor to ramp up to full 
operational capacity. 
4.3.2 Based on the information provided, what is a reasonable cost estimate for 
implementation? What key factors or risks could affect the timeline and cost? 

6 
Distribution Statement A. Approved for public release: distribution is unlimited. 
 
 
4.3.3 Describe your sustainment strategy, particularly how unique configurations are 
preserved during update cycles. 
4.3.4 To support a force of 10,000 Guardians by 2028 and 40,000 by 2040, what is the 
recommended size for the technical support/sustainment team and the corporate HR 
team? 
4.3.5 What training do you recommend for end-users, and what is the typical time required for 
a user to become proficient? 
 
4.4 : Past Performance & Market Position 
 
4.4.1 Describe your past experience providing Enterprise HR platforms to military and/or 
U.S. Government organizations, highlighting your approach to navigating complex 
policy and compliance environments. 
4.4.2 Who do you consider your primary competitors, and what are your key differentiators?

contract Mission Support Expenditures such as to support deployments, contingency operations, hostilities, 
war, national emergencies, critical mission systems and other unusual, critical operations and support

1. Will responses to Q&A be consolidated into a single amendment, and when is it expected 
to be released? 
a) Yes, all questions are contained within this response. 
2. Please clarify the expected on-site versus telework posture by labor category, including 
whether any positions are required to be full-time onsite and whether personnel must be 
physically present to initiate CAC/MCEN processing. 
a) Per PWS Sect 1.5.3, place of performance is 814 Radford Blvd, Maintenance 
Center Bldg. 2200, Albany, GA 31704. Per PWS Sect 1.5.3.1, teleworking will be 
considered on a case-by-case basis. 
Personnel do not need to be physically present to initiate CAC/MCEN processing. 
For contractors teleworking, we can ship an MCEN laptop to them and coordinate 
with a local reserve unit a day/time for the contractor to come to their location and 
load their profile. 
3. For Subfactor 3, please confirm the exact expectations for the Implementation Plan, 
including whether the Government expects coverage of all PWS paragraphs 4.1–4.5.7 or 
only the highlighted areas. 
a) Government expects coverage of all PWS paragraphs 4.1 – 4.5.7. Subfactor 3, 
first paragraph states “Quoters shall submit an Implementation Plan for PWS 
paragraphs and subparagraphs for 4.1 – 4.5.7 which shall demonstrate their 
knowledge and understanding of the PWS requirements. The Quoter shall 
demonstrate a thorough understanding of the efforts necessary to successfully 
accomplish the requirements of this order.” 
4. Please provide a baseline inventory for each in-scope MDMC IT system, including 
current hosting, environments, user base, major integrations, and operational status.  In 
addition, is there a preferred cloud required to migrate to? 
a) User base is Marine Depot Maintenance Command.

5. For MRO, which is identified as being removed by 5/31/2026, please confirm whether 
sustainment, transition, decommissioning, or data migration activities are in scope during 
the base period. 
a) Sustainment is in scope until decommissioned. The date has slipped from 
5/31/2026 to 7/31/2026. PWS has been updated to reflect change.   
Transition and data migration are out of scope of this contract. 
6. Please identify what transition artifacts the Government will provide at award, including 
incumbent documentation, SOPs, open backlog/tickets, architecture diagrams, ATO 
materials, asset inventories, and any expected overlap with the outgoing contractor. 
a) At award, vendor will be provided with access to private Microsoft Teams 
channels which the repository for working documents and SOPs. Final versions 
are uploaded to eMASS. 
7. The RFQ states the Government estimates 13 FTEs. Please provide the Government’s 
notional staffing mix by role or function, including whether the alternate PM is expected 
to be separately staffed within that estimate. 
a) The Government estimate of 13 FTEs is provided for planning purposes only and 
should not be interpreted as a mandated staffing structure. Quoters are encouraged 
to align proposed labor categories to the PWS task areas and clearly demonstrate 
how their approach effectively supports all required functions. PM is included in 
the estimated (13) FTEs.  
8. Please confirm the expected timeline for base access, CAC issuance, and MCEN account 
enablement for new personnel. 
a) Base access is approved in 1-2 business days on average. CAC issuance can be on 
the same day or could take a few days depending on contractor’s local Pass & ID 
availability. Some Pass & ID locations accept walk-ins whereas others only accept 
appointments. MCEN-N SAARs have historically been processed in 1 business 
day. Once SAAR is approved, Government will issue GFE the same day. If 
Contractor is teleworking, Government will ship GFE to contractor’s home 
address. 
9. PWS 4.1.5/4.1.7 references maintaining CMMI Level 3 certification. Please confirm 
whether a current formal certification is required at time of quote or award, and what 
supporting evidence the Government expects offerors to provide. 
a) CMMI Level 3 certification is not required at time of quote. Acceptable 
supporting evidence is a <insert vendor name> holds CMMI Level 3 certification 
in both Development and Services (certificate # 68535 / expires:<insert date 
MM/DD/YYYY>). 
 

10. Please clarify which software licenses, cloud services, admin tools, database tools, 
development/test tools, and other platform services will be Government-furnished versus 
contractor-provided. In addition, is there a preferred cloud required to migrate to? 
a) All software licenses, cloud services, admin tools, database tools, 
development/test tools, and other platform services will be Government-
furnished. The preferred cloud to migrate to is Azure GovCloud IL4. 
11. Please clarify the distinction between CLIN 0002 Mission Support and CLIN 0003 
ODC/Travel, including where software, software support, temporary labor, parts, surge-
related purchases, and other reimbursable items should be priced and administered. 
a) CLIN 0003 is for travel only. CLIN 0002 supports all unforeseen required 
software, software support, temporary labor. CLIN 0003 will have a Not-to-
Exceed amount set at $20,000.00 and CLIN 0002 will have a Not-to-Exceed 
amount set at $10,000.00 (see amendment 0001). 
12. PWS 1.3.4 requires three competitive quotes for RGS actions unless otherwise justified. 
Please confirm whether this applies to every RGS action regardless of dollar value, 
including urgent surge, overtime, software, and travel requests, and whether any 
emergency exceptions apply. 
a) Travel: 3 competitive quotes does not apply.

V1.0 4-16 
Synopsis: 
 
NASA/NSSC has a requirement for FY26 Zuken Maintenance Renewal.   
NASA/NSSC intends to issue a sole source contract to ZUKEN USA INC 238 Littleton RD STE 100 Westford, 
Massachusetts 01886 under the authority of FAR 12.102(a). It has been determined that ZUKEN USA INC is 
the sole provider of FY26 Zuken Maintenance Renewal. 
NASA/NSSC will be the procuring center for this effort. Performance will be located at NASA/ Langley 
Research Center (LARC). 
The Government intends to acquire Commercial items or services using the provisions, clauses and procedures 
prescribed in FAR Part 12 for Commercial 
 
The NAICS Code for this procurement is 541512. 
 
Interested organizations may submit their capabilities and qualifications to perform the effort in writing to the 
identified point of contact not later than 7 a.m. Central Standard Time on 4/10/2026. Such 
capabilities/qualifications will be evaluated solely for the purpose of determining whether or not to conduct this 
procurement on a competitive basis. A determination by the Government not to compete this proposed effort on 
a full and open competition basis, based upon responses to this notice, is solely within the discretion of the 
government. 
 
Oral communications are not acceptable in response to this notice. 
 
NASA FAR Supplement Clause 1852.215-84, Ombudsman, is applicable. The Center Ombudsman for this 
acquisition can be found at https://www.hq.nasa.gov/office/procurement/regs/Procurement-Ombuds-Comp-

search_text:
SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE ONLY 
FDA-SS-2026-040726 
 
   
 
1 | Page  
 
Title: ThinkTrends Software Implementation and Support  
Agency: U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) 
Notice Type: Sources Sought / Market Research 
Posting Date: April 7, 2026 
Response Due Date: April 17, 2026 
NAICS Code: 541511 – Custom Computer Programming Services (or appropriate) 
Size Standard: $30M 
1. Purpose 
The FDA is conducting market research to identify capable vendors to support the 
implementation and integration of ThinkTrends software and related human interventions 
across FDA operations. This enhanced effort will leverage Artificial Intelligence (AI), 
automation, and emerging technologies to improve operational efficiency, data management, 
and regulatory processes. 
This Sources Sought notice is for information and planning purposes only and is not a 
solicitation. Responses will be used to determine the availability of qualified vendors and to 
inform future acquisition strategy, including potential sole-source justification or competitive 
procurement. 
2. Background 
The BPA will support FDA’s efforts in: 
• Advanced Data Management: Data migration, integration, cleansing, cataloging, 
annotation, and implementation of data lakes or data mesh architectures.  
• Emerging Technology Integration: RPA, AI-based OCR (>90% accuracy), blockchain, 
edge computing, generative AI, NLP, and LLMs for document summarization.  
• Intelligent Question-Answering: AI-assisted drafting of regulatory responses, 
highlighting issues, and providing contextual/historical data.  
• Advanced Visualization & Analytics: Interactive dashboards, predictive analytics, and 
virtual/augmented reality for data visualization.  
• Automation & Process Enhancements: Workflow automation, AI-driven quality 
control, and regulatory tracking.  
• FDA System Enhancements: Next-generation OCR, FAERS support with AI signal 
detection, and intelligent digitization of document rooms.  

SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE ONLY 
FDA-SS-2026-040726 
 
   
 
2 | Page  
 
• Data-Driven Tools: AI decision support, predictive models, and digital twins to optimize 
regulatory processes.  
• User Experience Improvements: Intuitive AI-assisted interfaces, personalized 
dashboards, and voice-activated assistants.  
• Comprehensive Support: ThinkTrends license acquisition, agile project management, 
and continuous staff training.  
 
3. Capability Requirements 
Interested vendors should demonstrate experience in: 
1. Implementing AI-based software solutions in large-scale enterprise environments.  
2. Data migration, integration, and management using modern architectures (data lakes, data 
mesh).  
3. AI/ML model development, deployment, and explainability.  
4. OCR, NLP, RPA, and emerging technology solutions in highly regulated environments.  
5. Designing user interfaces and dashboards for diverse user roles.  
6. Agile project management and technology adoption support in federal or regulated 
organizations.  
 
4. Response Instructions 
Please submit responses in PDF format via email to Lilibeth Deato at 
lilibeth.deato@fda.hhs.gov by COB, April 17, 2026. Responses should be no longer than 6 
pages and include: 
1. Company name, business size, and point of contact.  
2. Description of relevant experience, including past performance.  
3. Capabilities related to AI, automation, and emerging technologies listed above.  
4. Any certifications, licenses, or unique capabilities relevant to the requirement.  
5. Ability to support a BPA with multiple optional tasks or pilot projects.  
6. If on GSA, identification of the firm's GSA Schedule contract(s) by Schedule number and 
SINs that are applicable to this potential requirement. Detailed list of items included and 
not included on the GSA contract to meet this requirement. 
 
 

SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE ONLY 
FDA-SS-2026-040726 
 
   
 
3 | Page  
 
5. Additional Information 
• This is market research only; no award will result from this notice.  
• Responses will assist the Government in determining:  
o Availability of qualified sources  
o Potential for small business set-asides  
o Whether a sole-source BPA is justified  
• All questions regarding this notice should be submitted in writing to Lilibeth Deato at 
lilibeth.deato@fda.hhs.gov by COB, April 17, 2026. 
 
6.Disclaimer and Important Notes  
 
This notice does not obligate the Government to award a contract or otherwise pay for the 
information provided in the response. The Government reserves the right to use information 
provided by respondents for any purpose deemed necessary and legally appropriate. Any 
organization responding to this notice should ensure that its response is complete and sufficiently 
detailed to allow the Government to determine the organization’s qualifications to perform the 
work. Respondents are advised that the Government is under no obligation to acknowledge 
receipt of the information received or provide feedback to respondents with respect to any 
information submitted. After a review of the responses received, a pre-solicitation synopsis and 
solicitation may be published. However, responses to this notice will not be considered adequate 
responses to a solicitation. 
 
7.Confidentiality  
 
No proprietary, classified, confidential, or sensitive information should be included in your 
response. The Government reserves the right to use any non-proprietary technical information in 
any resultant solicitation(s). 
 
(End of SS)

FAR Part 8 GSA Order LSJ 
Rev 8 dtd 22 Sep 2023 
 
 
         
DEPARTMENT OF THE NAVY
DIRECTOR STRATEGIC SYSTEMS PROGRAMS
1250 10TH STREET SE, SUITE 3600 
WASHINGTON NAVY YARD, DC  20374-5127 
                      IN REPLY REFER TO 
                          SPJA26-51 
JUSTIFICATION FOR OTHER THAN FULL AND OPEN COMPETITION
SOLE SOURCE FAR PART 13.501
 
1. Agency and Contracting Activity 
The agency and contracting activity for this effort is the United States Department of the Navy, 
Strategic Weapons Facility Pacific (SWFPAC) located on Naval Base Kitsap – Bangor, at 6401 
Skipjack Circle, Silverdale, WA 98315.  
2. Description of the Action Being Approved 
The acquisition of the supplies/services particular to one manufacturer or company because the 
supplies/services are unique or highly specialized under a Sole Source Justification. 
3. Description of Supplies/Services and Estimated Value 
SWFPAC currently supports a mission-critical 2003 Microsoft Access database which is in need of 
immediate migration to Nintex Automation (a.k.a. K2). Microsoft products no longer support 
2003 Microsoft Access without a separate extended support subscription for Windows 11. 
SSP/SWFPAC’s extended support subscription ends in July 2026, and no further subscriptions 
are authorized, which is driving the requirement for immediate migration of all SWFPAC 
legacy 2003 Microsoft Access databases to Nintex Automation.   SWFPAC requires 
professional assistance to accelerate the migration in order to meet the deadline while 
ensuring required data integrity, database access, and solution operability. 
 
Due to both the critical nature of the data to be transferred and the compressed timeline in 
which the migration must occur, SWFPAC requires professional assistance for the migration 
of its Microsoft Access legacy database to the Nintex Automation product. The knowledge 
and skill required to perform this crucial data migration under a compressed timeframe far 
exceeds that of SWFPAC’s personnel.  
The total estimated dollar value is    
4. Statutory Authority Permitting Sole Source Justification  
This action is being executed in accordance with FAR 13.106(b)(1)(i) as only one source is 
reasonably available.


Enclosure 
FAR Part 8 GSA Order LSJ 
Rev 8 dtd 22 Sep 2023 
 
 
5. Rationale Supporting Use of Cited Statutory Authority  
 
In August 2025, SWFPAC contract N0040625PS379 was awarded to Flowcision LLC, for 
a similar data migration using the contractor’s proprietary form building tools; Apper and 
FlowBase.  In FY26, Ai Global Solutions acquired exclusive rights to these proprietary 
tools when it hired the developer of these tools from Flowcision. Ai Global Solutions is 
now the only vendor with rights to the Apper and FlowBase tools used in the Nintex 
Automation On-Premise platform, which SWFPAC currently utilizes. 
6. Market Research Conducted 
In summary, market research did not yield another company that could provide services with the 
form building tools Apper and FlowBase which are a requirement to successfully and seamlessly 
perform the database migration under the required timeframe.  
7. Description of Efforts Made to Solicit Offers from as Many Potential Sources as 
Practicable
In addition to the market research described in Section 6 above, the requirement will be posted to 
the SAM.gov allowing as many potential sources as practicable to review the requirement and 
submit a quote or offer.  
8. Fair and Reasonable Determination
All quotations or offers will be evaluated in accordance with FAR 13.106-3(a) and award will 
not be made unless the Contracting Officer can find the price fair and reasonable. 
9. Actions to Remove or Overcome Barriers to Competition 
Based upon the aforementioned facts and details identified in Paragraphs 5 and 6 above, the 
Government does not foresee a feasible alternative to the selected source
SWFPAC will continue to monitor the marketplace for viable, cost-effective options to meet this 
requirement.

Section C - Description/Specifications/Statement of Work
Statement of Work
Nintex Automation Migration Services
1.0 Background:
Strategic Weapons Facility Pacific (SWFPAC) is migrating forms/workflow services from legacy Microsoft Access database products to the new 
product Nintex Automation (aka K2).
SWFPAC currently supports a legacy Microsoft Access database identified as mission critical and is in need of immediate migration. SWFPAC 
requires professional assistance to accelerate the migration in order to meet the deadline while ensuring required data integrity, database access, 
and solution operability. 
2.0  Scope:
Rebuilding of SWFPAC's existing Microsoft Access solution in Nintex Automation consists of a series of lookup tables that feeds the 
Transactions, SL-3 Gains/Transfers and Form 10576 forms, which are the core functionality of the solution. SWFPAC will provide exported 
copies of all specifications and data structures for the forms and workflows from the existing solution. SWFPAC will submit the exports via 
uploading to the vendors secure file storage. The vendor will send back the completed products via the same secure file storage.
3.0 Deliverable: 
The contractor shall provide up to   223 hours of consultation/architect services to complete the migration of the solution, including the following 
solution items: 
Solution Item Qty.
SmartObjects 36
SQL Stored Procedures 4
Lookup Table Forms 12
Lookup Table Approval Workflow 1
Additional Approval Workflows 11
SL-3 Gains / Transfers Form 1
SL-3 Gains / Transfers Approval Workflow 1
Transactions Form 1
Transactions Approval Workflow 1
10576 Form 1
10576 Form - Print PDF 1
N6471026Q0004
Page 4 of 37

Auditing for SQL tables 1
Solution Architecture Documentation 1
Knowledge Transfer 1
 
     4.0 Place of Performance:
     Vendor will work remotely on their own equipment and with their own software.
     5.0 Period of Performance:  
     All work must be completed no later than June 5, 2026.
     6.0 Security:
     All data exported from the SWFPAC environment will be scrubbed of any Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) by SWFPAC personnel.
 
Requirements
Support services for the migration of SWFPACs existing Microsoft Access solution in Nintex Automation on-
premises. This solution consists of the use proprietary tools, Apper and FlowBase, and K2 templates to build 
and assist in migrating this complex solution with associated forms and workflows currently in Microsoft 
Access to Nintex Automation.

GENERAL INFORMATION

PLACE OF PERFORMANCE

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

DESCRIPTION

The Department of Veterans Affairs, Network Contracting Office 17  Regional Procurement Office West 124 E Hwy 67 Suite 100 Duncanville TX intends to solely negotiate with AgileRank, LLC 21515 Ridgetop Circle, Suite 310 Sterling, VA 20166-6509 for the procurement of Operations Room Control automation features tied to the implementation of a Real Rime Location Systems (RTLS) such as CenTrak to assist location at every level of locating using communication devices.  This service will be procured using existing Service Maintenance Agreements between AgileRank and the VA to maintain and upgrade RTLS. 

This procurement is being conducted in accordance with RFO 12.102 (a), Soliciting from a Single Source.

THIS NOTICE OF INTENT IS NOT A REQUEST FOR COMPETITIVE QUOTES. However, Contractors may submit a capability statement, proposal, or quotation which shall be considered by the contracting officer whether to continue with the sole source procurement or to provide for competition.  Such responses MUST be received no later than Tuesday April 14, 2026, at 3:00 PM CST.  Forward responses by e-mail to Francisco.Mendoza@va.gov .  Due to technical issues, please allow additional time if you’re using electronic means.  

Responses received will be evaluated; however, a determination by the Government not to compete the proposed procurement based upon responses received to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government.

The Department of Veterans Affairs, NCO 17 will NOT be responsible for any costs incurred by interested parties in responding to this notice of intent. Only written responses will be considered. All responses from responsible sources will be fully considered. As a result of analyzing responses to this notice of intent, the Government shall determine if a solicitation will be issued. Any prospective contractor must be registered in the System for Award Management (SAM) in order to be eligible for award. Also, prospective contractor shall be an Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) authorized dealer, authorized distributor or authorized reseller to the Department of Veterans Affairs for the proposed equipment/system or service such that OEM warranty and service are provided and maintained by the OEM. All software licensing, warranty and service associated with the equipment/system shall be in accordance with the OEM terms and conditions. Interested parties are encouraged to furnish information by email only with Response to this Intent to Sole Source this acquisition. All documents submitted will not be returned. All interested parties shall submit clear and convincing documentation demonstrating their capabilities to satisfy the requirement. 

DISCLAIMER:  This notice is issued informational purposes pursuant to RFO 5.301.  Responses to this notice are not offers and cannot be accepted by the Department of Veterans Affairs to form a binding contract. 

End of Document

Subject: Notice to Industry – Planned Solicitation Will Be Issued via GSA

Dear Industry Partners,

The Government thanks all parties who responded to the recent Request for Information (RFI) regarding 36C10B26Q0224 Contact Center Consolidation - RFI -PWS. The feedback and capability statements received were valuable in helping the Government assess available solutions and determine the most appropriate acquisition strategy.

Based on the information gathered through the RFI, the Government has determined that the forthcoming solicitation will be issued through the GSA MAS.

Industry partners interested in participating in this requirement should ensure they hold the appropriate GSA contract vehicle and the applicable Special Item Number(s) (SINs) at the time of solicitation issuance.

This notice is for informational purposes only and does not constitute a solicitation or a commitment by the Government. Additional details, including the anticipated release date of the solicitation, will be made available through GSA and/or SAM.gov as appropriate.

Thank you for your continued interest and engagement.

GENERAL INFORMATION

PLACE OF PERFORMANCE

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

DESCRIPTION

See Attached

Request for Information 

Revenue Operations Workflow, Payer Compliance and Technical Consulting

VA-26-00042471

Introduction

This Request for Information (RFI) is for planning purposes only and shall not be considered an Invitation for Bid, Request for Task Execution Plan, Request for Quotation or a Request for Proposal.  Additionally, there is no obligation on the part of the Government to acquire any products or services described in this RFI.  Your response to this RFI will be treated only as information for the Government to consider.  You will not be entitled to payment for direct or indirect costs that you incur in responding to this RFI.  This request does not constitute a solicitation for proposals or the authority to enter into negotiations to award a contract.  No funds have been authorized, appropriated or received for this effort.  Interested parties are responsible for adequately marking proprietary, restricted or competition sensitive information contained in their response.  The Government does not intend to pay for the information submitted in response to this RFI.

The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) for this requirement is 541512 with a size standard of $34 million.  

2. Submittal Information:

All responsible sources may submit a response in accordance with the below information.  

There is a page limitation for this RFI of 15 pages. The Government will not review any other information or attachments included, that are in excess of the 15-page limit. NO MARKETING MATERIALS ARE ALLOWED AS PART OF THIS RFI.  Generic capability statements will not be accepted or reviewed.  Your response must address capabilities specific to the services required in the attached PWS and must include the following:

Interested Vendors shall at a minimum, provide the following information in the initial paragraph of the submission:

Name of Company

Address

Point of Contact

Phone Number

Fax Number

Email address

Company Business Size and Status

For VOSB and SDVOSBs, proof of SBA certification. SDVOSB/VOSB vendors that have not been certified by SBA should not respond to this notice.	

NAICS code(s)

Socioeconomic data

Unique Entity Identifier (UEI) Number

Existing Contractual Vehicles (GWAC, FSS, or MAC)

Provide a summary of your ability to meet the requirements contained within the draft PWS for the following areas:

Provide a summary of your technical capability to meet PWS paragraphs 5.2- Revenue Operations Workflow Functionality and Cloud Computing, 5.3- Claims Scoring, Reporting, and Payment Efficacy, and 5.4- Managerial and Technical Support.  Your response shall include all software tools that will be leveraged in order to fulfill the PWS requirements.

If you were to submit a proposal, would your company be the sole provider of the complete scope of services, or is your company planning to provide any of the services sought in partnership with other entities? If it will be in partnership with another entity (or entities), identify the scope of services that will be provided by subcontractors or partners, the names of anticipated partners and the percentage(s) of the service(s) that will be provided by each of the subcontractor(s) or partner(s).Provide an overview of your company including primary services that you provide, size of the company, year established etc. 

Describe your relevant experience in providing a similar scope of services to Government agencies or commercial organizations of similar scope, size and complexity to the VA. Include the agency name, project type, scope, length, and contract number (if applicable). Your response shall address the following areas:

Describe any past or present cloud migration and implementation activities your company has managed or supported across a business enterprise. Please explain in detail the services provided in a cloud-based environment to include sustainment services for a customer base similar in size to Revenue Operations and its seven CPACs.

Please explain your experience executing revenue centric workflow management services. Workflow that generates items that are both automated and set for manual review and subsequently distributed to staff throughout a large enterprise. 

Please provide your past or present approach to providing robotic process automation services that support automation assistance to approved work processes that reduced manual staff intervention to increase business process efficiencies.

Please provide your past or present experience in providing claims scoring, reporting and payment efficacy for a large healthcare entity.

Provide your experience with building, updating and maintaining compliant modeling of insurance carrier agreements comparing actual reimbursement to expected payments while quantifying payment variances based on reimbursement methodology.

Please provide your approach to past or present enterprise-wide operational process and staffing improvement support for a large organization in support of tactical and strategic goals/metrics.

Provide your companies experience supporting a national level program in the following:

Developing, optimizing, maintaining  and assisting in the delivery of operational training and policy development, refinement.

Electronic HealthCare Record Management (EHRM) industry best practice requirements development, implementation and “go live” readiness.

Change management support on large scale initiatives and operational activities.

HIPAA / X12, NCPDP EDI subject matter expertise in a Healthcare Revenue cycle environment.

The development of executive level operations reports and business case products on both a regular and as needed (ad hoc) basis. These briefings often require a comparison of private sector Revenue Operations’ best practices, business standards or metrics.  These executive level documents and briefings may be extended to VA under-secretary, Legislative and Office of the Inspector General (IG) personnel.

For SDVOSB/VOSB vendors: Your company’s intent and ability to meet the set aside requirement in accordance with VAAR 852.219-73 (JAN 2023) (DEVIATION) VA Notice of Total Set-Aside For Certified SDVOSBs and 13 CFR §125.6, which states the contractor will not pay more than 50% of the amount paid by the government to the prime for contract performance to firms that are not certified SDVOSBs listed in the SBA certification database (excluding direct costs to the extent they are not the principal purpose of the acquisition and the SDVOSB/VOSB does not provide the service, such as airline travel, cloud computing services, or mass media purchases). When a contract includes both services and supplies, the 50 percent limitation shall apply only to the service portion of the contract. 

Your response shall include information as to available personnel and financial resources; full names of proposed team members and the PWS requirements planned to be subcontracted to them, which must include the prime planned percentage or the names of the potential team members that may be used to fulfill the set aside requirement. 

Has the draft PWS provided sufficient detail to describe the technical requirements that encompass the software development and production operations support services to be performed under this effort. 

______ YES ______ NO (if No, answer question f)

If “NO”, please provide your technical comments/recommendations on elements of the draft PWS that may contribute to a more accurate proposal submission and efficient, cost effective effort.

 

Responses are due no later than 10:00am EST on February 27, 2026 via email to Gary Throckmorton, Contract Specialist at Gary.Throckmorton@va.gov and Christopher Minetti, Contracting Officer at Christopher.Minetti@va.gov.  Please note “RFI - Revenue Operations Workflow, Payer Compliance and Technical Consulting” in the subject line of your response.  Mark your response as “Proprietary Information” if the information is considered business sensitive.  The email file size shall not exceed 5 MB.

BACKGROUND

The Department of Veterans Affairs (VA), Veterans Health Administration (VHA), is one of the world’s largest health care delivery organizations. The Revenue Operations (RO) team is seeking ongoing healthcare revenue industry modeled best practice management consulting services and healthcare revenue operations business information technology tools to sustain, transform and continuously improve revenue cycle activities within the Office of Finance (OF). Revenue Operations (RO) and the seven (7) regionally aligned Consolidated Patient Account Centers (CPACs) support a vast healthcare enterprise in the service of our Nation’s Veterans. The goal of RO is to support the field in the management of the revenue cycle. As part of that effort, RO provides a variety of program services including the following: payer relations, metric-based quality / policy operational analyses, rate development, eBusiness EDI solutions and best practice modeling with a focus on process standardization to enhance revenues.

The Consolidated Patient Account Center (CPAC) uses standardized methods, tools, and processes to improve efficiency, effectiveness, and accountability in revenue operations. CPAC’s state-of-the-art business processes ensure that VA collects revenue from private health insurance carriers for nonservice-connected care provided to insured Veterans. These funds go back to the VA Medical Centers (VAMCs) to provide health care services to our Veterans. There are seven regional CPACs located around the country that provide revenue cycle management to all the  Veterans Integrated Service Network (VISNs) and their associated VAMCs. CPACs perform back-end revenue cycle processes while the VAMCs maintain ownership of key Veteran-facing revenue functions.


Transformation from the VA current state legacy system to the Oracle Health Electronic Health Record (OH EHRM) and its related health and revenue information technology tools is a critical endeavor for Revenue Operations. The RO team requires support to maintain and improve its best practice private sector revenue cycle performance while it transforms, as necessary, to the Oracle Health revenue technology tools. During preparation, deployment and stabilization of OH EHRM, RO seeks to continue its utilization of healthcare revenue business best practices and healthcare revenue business tools which provide work drivers to track and prioritize key revenue cycle activities, provide improved revenue collection services that focus on integral cash generation, improved payer management, standardization of processes, training and development of staff, along with extensive use of metrics and analytics to measure and validate performance.

These efforts are in response to Public Law 110-387, Section 406, which requires Revenue Operations and the CPACs to carry out the following functions: (1) Reengineer and integrate all business processes of the revenue cycle of the Department; (2) Standardize and coordinate all activities of the Department related to the revenue cycle for all health care services furnished to Veterans for nonservice-connected medical conditions; (3) Apply commercial industry standards for measures of access, timeliness, and performance metrics with respect to revenue enhancement of the Department; and (4) Apply other requirements with respect to such revenue cycle improvement as the Secretary may specify.  In meeting these goals, VA VHA strives to provide high quality, effective, and efficient Information Technology (IT) services to those responsible for providing care to the Veterans at the point-of-care as well as throughout all the points of the Veterans’ health care in an effective, timely and compassionate manner.  VA depends on Information Management/Information Technology (IM/IT) systems to meet mission goals.

The VA OIT Health Finance Product Line (FPL) supports Revenue Operations.  FPL strives to
