Statement of Work (SOW)

Application Development and Support Services (ADSS)
FMCS

1.0 BACKGROUND

The Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service (FMCS) is an independent federal agency established under the Labor Management Relations Act of 1947 (Taft-Hartley). FMCS provides mediation, conflict resolution, and training services nationwide and internationally.

FMCS operates a cloud-based digital environment built primarily on Microsoft 365 (M365), including SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams & Integrations, Power Platform components, and integrated third-party systems such as Collabware Collabspace for Electronic Records Management (ERM) and LMS365 for learning management. The environment supports enterprise-wide collaboration, records management, training systems, and operational applications 

FMCS requires contractor support to provide Application Development and Support Services (ADSS) across its enterprise Microsoft 365 environment and all related systems. The Contractor shall deliver comprehensive, full lifecycle IT services, to include application development, modernization, system integration, operations and maintenance (O&M), system assessments, cloud migration support, and security and compliance services. These services will ensure FMCS systems remain secure, reliable, scalable, and aligned with agency mission needs.

1.1 PURPOSE

To obtain comprehensive Application Development and Support Services (ADSS) to support FMCS’s enterprise application ecosystem, cloud platforms, and digital services.

1.2 OBJECTIVE

To provide full lifecycle support for application development, modernization, integration, and operations and maintenance (O&M) across FMCS’s Microsoft 365 environment and all related systems.

1.3 CONTRACT OVERSIGHT

All work shall be performed under the direction of the FMCS Chief Information Officer (CIO) and the Contracting Officer Representative (COR). The contractor’s program management activities shall also provide support for change management, risk management, and performance management in alignment with the CIO’s strategic goals.

1.4 MONTHLY STATUS REPORT (MSR)

The Contractor shall provide a Monthly Status Report (MSR) summarizing project activities, progress, risks, and performance. The MSR shall be submitted no later than the 5th of each month via email to the COR and CIO.

1.4.1 Contents

Executive Summary

Status of all current projects, including: 

Risks and issues

Planned and actual completion dates

Current phase

Disposition (red/yellow/green)

Key milestones

Upcoming activities

Staffing updates

Operations Performance Matrix

1.4.2 Format

One-page coversheet

No more than 3 pages per project

Moderate margins

12-point Times New Roman

1.4.3 Final MSR

A final MSR shall be submitted on the last business day of the final period of performance.

1.4.4 Monthly Financial Dashboard

Delivered monthly to the COR, including:

Planned

Actuals

Variance

2.0 SCOPE OF WORK

The Contractor shall provide full lifecycle Application Development and System Support (ADSS) services across FMCS’s Microsoft 365 ecosystem and associated application environments. The Contractor shall deliver secure, compliant, and modernized solutions that support FMCS mission needs, ensure operational continuity, and enable ongoing modernization.

2.1 Required Services

The Contractor shall provide, at a minimum, the following services:

• Application Development, Modernization, Enhancement, and Integration

Design, develop, enhance, and integrate applications across FMCS environments.

Modernize legacy applications and support cloud-ready architectures.

• Microsoft 365 Platform Support

Provide support for SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, Power Apps, Power Pages, and Power Automate.

Maintain and enhance workflows, forms, portals, and collaboration solutions.

• Enterprise Systems Support

Provide operational and enhancement support for FMCS’s Enterprise Records Management (ERM) and Learning Management System (LMS) platforms.

• Operations and Maintenance (O&M)

Deliver ongoing O&M and support for all supported systems, including troubleshooting, patching, monitoring, and performance optimization.

Maintain system documentation, run books, and configuration records.

• System Assessments and Modernization Recommendations

Conduct assessments of existing systems and provide modernization roadmaps aligned with industry standards and best practices.

• Security and Compliance

Ensure compliance with FISMA, NIST, and OMB A-130 requirements.

Ensure Section 508 accessibility compliance for applicable ICT.

Support governance, data management, and security documentation.

• Cloud Migration and Legacy Modernization

Provide planning, analysis, and execution support for cloud migration initiatives.

Modernize legacy systems to align with FMCS’s cloud strategy.

• Enhancement of Systems and Processes

Improve systems, applications, and business processes to align with industry best practices and evolving FMCS needs.

2.2 Contract Line-Item Numbers (CLINs)

CLIN 1 – Operations & Maintenance (O&M), Minor Enhancements, and Support Activities

The Contractor shall:

Provide O&M & support for all Microsoft 365 systems, CCMS, Arbitration systems, and FMCS web.

Perform minor development or enhancement tasks requiring less than 40 hours per issue or task.

Provide security compliance support (Agency/Federal) and transition completed work into O&M.

Provide planning, coordination, and support for new initiatives.

Maintain system documentation, run books, and technical artifacts.

CLIN 2 – Major Enhancements and New Initiatives

The Contractor shall:

Perform major enhancements requiring greater than 40 hours per issue or task.

Support development activities for new initiatives.

Provide security compliance support (Agency/Federal) and transition completed work into O&M.

Develop and maintain documentation, run books, and technical artifacts.

3.0 CONTRACTOR REQUIREMENTS

3.1 Technical Qualifications

The Contractor shall possess:

Demonstrated expertise in Microsoft 365 development and administration

Experience with Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Power Pages, Power BI), Microsoft Teams Integrations, Azure Services

Experience supporting enterprise content management and LMS systems

Familiarity with federal IT security, compliance, and governance requirements

Experience ensuring achieving ATO and establishing continuous monitoring for compliance with FISMA (High and Moderate), NIST 800-53, OMB A-130, FMCS IT security policies, and Section 508 accessibility requirements for all applicable Information and Communication Technology (ICT).

Experience with cloud migration planning and modern application architecture

Expertise in modern, OpenSource platform capabilities, including application development, data modeling, integration, and migration planning

Ability to document and analyze legacy systems (Dynamics, Power Apps, Dataverse, C#, .NET, SQL Server)

CMMI Level 3 Dev and services

Experience with relational Databases: SQLServer, Postgres, Oracle

Experience design and implement enterprise reports and dashboards

Experience implementing forms and workflows

Experience with regulatory agencies, and distributed userbase across the country and the world.

Experience with environments in Azure cloud

3.2 Experience

Minimum five (5) years providing ADSS in cloud environments

Experience supporting small to medium federal agencies and sub agencies

Experience supporting modernization or migration initiatives

Minimum ten (10) years implementing, modernizing and maintaining enterprise, mission-critical systems

3.3 Personnel Security

Tier 2 (Moderate Risk) background investigation with favorable adjudication

4.0 TASKS

4.1 INVESTIGATION PHASE – 3 Months

The Contractor shall conduct a comprehensive investigation of the FMCS Microsoft 365 environment during the initial three-month period. This phase runs concurrently with the O&M Phase.

4.1.1 Investigation Activities

Kickoff meeting within one (1) week of award.

Evaluate the current M365 environment (SharePoint, Teams, Power Apps, Power Pages, Power BI, LMS365, Collabspace, Dataverse, SQL, Azure Services).

Evaluate incomplete tasks from previous vendor.

Conduct interviews and review processes to identify issues and errors.

Collect requirements for new features and applications.

Submit documentation and present findings.

Provide modernization and mitigation recommendations, including data storage recommendations (SharePoint lists, Dataverse, SQL).

4.2 OPERATIONS AND MAINTENANCE PHASE – 12 Months (Concurrent)

4.2.1 O&M Activities

Redevelop under-functioning Power Apps.

Develop new sites, Power Apps, and Power Pages.

Update recordkeeping strategies to meet NARA/OMB requirements.

Implement new LMS365 features and support separation of LMS and Intranet home sites.

Conduct ongoing O&M for SharePoint, Collabspace, LMS365, Power Apps, Power Pages, and Dataverse.

Provide collaboration and training for FMCS IT staff and the eLearning Training Specialist.

Procure and manage licensing for Collabspace, LMS365, and approved add-ons.

Provide full documentation and training for applications and customizations.

Continuously evaluate FMCS systems against industry standards and recommend enhancements to improve performance, usability, accessibility, maintainability, and security.

4.2.2 Active Applications Currently Maintained Under This Contract

4.3 OPTION PERIODS – Two (2) Option Years, 12 Months Each

Development and modernization

Operations and maintenance

Continued licensing of Collabspace and Collabmail

4.4 COMPLIANCE, GOVERNANCE, AND RECORD MANAGEMENT

The Contractor shall:

Support NARA/OMB compliance

Maintain ERM tools and processes

Ensure adherence to FMCS governance, accessibility, and data-management standards

Ensure all systems comply with FISMA, NIST 800-53, OMB A-130, FMCS IT security policies, and Section 508 accessibility requirements, including conformance with the Revised 508 Standards and WCAG 2.0 Level AA for all applicable Information and Communication Technology (ICT).

Support ATO documentation, POA&Ms, and continuous monitoring

Implement secure development practices

Ensure enhancements align with industry standards for security, accessibility, data governance, and digital service delivery

4.5 LICENSING AND PLATFORM SUPPORT

Assist with license management

Provide optimization recommendations

4.6 OPTIONAL TASK – Legacy Systems, Web, and Cloud Migration Roadmaps

All optional task deliverables shall include:

Cost estimates for recommended modernization activities

Cost-benefit analysis

Industry-standards alignment analysis

Security modernization recommendations

Future-state architecture

Implementation prerequisites and sequencing

4.6.1 CCMS Documentation, Modernization Assessment, Migration Roadmap & Migration Implementation

Current System Overview

Platform: Microsoft Dynamics / Power Apps

Hosting: Azure GCC Medium

Criticality: Medium

Known Issues: Aging architecture, limited extensibility

A. CCMS Documentation

Document architecture, data model, workflows, integrations, security roles, hosting configuration, and compliance posture.

B. CCMS Modernization Assessment

Identify modernization opportunities, technical debt, data strategy improvements, and alignment with industry standards.

C. Migration Roadmap (Planning Only)

Includes feasibility analysis, migration approach, data mapping, integration requirements, security requirements, cost estimates, cost-benefit analysis, future-state architecture, and sequencing.

D. CCMS Migration Implementation (Optional Execution Task)

If exercised, includes configuration, development, data migration, integration, testing, deployment, training, and post-migration support.

E. Deliverables

CCMS Documentation Package

Modernization Findings Report

Migration Roadmap

Optional: Migration Implementation Plan

Optional: Completed Migration

4.6.2 Arbitration System Documentation, Modernization Assessment, Migration Roadmap & Migration Implementation

Current System Overview

Tech Stack: Custom .NET application on Azure VMs with SQL Server backend

Users: Public-facing; ~661 arbitrators; 12,000+ external users

Criticality: High/Medium

Known Issues: Legacy architecture, security vulnerabilities

A. Arbitration System Documentation

Document architecture, SQL schema, workflows, integrations, hosting configuration, and compliance posture.

B. Modernization Assessment

Identify modernization opportunities, data strategy improvements, and alignment with industry standards.

C. Migration Roadmap (Planning Only)

Includes feasibility analysis, SQL mapping, integration requirements, security requirements, cost estimates, cost-benefit analysis, future-state architecture, and sequencing.

D. Arbitration Migration Implementation (Optional Execution Task)

If exercised, includes configuration, development, data migration, integration, testing, deployment, training, and post-migration support.

E. Deliverables

Arbitration Documentation Package

Modernization Findings Report

Migration Roadmap

Optional: Migration Implementation Plan

Optional: Completed Migration

4.6.3 FMCS Web Environment Documentation 

Current System Overview

Tech Stack: WordPress, Apache, AWS

Users: 118,000+ annually

Criticality: High

Known Issues: OMB compliance risks, accessibility issues

Document WordPress configuration, Apache and AWS architecture, DNS/DNSSEC, integrations, security posture, modernization opportunities, and cost estimates.

4.6.4 AWS to Azure Migration Roadmap & Migration Implementation

A. AWS Environment Assessment

The Contractor shall assess the current AWS hosting environment, including workloads, architecture, DNS/DNSSEC, integrations, performance, and security posture.

B. Azure Migration Roadmap (Planning Only)

The Contractor shall develop a detailed migration roadmap including:

Migration strategy

Future-state Azure architecture

Data and application migration plan

Security and compliance mapping

DNS/DNSSEC transition plan

Cost estimates for migration, hosting, re-architecture, and decommissioning

Implementation sequencing and dependencies

C. AWS to Azure Migration Implementation (Optional Execution Task)

If exercised, the Contractor shall:

Prepare Azure environment

Migrate applications, data, and services

Re-platform or re-architect components as required

Transition DNS/DNSSEC

Conduct testing and deployment

Provide post-migration stabilization

Decommission AWS resources

D. Deliverables

AWS Environment Assessment Report

Azure Migration Roadmap

Optional: Migration Implementation Plan

Optional: Completed AWS→Azure Migration

Optional: AWS Decommissioning Report

5.0 DELIVERABLES

5.1 Project Plan for Investigation Phase
5.2 Investigation schedule and stakeholder list
5.3 Investigation report
5.4 Monthly Status Reports (MSR)
5.5 Monthly Financial Dashboard

STATEMENT OF OBJECTIVES FOR: INMATE 
TABLET INITIATIVE 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
JANUARY
 2026 
 

Inmate Tablet Initiative 
Statement of Objectives 
Page 2 of 32 
 
Executive Summary 
The FBOP Enterprise Tablet Initiative seeks to design, deploy, and sustain a secure, scalable, and 
compliant digital services platform that will provide approximately 150,000 users across 120 facilities 
nationwide with equitable access to education, healthcare, legal, religious, and communication 
resources. The program’s purpose is to enhance service delivery while maintaining strict adherence to 
federal IT security and accessibility standards. 
The initiative encompasses the full lifecycle of planning, procurement, installation, configuration, and 
sustainment of tablet devices and supporting infrastructure. This includes extending wired and wireless 
network access to the housing unit bed level and designated service areas (visitation, education, 
medical, psychology, religious, and recreational spaces) with explicit prohibitions on wireless bridging. 
All tablet devices will support modern Wi-Fi standards (IEEE 802.11a/b/g/n/ac, Wi-Fi 5, Wi-Fi 6, Wi-Fi 6E, 
and forward compatibility with Wi-Fi 7). All other equipment will support wired Ethernet connectivity 
(IEEE 802.3 over Cat6 cabling). Hosting will reside exclusively in GovCloud environments, with 
mandatory FedRAMP High certification and full FISMA compliance. 
The solution must enable secure subscription-based access to streaming, downloadable content, and 
learning modules, while enforcing restrictions such as single-player gaming only. Additional services 
include AM/FM radio - television, mail scanning, and secure international and local calling capabilities. 
Telehealth (psychology and medical visits), religious programming, legal, and educational visits will be 
proctored or confined to approved designated locations. To ensure adherence to FBOP IT Standards the 
vendor must enable FBOP administrative oversight through role-based access controls with nested 
group management up to five layers deep, ensuring granular control across devices, groups, locations, 
and content. 
A seven-day Service Level Agreement (SLA) for device repair or replacement is required to minimize 
downtime, ensure service continuity, and minimize user volatility due to device failure. All software and 
firmware updates must be applied within 14 days of release, with vendors required to provide 
documentation, testing certifications, and ongoing interoperability validation. Accessibility remains a 
cornerstone of the program, with Section 508 compliance and features such as closed captioning, TTY 
integration, and note-taking functionality required to support users with disabilities. 
Point of Contact  
Ryan Wynne, Section Chief Technology Procurement Section 
Phone:  202-616-5860 
Email:  rwynne@bop.gov

Statement of Objectives 
Page 5 of 32 
 
1. Purpose  
The initiative enhances the Federal Bureau of Prisons ( FBOP) rehabilitative mission by expanding 
digital access in a controlled, compliant environment. Tablets will serve as a unified delivery 
mechanism for educational modules, tele -health, family communication, legal resources, reentry 
programming, and approved leisure content. This initiative also strengthens institutional safety by 
digitizing forms, reducing paper handling, enabling controlled communication channels, and 
increasing transparency around user activity. 
2. Scope 
The program includes enterprise-wide deployment of secure tablets and supporting wired  back-end 
infrastructure with wireless connectivity to endpoint devices  spanning to the housing unit bed level 
and all designated program areas . The solution must operate within FedRAMP -High GovCloud 
environments, comply with FISMA and DOJ cybersecurity directives, and integrate seamlessly with 
FBOP’s existing IT landscape. Vendors shall provide network architecture capable of supporting long-
term expansion. The initiative seeks an enterprise solution that: 
• Provides secure tablets and supporting network infrastructure reaching all designated areas, 
including housing units (bed -level), visitation, education, medical, psychology, religious, and 
recreational areas. 
• Enables subscription-based leisure content, educational , recreational, religious and 
psychological resources, and controlled communication services. 
• Supports tele-health, legal visits, educational sessions, and religious programming in 
designated locations only. 
• Ensures strong administrative oversight using role -based access controls, nested security 
groups, content filtering, and usage controls. 
• Includes enterprise-wide support services, hardware replacement SLAs, software/firmware 
update compliance, and performance reporting. 
• Integrates AI -enabled analytics to improve intelligence gathering, anomaly detection, and 
operational insight while maintaining privacy and National Institute of Standards and 
Technology
 aligned safeguards. 
3. Period and Place of Performance 
The Cumulative Period of Performance will be a base period of 48 months from the Notice to Proceed  
followed by  three independently exercised 12 -month option periods and one 6 -month extension 
period. Services will be provided at All Federal Bureau of Prison Institutions (approx. 120 institutions 
as well as Satellite facilities (camps, and detention centers, etc.). 
Service Level Agreement (SLA): 
• Vendor must meet a 7 -day repair/replacement SLA for any tablet, kiosk and/or charging 
device failures.

PERFORMANCE WORK STATEMENT (PWS)

Enterprise Technical Documentation, Modernization Strategy, and Roadmap Development Services.

BACKGROUND

The Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service (FMCS) operates a cloud‑based enterprise digital environment centered on Microsoft 365 (M365) services and integrated commercial and custom applications. The current ecosystem includes SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, Power Platform components, Dataverse, SQL Server, Collabspace (ERM), LMS365, Azure cloud services, a .NET C# application, an AWS‑hosted WordPress public website, and multiple legacy custom-developed systems.

FMCS requires performance‑based contractor support to develop authoritative enterprise technical documentation, including system overviews, architecture descriptions, and functional designs that define how FMCS business processes, user interactions, data flows, and system capabilities will operate within the future‑state enterprise environment. The Contractor shall also develop modernization strategies, migration roadmaps, and future‑state architecture recommendations. All services must align with federal IT, cybersecurity, and records management requirements and support the Agency’s long‑term goals for modernization, interoperability, and operational resilience.

2.0 PURPOSE

To obtain performance-based services for enterprise technical documentation,

modernization strategy development, and future-state roadmap generation across the

Agency’s digital ecosystem.

3.0 OBJECTIVE

The Contractor shall deliver comprehensive system documentation packages,

modernization assessments, cloud migration roadmaps, Migration feasibility studies and

analysis of alternatives (Salesforce, Power Platform, Opensource, others), enterprise future-state architecture blueprints, governance and compliance alignment frameworks, and technology strategy plans supporting executive decision-making and digital

transformation.

4.0 PERFORMANCE-BASED SCOPE

The Contractor shall perform work based on measurable outcomes including

documentation completeness, modernization recommendations, compliance traceability,

and roadmap sequencing.

4.1 ENTERPRISE TECHNICAL DOCUMENTATION

Deliver complete documentation packages including architecture diagrams, data

models, workflow documentation, integration maps, security models, hosting

configurations, compliance posture mapping (FISMA, NIST 800-53, OMB A-130,

Section 508), dependency mapping, risk identification, and licensing inventories.

4.2 MODERNIZATION ASSESSMENTS

Provide structured modernization analysis including technical debt assessment, security

posture evaluation, accessibility review, governance assessment, scalability analysis,

prioritized recommendations, and modernization sequencing.

4.3 FUTURE-STATE ARCHITECTURE & STRATEGY

Develop a 1–3 year enterprise modernization strategy including future-state

architecture, application rationalization, cloud optimization roadmap, data strategy,

governance enhancements, security modernization plan, DevSecOps framework,

reporting and analytics strategy, and budgetary planning framework.

4.4 SYSTEM-SPECIFIC DOCUMENTATION & ROADMAPS

Produce documentation and strategy deliverables for:

• Microsoft 365 ecosystem

• CCMS (Dynamics / Power Apps – Azure GCC)

• Arbitration System (.NET / SQL / Azure)

• WordPress Public Web Environment (AWS)

• WordPress AWS to Azure Migration

Each shall include current-state documentation, modernization assessment, cost-benefit

analysis, future-state architecture, and migration sequencing plan.

4.5 COMPLIANCE & GOVERNANCE ALIGNMENT

All deliverables shall align with FISMA , NIST 800-53, OMB A-130, Section 508 (WCAG

2.0 AA), NARA requirements, secure development practices, ATO documentation,

POA&M tracking, and continuous monitoring frameworks.

4.6 INVESTIGATION PHASE (FIRST 90 DAYS)

Deliver Investigation Project Plan, stakeholder interview summaries, system inventory,

architecture baseline report, technical debt register, risk register, and modernization

opportunity matrix within 90 days of award.

4.7 REPORTING REQUIREMENTS

Provide Monthly Status Reports including executive summary, documentation progress,

roadmap development status, risks/issues, milestones, and financial dashboard

(planned vs actual vs variance).

5.0 DELIVERABLE SCHEDULE

• Investigation Project Plan – 20 Days; Day 20

• Baseline Documentation – 45 Days; Day 45

• System Documentation Packages – Rolling Delivery

• Modernization Reports – Within 45 Days after Baseline; Day 90

• Enterprise Strategy Report – 60 Days; Day 60

• Migration Roadmaps – 90 Days; Day 90

• Final Consolidated Strategy & Roadmap – 120 Days; Day 120

6.0 PERFORMANCE METRICS

• Documentation Accuracy ≥90%

• Cost Estimate Reliability ±20%

• Compliance Coverage 100%

• On-Time Delivery ≥90%

7.0 PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE

Base Period: 4 months

8.0 ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

Deliverables will be accepted when documentation is complete and accurate, roadmaps

include sequencing and cost models, compliance traceability is validated, and executive

stakeholders approve.

SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE 
Enterprise Technical Documentation, Modernization 
Strategy, and Roadmap Development Services 
Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service (FMCS) 
NAICS Codes Under Consideration: 541512, 541519 
_________________________________________________________________________________________________________ 
GENERAL INFORMATION 
THIS IS A SOURCES SOUGHT ANNOUNCEMENT ONLY. This notice is issued for market 
research purposes in accordance with FAR Part 10. This announcement does not constitute 
a solicitation or a promise to issue a solicitation in the future. The Government will not pay 
for any information submitted or for any costs associated with providing a response to this 
notice. 
 
The Federal Mediation and Conciliation Service (FMCS) is conducting market research to 
identify qualified and interested firms capable of providing enterprise technical 
documentation, modernization strategy development, and roadmap development services. 
 
The Government intends to use responses to this Sources Sought notice to support market 
research, refine the draft scope, develop an Independent Government Cost Estimate (IGCE), 
and determine the appropriate acquisition strategy, including potential small business set-
aside decisions. 
BACKGROUND 
FMCS operates a cloud-based enterprise digital environment centered on Microsoft 365 
(M365) and integrated systems including SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, Power 
Platform, Dataverse, SQL Server, Collabspace (ERM), LMS365, Azure cloud services, custom 
.NET applications, AWS-hosted WordPress environments, and legacy systems. 
REQUIREMENT OVERVIEW 
FMCS seeks contractor support to develop enterprise technical documentation, 
modernization strategies, migration roadmaps, future-state architecture, and 
governance/compliance frameworks supporting agency modernization and executive 
decision-making. 
SCOPE SUMMARY 
• Enterprise technical documentation (architecture diagrams, workflows, data 
models, integration maps) 

• Modernization assessments (technical debt, security posture, scalability, 
governance) 
• Future-state architecture and strategy (1–3 year roadmap, cloud optimization, 
DevSecOps) 
• System-specific documentation and roadmaps (M365, CCMS, Arbitration system, 
WordPress, migration planning) 
INVESTIGATION PHASE 
• System inventory and stakeholder interviews 
• Architecture baseline assessment 
• Risk and technical debt identification 
• Modernization opportunity analysis 
• This phase is anticipated to occur within the first 90 days of performance. 
DELIVERABLES (REPRESENTATIVE) 
• Investigation Project Plan 
• Baseline Documentation 
• System Documentation Packages 
• Modernization Reports 
• Enterprise Strategy Report 
• Migration Roadmaps 
• Final Consolidated Strategy 
PERIOD OF PERFORMANCE 
Base Period: Approximately 4 months 
The Government requests feedback from industry on whether the proposed period of 
performance is appropriate for the scope of work described. 
REQUESTED INFORMATION FROM INDUSTRY 
A. Company Information 
• Company name 
• UEI 
• CAGE Code 
• Business size 
• Socioeconomic status 
• Point of contact 
B. Capability Statement 
• Experience with enterprise documentation 

• Experience with modernization strategy 
• Experience with cloud migration planning 
• Experience supporting federal IT environments 
C. Relevant Experience 
• Similar contracts 
• Scope and complexity 
• Contract value 
• Period of performance 
D. Pricing / IGCE SUPPORT (IMPORTANT) 
• Provide a Rough Order of Magnitude (ROM) 
• Include labor categories, hours, and rates if available 
• Break out assumptions for investigation phase, documentation, and strategy work 
E. Acquisition Strategy Input 
• Recommended contract type 
• Recommended pricing structure 
• The Government anticipates a performance-based contract structure with defined 
deliverables and requests industry feedback on the most appropriate contract type 
for this requirement, including whether a Firm Fixed Price (FFP) or hybrid approach 
is most suitable. 
• Recommended approach (deliverable vs labor based) 
F. NAICS Recommendation 
Recommend the most appropriate NAICS code and provide rationale. 
G. Feedback on Draft Requirement 
• Identify unclear requirements 
• Suggest improvements 
• Identify restrictive elements 
H. Labor Categories 
Provide typical staffing mix for this type of work. 
I. Contract Vehicles 
Identify relevant contract vehicles (GSA MAS, GWACs, etc.). 
RESPONSE INSTRUCTIONS 

Responses shall include the subject line: 'Sources Sought Response – FMCS Enterprise 
Documentation & Strategy' 
 
Responses are due by: April 10, 2026 at 5:00 PM EST 
 
Submit responses to: 
Bronson Madero 
bmadero@fmcs.gov 
 
Telephone inquiries will not be accepted. 
IMPORTANT NOTICE 
This notice is issued solely for market research purposes and does not constitute a 
solicitation. The Government does not intend to award a contract based solely on responses.

6.8. Senior Technical Instructor .................................................................................... 12 
6.9. Technical Instructor ................................................................................................ 13 
6.10. Senior Logistician ............................................................................................... 13 
6.11. Logistician .......................................................................................................... 13 
7. Requirements for Specialties and Disciplines ............................................................... 13 
7.1. Information Technology Specialties ...................................................................... 13 
7.2. Information Technology Disciplines ...................................................................... 14 
7.3. Security Group Requirements ................................................................................ 14 
 

1. Qualifications. 
The personnel qualifications for support of this contract are listed below.  For all qualifications 
requiring experience, the years of experience must have been recent and relevant and in the 
capacity detailed in the labor categories listed below.  Required technical certifications and other 
relevant qualifications may be further defined in the individual task order. 
2. Job Groupings. 
The following labor categories will be grouped into 4 distinct areas: (1) Engineer/Scientist; (2) 
Technician; (3) Security; and (4) Management/Management Support Staff.   
3. Engineer/Scientist Group 
3.1. Information Systems Engineer Level 4 
Lead contractor related efforts and provides expert consultation in the translation of business 
requirements into IT requirements, designs, and acquisition packages. Provide in-depth 
engineering analysis of IT alternatives for the U.S. Army in support of strategic modernization 
efforts. Provide telecommunications enhancement designs for medium and large-scale 
telecommunication infrastructures.  Provide interface support to IT end users, DoD IT service 
provider personnel, USAISEC, and program management offices.  Apply knowledge of 
engineering and scientific principles, computer technologies, and human cognition to design, 
implement and manage information systems.  The information systems engineer may be called 
upon to design and develop databases, develop system architectures, establish system standards, 
integrate an information system with varied data sources and networks, and generally to plan, 
integrate, design, test or operate information systems.  Bachelor of Science degree in electrical, 
computer, or electronics engineering (or closely related curriculum) from an ABET accredited 
institution with a minimum of 15 years of information systems engineering experience in two or 
more information technologies as identified in paragraph 7.1 or as specified in the individual task 
order. 
3.2. Information Systems Engineer Level 3 
Lead contractor related efforts in the translation of business requirements into IT requirements, 
designs and acquisition packages.  Provide in-depth engineering analysis of IT alternatives for 
the U.S. Army in support of strategic modernization efforts. Provide telecommunications 
enhancement designs for medium and large-scale telecommunication infrastructures. Provide 
interface support to IT end users, DoD IT service provider personnel, USAISEC, and program 
management offices.  Apply knowledge of engineering and scientific principles, computer 
technologies, and human cognition to design, implement and manage information systems.  The 
information systems engineer may be called upon to design and develop databases, develop 
system architectures, establish system standards, integrate an information system with varied 
data sources and networks, and generally to plan, integrate, design, test or operate information 
systems.  Bachelor of Science degree in electrical, computer, or electronics engineering (or 
closely related curriculum) from an ABET accredited institution with a minimum of 10 years of 
information systems engineering experience in one or more information technologies as 
identified in paragraph 7.1 or as specified in the individual task order. 

3.3. Information Systems Engineer Level 2 
Support efforts in the translation of business requirements into telecommunications 
requirements, designs and acquisition packages.   Provide in-depth engineering analysis of IT 
alternatives for the U.S. Army in support of strategic modernization efforts. Provide IT 
enhancement designs for medium and large-scale IT infrastructures. Provide interface support to 
IT end users, DoD IT service provider personnel, USAISEC, and program management offices.  
Apply knowledge of engineering and scientific principles, computer technologies, and human 
cognition to design, implement and manage information systems.  The information systems 
engineer may be called upon to design and develop databases, develop system architectures, 
establish system standards, integrate an information system with varied data sources and 
networks, and generally to plan, integrate, design, test or operate information systems.  Bachelor 
of Science degree in electrical, computer, or electronics engineering (or closely related 
curriculum) from an ABET accredited institution with a minimum of 5 years of information 
systems engineering experience in one or more information technologies as identified in 
paragraph 7.1 or as specified in the individual task order. 
3.4. Information Systems Engineer Level 1 
Support efforts in the translation of business requirements into IT requirements, designs and 
acquisition packages. Provide engineering analysis of IT solutions for the U.S. Army in support 
of strategic modernization efforts.   Provide IT designs for segments of medium and large-scale 
infrastructures. Provide engineering support to Master, Senior and Journeyman level engineers, 
USAISEC, and program management offices.  Bachelor of Science degree in electrical, 
computer, or electronics engineering (or closely related curriculum) from an ABET accredited 
institution. 
3.5. Computer Scientist Level 4 
Possess thorough expert knowledge of current and developing technologies related to the 
interoperability of information systems, information technologies, software, corporate level 
databases, protocols, networking, information processing methods, information/computer 
security, and the use of simulation and modeling tools.  Bachelor of Science degree in Computer 
Science, Information Systems, or Software Engineering from an ABET accredited institution 
with 30 semester hours in a combination of mathematics, statistics, or computer science.  Must 
have a minimum of 15 years of experience in systems design of major information technology 
systems. 
3.6. Computer Scientist Level 3 
Possess thorough knowledge of current and developing technologies related to the 
interoperability of information systems, software, corporate level databases, protocols, 
networking, information processing methods, information/computer security, and the use of 
simulation and modeling tools.  Bachelor of Science degree in Computer Science, Information 
Systems, or Software Engineering from an ABET accredited institution with 30 semester hours 
in a combination of mathematics, statistics, or computer science.  Must have a minimum of 10 
years of experience in systems design of major information technology systems. 

3.7. Computer Scientist Level 2 
Possess a working knowledge of current and developing technologies related to the 
interoperability of information systems, software, corporate level databases, protocols, 
networking, information processing methods, information/computer security, and the use of 
simulation and modeling tools.  Bachelor of Science degree in Computer Science, Information 
Systems, or Software Engineering from an ABET accredited institution with 30 semester hours 
in a combination of mathematics, statistics, or computer science.  Must have a minimum of 5 
years of experience in systems design of major information technology systems. 
3.8. Computer Scientist Level 1 
Analyzes information requirements.  Evaluates analytically and systematically problems of 
workflow, organization, and planning and assists Senior Computer Scientists and Computer 
Scientists develop appropriate corrective action.  Help develop plans for automated information 
systems from project inception to conclusion.  Identify and define problems.  Develop system 
requirements and program specifications, from which programmers prepare detailed flow charts, 
programs, and tests.  Develop, in conjunction with functional users, system alternative solutions.  
Bachelor of Science degree from an ABET accredited institution with 30 semester hours in a 
combination of mathematics, statistics, or computer science. 
3.9. Senior Computer Programmer 
Must be capable of using third and fourth generation or current state-of-the-art information 
technology equipment and languages to analyze system requirements and devise program logic 
for business, management, communication, tactical, and technical problems.  Must be able to 
develop detailed flow charts and instructions for programs, develop general run diagrams and 
process flow charts.  Must be able to develop tape layouts and record formats and add additional 
data items necessary to accomplish work products.  Must be experienced in relational and object 
oriented languages, applications, and operating systems.  Must have a minimum of 10 years of 
programming experience in software development or maintenance with a Bachelor of Science 
degree from an ABET accredited institution, or equivalent.

Statement of Work (Revised) 
AFLCMC/ES VTC and A/V System Maintenance 
 
1.0 Purpose 
The purpose of this Statement of Work (SOW) is to secure a contractor to perform 
comprehensive maintenance and support for the Video Teleconferencing (VTC) and 
Audio/Visual (A/V) system located in building 1624, conference room 203, at 5 Eglin Street, 
Hanscom AFB, MA 01731. The contractor shall ensure the system remains fully functional 
through proactive maintenance, timely hardware replacement, on-call support, and user 
training. 
2.0 Scope of Work 
• Provide maintenance for a full audio visual and URL solution for one independent 
conference room that is capable of both classiﬁed and unclassiﬁed video 
teleconferencing (VTC) connectivity.  
• Classiﬁed and unclassiﬁed VTC utilizes a single CODEC, automated periods 
processing hardware, and has NIPR/SIPR Teams capabilities.  
• Replacement parts to the VTC AV system shall comply with the established Defense 
Information Systems Agency (DISA) policy, Video Services Policy Security Technical 
Implementation Guide (STIG).  
• The system will maintain GVS comparability and remain on the Defense Information 
Systems Agency (DISA) Approved Products List (APL) and be Joint Interoperability 
Test Command (JITC) approved.  
• Video Teleconference equipment must be searched as both terms; VTC and Uniﬁed 
Capabilities Conferencing Systems (UCCS) in the following DISA APL: 
https://aplits.disa.mil/processAPList.action.  
The contractor's responsibilities include: 
• Evaluating all existing A/V equipment against current security and compliance 
standards. 
• Replacing or upgrading components as required to meet the speciﬁcations outlined 
in this SOW. 
• Installing, integrating, programming, and testing all new equipment. 
• Providing comprehensive documentation and user training. 

• Delivering ongoing preventative maintenance and on-call support services. 
3.0 Technical Requirements 
3.1. System Security and Compliance 
Governing Standards: Cybersecurity requirements will be governed by the DISA Risk 
Management Executive (RME) Vendor Security Technical Implementation Guides (STIG) 
program. Interoperability requirements will be identiﬁed by the Uniﬁed Capabilities 
Requirements (UCR)-CORE document.  
(Please note: DISA APL has been sunset, so it is no longer the standard. Moving forward, 
cybersecurity requirements will transition to the DISA RME Vendor Security Technical 
Implementation Guides (STIG) program, while interoperability requirements will be 
identiﬁed by the Uniﬁed Capabilities Requirements (UCR)-CORE document and enforced 
through contractual provisions.) 
• Prohibited Technology: The system is non-radio. No components shall be enabled 
for Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, or any other wireless communication method. 
• Secure/Non-Secure Status: The existing secure/non-secure marquee indicators, 
both inside and outside the room, shall remain operational. 
• UPS Replacement: The rack UPS needs to be replaced to meet AF compliance. 
Smart Online UPS required to include Double Conversion feature, Utilizing current 
wall sockets and voltages in room 203.   
3.2. Hardware and Equipment Upgrades 
• Cameras: There are currently two cameras. A CISCO Precision 60 PTZ for SIPR & a 
recent QSC NC-20-60 PTZ for NIPR via Fiber.  
• The CISCO camera may need to be replaced with High deﬁnition PTZ camera 
that has two ports. One for SIPR GVS and one for adding SIPR TEAMS laptop 
app. Recommend QSC NC-20-60 so, matches current NIPR camera in looks, 
speciﬁcations and functionality.  
• Will consider alternate compatible business class PTZ camera’s that 
speciﬁcations are equivalent or better. 
• CODEC: The VTC CODEC for the classiﬁed and unclassiﬁed system shall be 
replaced with a modern, compliant equivalent that supports IP-based connections 
and unclassiﬁed Voice over IP (VoIP) audioconferencing. 

• All replacements must be a DISA approved product and compatible with the 
existing system. VTC unit can connect via Internet Protocol (IP) 
• In addition, it has the ability for Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) 
connections for unclassiﬁed audioconferencing. The audioconferencing is 
programmed to be disabled for classiﬁed conferences. 
• Operator Display Upgrade:  
Note: Operator desk currently has one GFE furnished 32in ViewSonic computer 
display as that is the largest ﬁt for console.  It shall be replaced with two monitors of 
desktop standard viewing size or larger. 
• Upgrade operator area display hardware & program conﬁguration so, any 
display output can be shown on any room display independently and 
simultaneously.  
• Ability to see Main desktop display presentations or communication apps 
and extended display separately simultaneously on front screens, that will 
mirror operator desk monitors.  
• The ability for operators to work on local desktop displays will not be seen by 
attendees so the operator can research questions or check emails.  
 
• Credenza: If current AV cabinet cannot properly be reconﬁgured to house all 
required equipment and ensure that the temperature does not exceed the device's 
maximum MFR operating temperature limits then, the contractor’s proposal will 
provide estimate for a new locking credenza on casters with built in racks.  After 
Award of contract, the credenza design, material and color must be pre-approved by 
the Government ES POC prior to purchase. Shall have proper AC voltages and surge 
protection available where UPS is not utilized. Use existing AV racks for audio-visual 
equipment that can be locked in a rack system. 
 
3.3. Control System and User Interface 
• Touch Panels: 
• If touch panel replacement is required for compatibility, new screens no 
smaller than 10 inches shall be installed. The existing design, coloring, and 
icon style of the touch panel interface shall not be changed without 
Government consent (21.5 in at Operator desk & 10.1in at conference table.)  

• The ES logo on the touch panels must be updated, and the time display must 
be set to Eastern Standard Time. This feature should be user-friendly for 
future modiﬁcations. 
• SIPR Teams Integration: The newly introduced SIPR Teams audio/video signal data 
will come from DoD SIPR laptop connected at operator console and shall be wired 
and programmed into AV rack/touch panel system.  
• May require adding a (3rd Freeport SCC unit to current periods processing 
system.) 
• Camera Control: The control system must allow the SVTC camera to be turned 
on/off and have its presets utilized without interrupting active service. 
• White Noise System: One white noise system with a minimum of two white noise 
speakers mounted on the outside of each door, wired safely in/thru wall with the 
ability to turn on and off from inside the room. The volume level to the white noise is 
adjustable from inside the room, if able to automate into AV system. No Bluetooth, 
wireless, or Wi-Fi system/speaker exist or are allowed. 
3.4. Cabling and Installation 
• The contractor shall provide all cables, connectors, power strips, power supplies, 
and miscellaneous materials required for a complete installation. All wiring at the 
A/V desk must be professionally managed and bundled. 
4.0 Contractor Deliverables 
• System Documentation: The contractor shall produce and develop supporting 
documentation, diagrams, and artifacts for all system modiﬁcations and 
replacements. 
• Source Code: Uncompiled CRESTRON source code shall be delivered in a DISC to 
the Government. (It should be uncompiled so that it can be edited in the future if 
you ever need to get the system updated with new components or add a capability 
like secure VTC etc.) 
• Diagrams: Signal ﬂow diagrams (for Audio, Video, and Control) and wiring 
schematics shall be provided in both PDF and large-format prints. All equipment 
and wiring must be clearly labeled. (Signal ﬂow diagrams should include separate 
sheets for 1) AUDIO 2) VIDEO 3) CONTROL Coding (raw code). Wiring Schematics- 
Label equipment and wiring. 

• Equipment Disposal: The contractor is responsible for the removal and disposal of 
all broken, unserviceable, or replaced components.  
• Purchase and Install: All new equipment included as needed after receiving 
approval from the Government representative. The government will need to be 
informed if any equipment is backordered or will not arrive on time for installation. 
• Training: The contractor shall provide training and orientation for at least two 
Government individuals on the use of all new equipment and system functionalities. 
5.0 Service and Support Requirements 
• Service Calls: A one-hour remote response time is required for all service calls, 
Monday-Friday, 0800-1800 ET. 
• On-site Support: If requested, the contractor must provide on-site support for 
troubleshooting and maintenance within one business day. It is expected that the 
assigned technician will be local to meet this requirement. Additionally, the 
technician will ensure installation, integration, programming and testing of the new 
equipment is up to customer standards. 
• Preventative Maintenance: The contractor shall perform preventative maintenance 
twice annually on a mutually agreed upon schedule. 
• Service Hours: The estimated yearly service commitment is 16 hours. 
6.0 Period of Performance and Warranty 
• Period of Performance: The contract will consist of one (1) base year plus four (4) 
one-year option periods, commencing promptly after the contract is awarded, with 
no break in service between periods. 
• System Warranty: If replacement equipment or hardware is required, the contractor 
shall provide a one-year warranty on all equipment and systems installed, effective 
from the date of installation. This warranty applies to the base year and all exercised 
option years. 
  

Appendix A: Current Equipment Inventory 
The following is a non-exhaustive list of equipment currently installed in the VTC/AV system. 
The contractor is responsible for maintaining or replacing this equipment to meet the 
requirements of this SOW. Equipment is not required to be brand name, but must be equal 
in quality, function, compatibility with the overarching system, and meet the security 
standards set out in Section 2.0. 
Approximate room 203 speciﬁcations:19’10” by 23’8” .  
 
Item Item 
ACE tower computer 7ft CAT6 SNAGLESS PATCH CBL BLUE 
Viewsonic LCD 32inch 7ft CAT6 SNAGLESS PATCH CBL RED

UNCLASSIFIED 
 
 
 
 
UNCLASSIFIED 
 
STATEMENT OF OBJECTIVES (SOO) 
COMMENT MATRIX FOR THE NGA Police Modernization 
REQUEST FOR INFORMATION 
 
COMMENT 
# 
[SELECT 
SOO [OR]  
SOW [OR]   
PWS] 
PAGE # 
[SELECT 
SOO [OR]  
SOW [OR]   
PWS]  
SECTION 
COMMENT/QUESTION/SUGGESTION 
1    
2    
3    
    
    
 
 
Requirements 
The NGA Police based in Saint Louis, Missouri and Springfield, Virginia have a requirement for 
a Police Administration System (PAS). Which would allow the NGA Police to manage, store, 
and retrieve information related to incidents, arrests, evidence, personnel files, and other critical 
data, to improve efficiency.  
Comparable Commercial-off-the-Shelf (COTS) items are acceptable for consideration.  
 
• Police Administration System shall follow 34 U.S. Code 41303 – Uniform Federal Crime 
Reporting Act of 1988 (UCR Reporting) 
• Police Administration System shall follow Federal Code of Regulations Title 28 Chapter 
1 Part 20 to ensure criminal history record information wherever it appears is collected, 
stored, and disseminated in a manner to ensure the accuracy, completeness, currency 
integrity, and security of such information and to protect the individual privacy.  
• Currently FEDRAMP High 
• Impact Level IV (to process law enforcement sensitive information) 
• Police Administration System certified to the DoD 5015.02 standard. 
• Software maintenance agreement 
• Software warranty  
• Vendor shall have established UEI Number, CAGE Code, registered in SAM. 

UNCLASSIFIED 
 
 
 
 
  PAGE 2 OF 42 
 
 
 
UNCLASSIFIED 
• Vendor will serve as the Prime contractor.  
• Demonstrated experience working within TS/SCI environment(s) 
• Shall be in compliance with NARA. 
• PAS shall abide by all Federal regulations required of a PAS system for police 
departments. 
• Form repository for all NGA Police forms. To enable all forms to be accessible and 
identifiable 
• Server storage space requirements/capabilities for on premises PAS 
• Single entry (i.e., no duplicate data entry)  
• Maximum use of Federal charge code tables  
• Ability to enter and query narrative(s)/text fields.  
• Spell check and formatting capability on narrative(s)/ text fields.  
• Single database (i.e., virtual or physical) Validation on data entry (i.e., logical edits, edit 
checks for all fields)  
• Common Access Card login 
• Name and Password login (option)  
• Track and add/remove people. To remove or add permissions.  
• Ability to connect to Missouri Uniform Law Enforcement System (MULES), Virginia 
Criminal Information Database, and National Crime Information Center integrated into 
PAS. 
• Citrix integrated into PAS 
• Receipts for vehicle maintenance recorded per vehicle in PAS. 
• Access to ALERTS system for reports, tickets – Specifically for SIPE Police  
 
Vehicles 
 
• Automatic Maintenance scheduler 

UNCLASSIFIED 
 
 
 
 
  PAGE 3 OF 42 
 
 
 
UNCLASSIFIED 
• Lifecyle Management System – Lets department know when vehicle has reached its 
lifecycle and needs replaced. 
• Tracks vehicle through its lifecycle 
• Tracks all vehicle related costs and totals how much has been spent. 
 
Officer Clothing Records 
 
• Clothing and Equipment record for each officer 
• Ability to use CAC to digitally sign clothing record. 
• All officers shall have their own clothing record recorded in their personnel file within 
the PAS. 
Investigations 
 
• Ability to connect to Digital Data Exchange (DDEX) is integrated into PAS. 
• Ability to upload case information from a suspicious activity report/DDEX into report in 
PAS. 
• Search link for DDEX in PAS 
• Ability to connect to the National Data Exchange (N-DEX) in PAS 
Police Operations 
 
• Officer scheduling program (identify shifts, supervisor, officer)  
• General easily identifiable tile in PAS for reports to be written. 
• Report writing program/template with required fillable fields. 
• User interface identifies what user has been granted access to 
• An agency’s PAS should provide the capabilities for users to generate inquiries to 
internal data sources from within each module where such inquiries make sense. 
• System will offer the ability for two levels of review for each police report. 

UNCLASSIFIED 
 
 
 
 
  PAGE 4 OF 42 
 
 
 
UNCLASSIFIED 
• There will be a module for a central records section where various reporting documents 
of the agency can be managed. 
• At the administrative level, for administrators to keep informed and allocate manpower 
and resources. 
• Easy ability to obtain the disposition of reports. A report will be created and sent to the 
FBI. The report must accurately reflect the results of charges filed alleging violations of 
the law. 
• Provide a fingerprint card with case number.  This will allow for ease of reference after 
booking and record keeping. 
• The disposition page of an arrest should show the FBI number on the Final Disposition 
Report. If the FBI number is unknown, then the subject’s complete name should be 
reported as it was sent on the FBI fingerprint image.   
• The local arrest number as shown on the fingerprint image for which the disposition is 
being submitted should also be stated on the Final Disposition.  
• The Final Disposition should show any changes from the original charge as it appears on 
the arrest fingerprint image to the charge for which conviction was obtained. 
• Ability to access the Criminal History Record Information (CHRI) (Internal PeopleSoft 
Process) is not needed to connect to outside sources. 
• Ability to access NCIC.  
• Originating Agency Identified (ORI) file – will hold contact information (such as an 
agency’s address and telephone number.  
• Uniform Crime Reporting 
• Reporting module. Easily be able to obtain UCR report for each month or years of 
information. 
▪ The following is necessary for any agency which expects to generate 
Uniform Crime Reports from its records system: 
▪ A permanent record of each crime is made at once upon receipt of the 
complaint. All reports of thefts or attempted thefts are included regardless 
of the value of property involved. 

UNCLASSIFIED 
 
 
 
 
  PAGE 5 OF 42 
 
 
 
UNCLASSIFIED 
▪ Crime trends data module. Should show the crime trend from one period 
to the next are presented in the UCR Programs annual report.  The crime 
trend module should show the percentage change in crime based on data 
reported in a prior equivalent period. Trends can be computed for any time 
limit, such as month, quarters, or years.  
▪ The UCR reporting should compute the volume trend and the rate trends. 
• Easily identifiable tiles for each respective police division (Operations, Tactical Response 
Team, Investigations, Canine).  Each division should be able to store personnel records, 
operational plans, Standard Operating Procedures etc.…  
• Police Administration System shall be in compliance with Section 889(a)(1)(A) which 
prohibits the Federal Government from procuring or obtaining, or extending or renewing 
a contract to procure or obtain “any equipment, system, or service that uses covered 
telecommunications equipment or services as a substantial or essential component of any 
system, or as critical technology as part of any system,” on or after August 13, 2019, 
unless an exception applies or a waiver is granted. 
PAS System and Vendor Requirements 
 
• Vendors must be willing to sign a statement verifying no covered telecommunications 
equipment or service that uses covered telecommunications equipment or services as a 
substantial or essential component of any system, or as critical technology as part of any 
system that are in violation of Section 889(a)(1)(A) were used.  
• PAS should have basic master indices that correlate and aggregate information in the 
following areas: people, locations, property, conveyances (e.g., vehicles), and 
organizations.  
• This is to eliminate redundant data entry by allowing the reuse of previously stored 
information and the automatic update of the master indices upon the entry of report 
information. 
• Master indices information shall be captured in a variety of ways, including during the 
input of information from incident, traffic accident, and vehicle reports and citation, 
booking, arrest, juvenile, fingerprint, and mug shot subsystems. Prior to accepting an 
entry, PAS should automatically give the user the option of deciding whether there is a 
match based on existing data. 

UNCLASSIFIED 
 
 
 
 
  PAGE 6 OF 42 
 
 
 
UNCLASSIFIED 
• The system should support the validation and linking of addresses, commonplace names, 
and street intersections. Linkages among any information contained in the master indices 
(e.g., people to places or person to person) must be included in PAS. 
• Query and retrieval by name, vehicle, location, organization, and/or property to produce a 
comprehensive response displaying all related records in the system. 
• The PAS Master Name Index (MNI) function links an individual master name record to 
every event (e.g., incident-report, arrest report, field interview, accident report, and 
license and permits) in which the individual was involved or associated. Every person 
found within these events is given a master name record. Should that person become 
involved in another event, the single master-name record is linked to all the other events 
so that by querying that one name, the system can produce a synopsis of all the 
involvements associated with that one person. It also eases the linking of other names to 
an individual master name record (i.e., alias information and relationship data). In 
querying an individual MNI record, the user would also be able to view all related 
records, as well as those associated with that individual. When a record or report is added 
to PAS and a person is linked (i.e., indexed) to that event, the system should perform a 
particularly important matching function using a rule-based process. The purpose of this 
matching function is to either automatically link an existing MNI record or to present the 
user with a list of potential matches to the name so that the user can make the matching 
decision. PAS should provide a matching algorithm that will offer the ability to search 
the name file by a variety of criteria, such as sound-alike searching; phonetic 
replacement; diminutive first names (e.g., James/ Jim/Jimmy) and other static 
demographic information, such as age, sex, and race. Once a list of potential matches is 
provided, the user can decide whether the information should be linked to an existing 
master name record or whether a new master name entry should be added.  In addition to 
names, the MNI should, at a minimum, capture and support information on: Physical 
characteristics (e.g., current and past descriptors) Race and ethnicity Location history 
(e.g., current and past residences) Employer information.  Telephone numbers Known 
associates Alias names/monikers Available mug shot(s) and photographs Scars, marks, 
and tattoos Modus operandi (i.e., unique method of operation for a specific type of crime) 
Identification (e.g., social security, driver’s license, and local and county identification) 
NCIC fingerprint classification.  
• More information can be added, but older information should be maintained and 
viewable. 

UNCLASSIFIED 
 
 
 
 
  PAGE 7 OF 42 
 
 
 
UNCLASSIFIED 
• The PAS MNI should also provide maintenance functions that will allow a record or 
report to be unlinked from one MNI and linked to another. Since it is not always possible 
to ensure that the correct MNI record is linked to an event record, it must be possible to 
correct it. Functions also should be provided that will allow two or more MNI records to 
be merged into one record. 
 
Master Vehicle Index 
When a vehicle is linked to an incident in PAS, it should be added to the vehicle record in the 
Master Vehicle Index (MVI), which provides an agency with a detailed, searchable store of 
information about vehicles. PAS should offer the capability to search on:  
• Vehicle Identification Number (VIN)  
• License plate number  
• License plate state  
• License plate year 
•  Registered owner 
 Description (e.g., make, model, year, color, style, and attributes 
 
• Tied to M.U.L.E.S, V.C.I.N., and NCIC 
• Field Interview Report check box to vehicle report. 
 
Master Property Index (MPI) 
Each record is catalogued by using unique property characteristics: 
 
• Make  
• Model  
• Brand  
• Description  
• distinguishing characteristics  
• serial number 

UNCLASSIFIED 
 
 
 
 
  PAGE 8 OF 42 
 
 
 
UNCLASSIFIED 
•  Industry property coding standards should be used during the entry of property records 
into PAS.  
• Any property records entered throughout PAS should automatically cross-reference MPI 
to find potential matches based on the unique property characteristics. 
• Location identifier of evidence i.e.…locker or evidence room 
• System should show who entered the information into the MPI. 
• Track the chain of custody. 
• Auto generate time stamp of logged evidence 
• Internal audit report review 
• Other supervisors can review reports if another supervisor is out of the office – ability to 
assign a proxy. 
• Records retention determined by (day, month, and year)  
• Storage for completed FOIA requests. 
• All calls for service (CFS) are recorded in a structured records environment, offering the 
ability to run reports on this data, while also maintaining a historical record on all calls.  
• The data are either segmented or identifiable by the agency. 
Incident Reporting 
 
• CFS record in PAS should be linked to the incident and should be easily accessible from 
the incident report. 
• Certain types of incident reports must be available to the public. Witness information, as 
well as the names of juveniles who are subjects or victims, may need to be redacted for 
public consumption. PAS must be able to recognize the age of the majority in the 
jurisdiction by the date-of-birth information entered into the system that is available to 
the public. The system should support the redaction prior to printing a public copy or 
making the report available to the public. 
• The data captured in this module must support the preparation and submission of all 
required federal crime reporting and provide the capability to print a copy of both the 
completed department’s incident report and the redacted incident report. 

UNCLASSIFIED 
 
 
 
 
  PAGE 9 OF 42 
 
 
 
UNCLASSIFIED 
• Reports based on period of time. 
• Area 
• Incident type  
• Location code (e.g., geocode)  
• Initial call type Offense type  
• Summary of incidents by officer 
• Incident reports have free-text fields, to allow the collection of an unlimited amount of 
narrative information. 
• System should offer the capability to search the narratives for a specific word or phrase. 
• Check box to send report to Detectives.  PAS will automatically send reports to 
Detectives. 
• Officers can vie

search_text:
SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE 

Tracking Number 612674211


The Defense Information Systems Agency (DISA) is seeking sources for Enterprise Integration and Innovation/Emerging Technology (EII/EM8) Office for Joint Interoperability of Tactical Command and Control Systems (JINTACCs) Software Development and Sustainment. 

CONTRACTING OFFICE ADDRESS:  DITCO-NCR, 6910 Cooper Ave, Fort Meade, MD 20755

INTRODUCTION:

This is a SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE to determine the availability and technical capability of small businesses (including the following subsets: Small Disadvantaged Businesses, Certified 8(a), Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Businesses, HUBZone Small Businesses and Woman-Owned Small Businesses) to provide the required products and/or services. 

The DISA / Emerging Technologies organization is seeking information from potential sources for software development and sustainment of two systems summarized below. 

Tactical Data Link Configuration Management Tool (TCMT)

TCMT is a Configuration Management (CM) application that was developed under a previous contract and to date can generate two documents 1) MIL-STD-6017 Variable Message Format (VMF) and 2) Joint Multi-Tactical Data Link (TDL) Operating Procedures (JMTOP) CJCSM 6120.01.  It was developed to perform CM of TDL MIL-STDs using a systematic approach that ensures consistency of processing and tracks the impacts to previously released products. The TCMT is a replacement for the legacy software TDL Document System (TDS). TCMT is composed of both Commercial-Off-The-Shelf (COTS) products and custom code. 

 

United States Message Text Format (USMTF) – Web Application (WA)

The USMTF-WA is an application in sustainment that supports the CM of MIL-STD-6040.  Specifically, the USMTF-WA is used to update the Message Text Format (MTF) message catalog.  The catalog contains over 300 message formats.  The messages are the official source for DoD entities to convey information in a non-real time format.  USMTF messages are made of Sets and Fields. Sets and Fields may be used in any of the 300+ MTF messages. 

The anticipated Period of Performance is Sep 2026 – Sep 2030.

The anticipated Place of Performance is both Fort George G. Meade (FGGM) and the contractor’s location (within 50 miles of FGGM).

DISCLAIMER: 

 

THIS SOURCES SOUGHT NOTICE IS FOR INFORMATIONAL PURPOSES ONLY. THIS IS NOT A REQUEST FOR PROPOSAL.  IT DOES NOT CONSTITUTE A SOLICITATION AND SHALL NOT BE CONSTRUED AS A COMMITMENT BY THE GOVERNMENT.  RESPONSES IN ANY FORM ARE NOT OFFERS AND THE GOVERNMENT IS UNDER NO OBLIGATION TO AWARD A CONTRACT AS A RESULT OF THIS NOTICE.  NO FUNDS ARE AVAILABLE TO PAY FOR PREPARATION OF RESPONSES TO THIS NOTICE.  ANY INFORMATION SUBMITTED BY RESPONDENTS IS STRICTLY VOLUNTARY.

CONTRACT/PROGRAM BACKGROUND: 

Contract Number: HC102818D0035  HC104724F6681 

Contract Vehicle: ENCORE III Small Business Suite

Incumbent and their size: AccelGov. / Small Business. 

Method of previous acquisition: Small Business set-aside. 

Period of performance: Sep 2024 – Sep 2025 

REQUIRED CAPABILITIES: 

JINTACCS SDS involves continuing development for the current TCMT and USMTF-WA.  Describe and provide an example of your company’s experience with developing custom built user interfaces and databases that integrate COTS products, e.g. JAMA, mySQL, Kubernetes, Angular, NodeJS and NGINX. 

JINTACCS SDS work involves using data from a legacy system.  The data needs to be provided into the new system without any loss or errors.  Describe and provide an example of your company’s experience with Extract, Transform, and Load (ETL) with databases and other data sources. 

JINTACCS SDS involves maintaining and sustaining current tools.  Describe and provide an example of your company’s experience with maintenance and sustainment of systems, especially those that involve Cloud based solutions (JWCC, Amazon Web Services (AWS) etc.), Red Hat Enterprise Linus (RHEL), Windows Server, mySQL, and MS SQL, ensuring the upkeep of development, pre-production, and production Impact Level 4 (IL4) environments.

The JINTACCS SDS tools have been developed using Agile sprint releases, and the Government team expects to continue this approach.  Describe and provide an example of your company’s experience in Agile development using scrum working with multiple teams.

The JINTACCS SDS tools make use of extensible markup language (XML).  USMTF is currently transitioning to using the National Information Exchange Model (NIEM).  Describe and provide an example of your company’s experience with XML and  NIEM.

SPECIAL REQUIREMENTS: 

N/A.

SOURCES SOUGHT:  

The North American Industry Classification System Code (NAICS) for this requirement is 541512, with the corresponding size standard of $34 million. 

The purpose of this Sources Sought notice is to identify vendors with the capability to perform the services listed under the 'Required Capabilities' section below. The Government is particularly interested in identifying vendors who currently hold government vehicles and have the immediate capacity to perform these services.

To assist DISA in determining the level of participation by small business in any subsequent procurement that may result from this Sources Sought Notice, provide information regarding any plans to use joint ventures (JVs) or partnering.  Please outline the company's areas of expertise and those of any proposed JV/partner who combined can meet the specific requirements contained in this notice.

In order to make a determination for a small business set-aside, two or more qualified and capable small businesses must submit responses that demonstrate their qualifications.  Responses must demonstrate the company’s ability to perform in accordance with FAR clause 52.219-14, Limitations on Subcontracting.

SUBMISSION DETAILS:

Responses should include:

Business name and address; 

Name of company representative and their business title; 

Small Business Socio-economic status (if small); 

CAGE Code;

Prime contract vehicles; to include ENCORE III, SETI, NIH CIO-SP4, NASA SEWP V, General Service Administration (GSA): OASIS, ALLIANT II, VETS II, STARS III, MAS (including applicable SIN(s), groups or pools), or any other Government Agency contract vehicle that allows for decentralized ordering.  (This information is for market research only and businesses with a valid cage that lack prime contract vehicles are still encouraged to respond to this notice.) 

Businesses who wish to respond must send a response via email NLT 4:00 PM Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) on  to Yajie “Amy” Kim at yajie.kim.civ@mail.mil.  Interested businesses should submit a brief capabilities statement package addressing the specific questions above (no more than five pages) demonstrating the ability to perform the services listed under Required Capabilities.      

Proprietary information and trade secrets, if any, must be clearly marked on all materials.  All information received that is marked Proprietary will be handled accordingly.  All submissions become Government property and will not be returned.  All government and contractor personnel reviewing submitted responses will have signed non-disclosure agreements and understand their responsibility for proper use and protection from unauthorized disclosure of proprietary information as pursuant to 41 USC 423.  The Government shall not be held liable for any damages incurred if proprietary information is not properly identified.

For information on DISA’s requirements, you can find current information on our home page: www.disa.mil – that includes current forecast and the current acquisition strategies. If there is no information regarding the requirement you may be inquiring about, then there is no additional publicly available information. Please continue to review www.disa.mil.
