ACQUISITION OF COMMERCIAL ITEMS:  

This Request for Quote (RFQ) solicitation is issued under the authority of FAR Part 12 Acquisition of Commercial Items and FAR Part 13 Simplified Acquisition Procedures.  FAR Part 15 will not be used. The procedures in Part 12 used in conjunction with Part 13 allow the Government to procure services resembling those in the commercial marketplace and streamline the procurement process.  From this RFQ, the Government intends to award a firm fixed price contract where the contractor provides a price that is not subject to any adjustment on the basis of the contractor’s cost experience in performing the contract. (Note: The terms “offeror” and “quoter” are both used in this Solicitation. For the purposes of this Solicitation, these terms are interchangeable; there is no difference in meaning between the two.)

SET-ASIDE (If any): See box 10 of STANDARD FORM 1449

SOLICITATION TIMELINE SUMMARY:

RFQ QUESTIONS:

In order to maintain integrity of this solicitation and subsequent award date, all offerors are advised that any question and answer requests must be submitted in a written form via e-mail to the Contracting Officer derrick.paquette@va.gov no later than Tuesday, February 17, 2026 at 6:00 AM, local time.  Contracting will issue a consolidated response via solicitation amendment to https://sam.gov Contract Opportunities within 2 business days to all parties

SOLICITATION CLOSES/QUOTES DUE:

See box 8 of STANDARD FORM 1449 or most recent Amendment. 

SUBMISSION OF QUOTE:

See 52.212-1 Instructions to Offerors—Commercial Products and Commercial Services and addendum for specific instructions to offerors. 

CONTRACTING OFFICER AUTHORITY:

The Contracting Officer is the only person authorized to approve changes or modify any of the requirements under this contract. The Contractor shall communicate with the Contracting Officer on all matters pertaining to contract administration.  Only the Contracting Officer is authorized to make commitment or issue changes that will affect price, quantity or quality of performance of this contract.  In the event the Contractor effects any such change at the direction of any person other than the Contracting Officer, the change shall be considered to have been made without authority and no adjustment will be made in the contract price to cover any increase in cost incurred thereof.

INVOICES:  

All payments by the Government to the contractor will be made in accordance with: FAR 52.232-33 PAYMENT BY ELECTRONIC FUNDS TRANSFER – SYSTEM FOR AWARD MANAGEMENT (OCT 2018)

Invoices shall be submitted quarterly in arrears in accordance with: 852.232-72 ELECTRONIC SUBMISSION OF PAYMENT REQUESTS (NOV 2018)

Payments shall be made in arrears upon receipt of a properly prepared invoice 

Facsimile, e-mail, and scanned documents are not acceptable forms of submission for payment requests.

The VA has mandated electronic invoice submission to the Veterans Affairs Financial Services Center (VAFSC). VAFSC has partnered with Tungsten Network e-Invoicing network, for submissions of all electronic invoices to VA.  Tungsten Network electronic invoicing is free to all VA vendors.  In order to submit electronic invoices, all VA vendors must register with Tungsten Network by submitting an email to VA.Registration@Tungsten-Network.com or calling  1-877-489-6135 for Enrollment.  

Contractor shall submit an electronic invoice by the tenth (10th) of the following month services were performed to the Veterans Affairs Financial Services Center (VAFSC) e-Invoice through the website at https://portal.Tungsten-Network.com/Login.aspx.  For questions regarding the submission of VA electronic invoices, Tungsten Network customer service may be contacted at 1-877-489-6135.  

All invoices shall reference the vendor name and address, customer name, contract number, appropriate obligation/funding order number, description of services provided, quantity, unit price, and total invoice amount (any additional info).  Invoices shall also include any payment discount terms.  

VA’s Electronic Invoice Presentment and Payment System – The Veterans Affairs Financial Services Center (VAFSC) uses a third-party contractor, Tungsten Network e-Invoice, to transition vendors from paper to electronic invoice submission. Please go to this website: http://www.tungsten-network.com/customer-campaigns/veteransaffairs/ to begin submitting electronic invoices, free of charge.

As of July 1, 2020, the VA10091 form has been eliminated and the VAFSC is requiring our vendors to update and add their information in the Customer Engagement Portal (CEP) at  https://www.cep.fsc.va.gov/. This is for new vendors being added to FMS only. 

For assistance setting up e-Invoice, the below information is provided:

* Tungsten Network e-Invoice Setup Information: 1-877-489-6135 
* Tungsten Network e-Invoice email: VA.Registration@Tungsten-Network.com
* FSC e-Invoice Contact Information: 1-877-353-9791 
* FSC e-invoice email: vafsccshd@va.gov   

FACILITY REQUIREMENTS: In addition to any other requirements set forth in this Contract, the Contractor, including its employees, agents, and subcontractors (collectively “the Contractor”), shall comply with the following facility requirements. Failure to comply with these requirements shall be considered a material breach of this Contract and may be a violation of Federal law resulting in charges answerable in the United States District Court.

Contractor is responsible to follow all on site facility policies 

All VA property is considered Federal property and all Federal laws are applicable. 

It is the responsibility of the Contractor to park in the appropriate designated parking areas. Information on parking is available from the VA Police Section. The VA will not invalidate or make reimbursement for parking violations of the Contractor under any conditions.   

All VA facilities are drug free. Possession of drugs, to include Marijuana and all Marijuana bi-products are prohibited. Enclosed containers, including tool kits, shall be subject to search. Violations of VA regulations may result in citation answerable in the United States (Federal) District Court, not a local district, state, or municipal court.

All contractor personnel are required to adhere to all VA rules and regulations.

Smoking is prohibited on the entire campus, both inside and outside the VA Facility 

Possession of weapons is prohibited. Enclosed containers, including tool kits, shall be subject to search. Violations of VA regulations may result in citation answerable in the United States (Federal) District Court, not a local district, state, or municipal court.   

VHA DIRECTIVE 1192: This Veterans Health Administration (VHA) directive establishes policy and provides guidance for the prevention of seasonal influenza in VHA facilities through the vaccination or masking of health care personnel (HCP). AUTHORITY: Title 38 United States Code (U.S.C.) 7301(b), 7318(b). Contractor is responsible in ensuring that all aspects of this directive are implemented, including providing face masks and influenza vaccines for all employees working at a VA facility.

SECURITY & PRIVACY CONTROL:  

PIV Card: 

Applies to unsupervised, full-time, logical and/or physical access for more than 6 months OR more than 180 aggregate days in a one-year period.

Defined in VA Directive 0735 as: “Personal Identity Verification (PIV) Credential: An identification card that complies with FIPS 201 and related guidance that contains a photograph and stored identity information so that the claimed identity of the cardholder can be verified by another person or an automated process. PIV credentials are issued to persons requiring routine access to VA facilities or information systems.”

All contractor employees are subject to the same level of investigation as VA employees who have access to VA Sensitive Information.  The level of background investigation commensurate with the level of access needed to perform in accordance with the statement of work is: Special Agreement Check (SAC) & National Agency Check with Inquiries (NACI).  This requirement is applicable to all subcontractor personnel requiring the same access.

National MOU/ISA is in place and applies to this contract. Sensitive information exposure as part of this contract involves applicable security controls within the facility as part of the VA Information Security.  If there are any questions related to privacy, please have a member of your staff contact the VA Privacy Service at (202) 461-6309.  If there are any questions related to the information security, please have a member of your staff contact the Office of Cyber Security at (304) 262-7733.

Routine Access and Personal Identity Verification of Contractor Personnel: 

The Contractor shall comply with agency personal identity verification procedures identified in the contract that implement Homeland Security Presidential Directive-12 (HSPD-12), Office of Management and Budget (OMB) guidance M-05-24 and Federal Information Processing Standards Publication (FIPS PUB) Number 201.

The Contractor shall account for all forms of Government-provided identification issued to the Contractor employees in connection with performance under this contract. The Contractor shall return such identification to the issuing agency at the earliest of any of the following, unless otherwise determined by the Government:

           (1) When no longer needed for contract performance.

           (2) Upon completion of the Contractor employee’s employment.

           (3) Upon contract completion or termination.

 The Contracting Officer may delay final payment under a contract if the Contractor fails to comply with these requirements.

It shall be the responsibility of the prime Contractor to return such identification to the VA.

RECORDS MANAGEMENT OBLIGATIONS

Contractor shall comply with all applicable records management laws and regulations, as well as National Archives and Records Administration (NARA) records policies, including but not limited to the Federal Records Act (44 U.S.C. chs. 21, 29, 31, 33), NARA regulations at 36 CFR Chapter XII Subchapter B, and those policies associated with the safeguarding of records covered by the Privacy Act of 1974 (5 U.S.C. 552a). These policies include the preservation of all records, regardless of form or characteristics, mode of transmission, or state of completion. 

In accordance with 36 CFR 1222.32, all data created for Government use and delivered to, or falling under the legal control of, the Government are Federal records subject to the provisions of 44 U.S.C. chapters 21, 29, 31, and 33, the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) (5 U.S.C. 552), as amended, and the Privacy Act of 1974 (5 U.S.C. 552a), as amended and must be managed and scheduled for disposition only as permitted by statute or regulation. 

In accordance with 36 CFR 1222.32, Contractor shall maintain all records created for Government use or created in the course of performing the contract and/or delivered to, or under the legal control of the Government and must be managed in accordance with Federal law. Electronic records and associated metadata must be accompanied by sufficient technical documentation to permit understanding and use of the records and data. 

[FACILITY] and its contractors are responsible for preventing the alienation or unauthorized destruction of records, including all forms of mutilation. Records may not be removed from the legal custody of [FACILITY] or destroyed except for in accordance with the provisions of the agency records schedules and with the written concurrence of the Head of the Contracting Activity. Willful and unlawful destruction, damage or alienation of Federal records is subject to the fines and penalties imposed by 18 U.S.C. 2701. 

In the event of  any unlawful or accidental removal, defacing, alteration, or destruction of records, Contractor must report to [FACILITY]. The agency must report promptly to NARA in accordance with 36 CFR 1230.

The Contractor shall immediately notify the appropriate Contracting Officer upon discovery of any inadvertent or unauthorized disclosures of information, data, documentary materials, records or equipment. Disclosure of non-public information is limited to authorized personnel with a need-to-know as described in the [contract vehicle]. The Contractor shall ensure that the appropriate personnel, administrative, technical, and physical safeguards are established to ensure the security and confidentiality of this information, data, documentary material, records and/or equipment is properly protected. The Contractor shall not remove material from Government facilities or systems, or facilities or systems operated or maintained on the Government’s behalf, without the express written permission of the Head of the Contracting Activity. When information, data, documentary material, records and/or equipment is no longer required, it shall be returned to [FACILITY] control or the Contractor must hold it until otherwise directed. Items returned to the Government shall be hand carried, mailed, emailed, or securely electronically transmitted to the Contracting Officer or address prescribed in the [contract vehicle]. Destruction of records is EXPRESSLY PROHIBITED unless in accordance with Paragraph (4).

Department of Veterans Affairs 

Enterprise Digital Government Enablement 

Request for Information 

Introduction

This is a Request for Information (RFI) is issued for information and planning purposes only and does not constitute a solicitation nor does it restrict the Government as to the ultimate acquisition approach. In accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation 15.101(c)(2), responses to RFIs are not offers and cannot be accepted by the Government to form a binding contract. 

The purpose of this RFI is to identify qualified vendors, who are able to meet the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) requirements for Office of Information and Technology (OIT), Enterprise Digital Government Enablement (EDGE). Accordingly, this RFI constitutes neither a Request for Quote (RFQ), Request for Proposal (RFP), nor a guarantee that one will be issued by the Government in the future; furthermore, it does not commit the Government to contract for any services described herein. Any contract/order that might be awarded based on information received or derived from this market research will be the outcome of the competitive process. The purpose of this RFI is to obtain market information on capable sources of supply, industry best practices, and input specific to the information provided. The Government is not responsible for any cost incurred by industry in furnishing this information. All costs associated with responding to this RFI will be solely at the interested vendor’s expense. Not responding to this RFI does not preclude participation in any future RFP, if any is issued. Any information submitted by respondents to this RFI is strictly voluntary. All submissions will become Government property and will not be returned. Interested vendors are responsible for adequately marking proprietary, restricted or competition sensitive information contained in their response. 

The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) for this requirement is 541512.

Requirement:

VA, OIT, Digital Service Delivery, Digital Transformation Center (DTC) strives to establish VA-wide Digital Modernization initiatives which can be supported and delivered via Product Delivery and Lifecycle Management methodologies to transform VA by tapping into emerging technologies, connecting developers with VA data, and making Human Centered Design the method of how all VA products are designed.

The DTC ecosystem is a persistent collaboration of platform management, program management teams, and system integrators (SI) across the entire capability delivery lifecycle with centralized enterprise platform management with quality approval gates assuring the SI’s delivery of high-quality low code/no code (LCNC) solutions. EDGE as a SI team is focused on delivering complex, large-scale solutions within the DTC Capability Delivery Lifecycle Management. DTC success is measured and validated through the incremental and iterative delivery of business and technology capabilities driving modernization efforts. 

As part of VA’s digital modernization, Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) is a strategic application delivery model. A SaaS Product is hosted on a cloud infrastructure outside the security boundary of VA and is provided to the Cloud Service Customer (CSC) over the internet. The CSC uses the SaaS offering via a thin-client interface, such as web-browser or a program interface. The CSC subscribes to the SaaS offering and is only responsible for limited application configuration settings. The Cloud Service Provider  offering the application is responsible for management of the application, safeguarding of data stores or processed by the application, and all elements of the underlying infrastructure.

To further accelerate the adoption of VA’s Cloud First policy, VA sponsors Hybrid and Managed Solutions in addition to the SaaS application delivery model. A Hybrid Solution is comprised of both SaaS and on-premise components which shall adhere to both SaaS and VA Enterprise Cloud established policies. A Managed Solution is defined as a single-tenant product not hosted within the VA Network. The mission of the VA is to provide benefits and services to Veterans of the United States. In meeting these goals, OIT strives to provide high quality, effective, and efficient Information Technology (IT) services to those responsible for providing care to the Veterans at the point-of-care as well as throughout all the points of the Veterans’ health care in an effective, timely, and compassionate manner. VA depends on Information Management/Information Technology systems to meet mission goals. 

In support of VA systems modernization and the agency’s mandate to move to the cloud, this task order seeks to continue developing Veterans Enterprise Systems Integrator Product-as-a-Service (PaaS)/SaaS Applications to modernize VA Health and Business specific case management solutions across the enterprise. The EDGE solution will utilize a platform agnostic approach to provide VA with modern solutions to support EDGE modular LCNC solutions that enables an innovative reusable service-focused software component architecture including PaaS functions/capabilities, SaaS integrations, Application Program Interfaces, and Digital modernization and emerging technology support. EDGE architecture will make configuring and customizing DTC PaaS and SaaS solutions easier to scale and faster to create, enabling innovation and accelerating time-to-market for new features. 

In addition, as an emerging technological part of VA’s digital modernization strategy, VA is increasingly embracing Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) as foundational capabilities integrated within SaaS, PaaS, and LCNC platforms to enhance productivity, decision-making, and service delivery across VA agencies. DTC enables the responsible adoption of AI technologies by embedding AI capabilities directly into the EDGE capability delivery ecosystem, ensuring alignment with VA policies, security requirements, and enterprise governance, including oversight by the VA Chief Information Officer.

RFI Response Instructions 

The capability package must be clear, concise, and complete. VA is under no obligation to provide feedback to the company, or to contact the company for clarification of any information submitted in response to this RFI.

There is a page limitation for this RFI of 10 pages. The Government will not review any information or attachments in excess of the page limit.

Provide the following information:

Company Information:

	

Company Name

CAGE/UEI Number under which the company is registered in SAM.gov

Company Address

Point of Contact name

Telephone number

Email Address

Socio-economic Data (If small business, please identify socio-economic category(ies)

For small business concerns, indicate whether at least 50 percent of the total amount paid by the Government will be paid to firms that are similarly situated.

Company Business Size and Status for NAICS (541512).

Existing contractual vehicles (GWAC, FSS, MAC, SEWP, etc.)

Be advised that set-aside decisions may be made based on the information provided in response to this RFI. Reponses should be as complete and as informative as possible.

Summary describing your company’s technical approach to meeting the requirements, to include:

Describe in detail your approach to directly supporting a minimum of 75+ concurrent LCNC projects/products and 75,000 end users (new development and enhancements) across multiple customer organizations, including the peak number of concurrent projects supported, specific LCNC platforms utilized, size of your reusable asset library, the scaled staffing model (number of full-time architects, developers, integration specialists, and DevSecOps engineers), proven integration patterns and architecture that sustained surges of several million transactions per day while consistently meeting or exceeding SLAs, mechanisms for dynamic allocation of scarce specialists across the portfolio, and measured outcomes such as delivery velocity improvement, asset reuse percentage, SLA compliance rates, and peak transaction throughput achieved.

Describe in detail your approach and operational expertise with modern delivery frameworks to include concurrent linear and iterative delivery frameworks to attain high delivery confidence for a large-scale enterprise solution that encompasses independent product team priorities; LCNC products and platforms; and VA source systems with varying degrees of system maturity, stability, and SLAs.

Describe in detail your approach to implementing an enterprise-wide automation framework and continuous improvement program that reduced manual interventions by at least 60% while supporting end users of varying technical complexity. State the peak number of concurrent projects directly managed and the measured overall reduction in manual touchpoints achieved through robotic process automation, intelligent workflow orchestration, automated testing/regression suites, or AI-assisted requirement triage tools. Describe the governance model and tools that enabled rapid incorporation of changing or emerging requirements within sprint cycles.

Describe your experience in simultaneously staffing and managing multiple concurrent Scrum teams (minimum of 200 teams at once) across different task areas or product lines throughout the full lifecycle. Include specific examples of how you determined optimal Scrum team sizes (e.g., 5–9 members), composed cross-functional skillsets (Dev, QA, DevSecOps, UX, etc.), and dynamically adjusted team structures and personnel across 200+ concurrent Scrum teams to meet evolving performance requirements and delivery schedules.

The Government is seeking industry feedback to determine vendor capabilities, socio-economic categories, industry best practices, and alternative contract vehicles and models well-suited for this requirement.  The Government values candid, experienced-based input focused on concepts, approaches and lessons learned.

Identify and describe any innovative pricing models you would recommend for this requirement.  What are the strengths and limitations of such pricing models?

Identify and describe any innovative contracting methodologies you would recommend for this requirement.  

Please provide any questions or comments on the Draft PWS. The Government does not anticipate answering questions prior to the RFI closing date.  

Responses to this RFI shall be submitted electronically by 3:00 PM Eastern Daylight Time, March 25, 2026 via email the Technology Acquisition Center (TAC) points of contact:

Contract Specialist: Mario Awad at Mario.Awad@va.gov 

Contracting Officer: Kathryn Pantages at Kathryn.Pantages@va.gov

Please note EDGE RFI in the subject of your response. Mark your response as “Proprietary Information” if the information is considered business sensitive.  

All proprietary/company confidential material shall be clearly marked on every page that contains such. The file size shall not exceed 5 MB.

RESPONDENTS ARE ADVISED THAT THIS REQUIREMENT MAY BE DELAYED, CANCELED, OR REVISED AT ANY TIME.

	See attached document: PWS – Draft.

search_text:
SOO for new Summit Contract

Background:

The Summit Data Platform (Summit) is the Department of Veterans Affairs’ (VA) enterprise cloud-based data and analytics solution. The platform offers self-service analytics, providing customers (data practitioners across VA) with data storage, tools, integrations, and governance empowering its customers. It also supports advanced use cases, such as data science, machine learning (ML), and artificial intelligence (AI), enabling VA teams to make evidence-based decisions that improve services, operational efficiencies, and outcomes for Veterans, families, and caregivers.

Summit is designed to modernize VA’s Corporate Data Warehouse (CDW) by migrating CDW data products and workloads into the cloud, improving data freshness and governance, in addition to enabling advanced analytics and AI capabilities. Summit enables program offices across the agency to host their own data analytics platforms, providing security, infrastructure, some shared services, etc. It currently provides the underlying technical architecture for VA's curated data platforms, Advanced Health Insights (AHI) and Customer Experience Insights (CXI).

Currently, Summit supports over 2,500 active users and has migrated 160 workgroups from CDW. However, there is increased demand across the enterprise and over 1,400 CDW workgroups remain to be migrated. In addition, Summit faces the following challenges:

Intake processes are slow and inconsistent (2 weeks to 6 months)

Limited post-handoff support and unclear documentation

Resource constraints and coordination gaps

Delayed CDW migration and feature integration

These issues limit adoption and utilization of the data platform, which in turn, limit VA’s ability to deliver timely insights and scale AI capabilities.

Statement of Need:

Summit Data Platform must evolve past its current state to deliver a secure, reliable, and customer-centric environment at scale, addressing operational inefficiencies and leveraging resources strategically. To achieve this, the following needs must be met:

Streamlined customer onboarding & intake

Publish and communicate clear requirements, expectations, and obligations to customers for intake, onboarding, and use case definition. 

Reduce intake time from the current 2-6 months to under 2 weeks for standard requests.

Implement structured intake workflows with automated tracking and status visibility.

Establish a unified model that routes requests intelligently across Summit, AHI, CXI, or to other data platforms, where appropriate.

Migration & data architecture modernization

Establish and execute a structured migration strategy for CDW workgroups and data products.

Provide tool and data model parity to ensure continuity for CDW users.

Develop reusable ingestion and integration pipelines for batch and streaming sources.

Standardize data modeling practices to ensure consistency across domains.

Governance, security, & compliance

Enable and enforce VA-wide governance policies for fine-grained, policy-based access control.

Expand metadata coverage and improve usability of the Enterprise Data Catalog to increase data discoverability.

Implement automated data quality checks and observability dashboards to monitor pipeline health.

Maintain compliance with federal security and privacy standards across all environments

Ensure cloud resources are sized, provisioned, designed, and managed efficiently to reduce cost. 

Data science & AI enablement

Provide scalable infrastructure and tooling for AI/ML workloads.

Develop reusable templates and patterns for predictive modeling, NLP, and generative AI use cases.

Support integration of AI-driven solutions for clinical decision support, risk prediction, and operational optimization.

Customer experience & support

Create centralized documentation, FAQs, and self-service resources for common tasks to increase adoption.

Establish robust post-handoff support processes to ensure customers can effectively use provisioned environments.

Develop CRM to track customer engagement history, ensuring consistent and context-aware responses across Summit teams.

Track and report key metrics, such as adoption rates and utilization.

Implement feedback loops and satisfaction surveys across key customer touchpoints to identify pain points and opportunities.

Change management & training

Deliver structured real-time and async training programs for VA data customers on Summit tools, governance, and best practices.

Provide targeted end-to-end enablement for high priority use cases.

Develop change management strategy to track, measure, and ensure long-term adoption.

Technical Environment:

Summit is built on a cloud-native architecture primarily leveraging Azure and with a smaller AWS footprint

Data ingestion – Synapse Pipelines, Azure Data Factory, Synapse Link (batch); Oracle Golden Gate, Azure Function Apps, Event Hubs, Mulesoft (streaming)

Big Data ELT & data wrangling – Azure Data Factory, Synapse Analytics, Databricks, Starburst, 

Data storage & format – Azure Data Lake Service, Delta Parquet

SQL & NoSQL query engines – Databricks, Starburst, Synapse Analytics, Cosmos DB, Cockroach DB, SQL Managed Instance

Data catalog – VA’s Enterprise Data Catalog (Collibra)

AI/ML – Azure ML, Azure AI (Cognitive) Services, Azure Open AI, Databricks

Visualization & BI – Pyramid Analytics, PowerBI (SSAS and SSRS legacy functionality to be migrated)

Applications – COTS products hosted on OpenShift

Data governance & security – Immuta

Customer Resource Management – Microsoft D365 Application

Approach:

The work will follow modern digital service delivery principles, following practices described in the “Digital Services Playbook” (https://playbook.cio.gov). Specifically, the contractor shall: 

Agile Delivery: Execute in two-week sprints with clear sprint goals and demos.

Quarterly Planning: Align priorities and roadmap with stakeholders every quarter.

User-centered Design: Focus on customer experience, transparency, and iterative improvement.

Open Collaboration: Maintain continuous communication with VA teams and partners, which may include individual contributors from VA or other vendors.

Compliance by Design: Integrate security, privacy, and governance into all workflows.

Location and Period of Performance:

Efforts under this task order can be performed at any location within the United States. Work shall be performed at the Contractor’s facilities, using Government‑furnished equipment and credentials, and VA‑approved tools/services.

The period of performance is 1 12-month Base, plus 2 12-month option periods.

Operating Constraints:

Data Governance & Privacy: All activities must comply with federal privacy regulations and VA data and AI governance requirements.

Accessibility: Ensure all user-facing artifacts (documentation, dashboards, etc.) meet Section 508 standards.

Introduction:

This is a Request for Information (RFI) only issued for conducting market research.  Accordingly, this RFI constitutes neither a Request for Quote (RFQ), Request for Proposal (RFP), nor a guarantee that one will be issued by the Government in the future; furthermore, it does not commit the Government to contract for any services described herein. The Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) is not, at this time, seeking proposals or quotes, and therefore, will not accept, review, or evaluate unsolicited proposals or quotes received in response hereto. This notice is not to be construed as a commitment on the part of the Government to award a contract, nor does the Government intend to pay for any information submitted because of this request. The Government does not reimburse respondents for any costs associated with submission of the information being requested or reimburse expenses incurred for responses to this RFI. The information provided may be used by VA in developing its acquisition strategy and Performance Work Statement (PWS). Any information submitted by respondents to this RFI is strictly voluntary; however, any information received shall become the property of the Government and will not be returned to the respondent. Interested parties are responsible for adequately marking proprietary, restricted, or competition sensitive information contained in their response. This is a request for information and does not obligate the Government in any way, nor does it commit the Government to any specific course of action. Product information, brochures, part numbers, and/or other description information may be included with the submission.  

Requirement:

The Department of Veterans Affairs (VA), Office of Information and Technology (OI&T), has a requirement to evolve its Summit Data Platform (Summit) by:

Streamlined customer onboarding & intake

Migration & data architecture modernization

Governance, security, & compliance

Data science & AI enablement

Customer experience & support

Change management & training

Please see attached Statement of Objectives (SOO) for additional information. 

Provide the following information with your RFI Response: 

Include the following identification information:

Name of Company

Address

Point of Contact

Phone Number

Fax Number

Email address

Company Business Size

NAICS code(s)

Any Schedules held on General Services Administration (GSA), Government-Wide Acquisition Contracts (GWAC) held on the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) Solutions for Enterprise Wide Procurement (SEWP) V, or any other schedule not mentioned above to assist in determining acquisition strategy if you are included in one or more. Please also include Contract Number, expiration date, etc.

For small business concerns, indicate whether at least 50 percent of the total amount paid by the Government will be paid to firms that are similarly situated.

Service Disabled Veteran Owned Small Businesses (SDVOSB) and Veteran Owned Small Businesses (VOSB) must indicate whether at least 50% of the cost incurred would be expended for prime employees or employees of other eligible SDVOSB/VOSB firms. This should also include the prime planned percentage and if under 50%, the names of the potential team members that may be used to fulfill the 50% SDVOSB/VOSB requirement.

If your company is a Small Business, also include the following:

The intent and ability to meet set-aside requirements for performance of this effort.

Information as to available personnel and financial resources to support a requirement of this magnitude.

Information as to proposed team members, the percentage of work each is to perform and which PWS requirements would be subcontracted.

Please provide your response to the following:

Technical Approach:

What’s your company’s technical approach for delivering a comprehensive data analytics platform solution. Include core architectural components, major subsystems, recommended technologies, and any commercial products or cloud services?

How would your company be able to provide  scalability, availability, and managing large volumes of structured, unstructured, and semi-structured- data? Provide any relevant performance benchmarks or architectural considerations.

Describe your cybersecurity approach, including identity management, Zero Trust alignment, FedRAMP compliance, RMF expectations, audit logging, monitoring, and incident response.

Describe your technical approach to modernizing and migrating existing CDW data products and workloads to the cloud. What methodologies and tools will you employ to ensure data freshness, governance, and continuity for CDW users?

Staffing Approach:

Describe your recommended staffing model, including team structure, labor categories, roles, and skills needed to design, deploy, operate, and sustain the solution.

Describe your approach to staffing continuity, knowledge transfer, operational readiness, surge support, and minimizing disruptions during onboarding or system transition.

Provide examples of relevant federal or large--scale enterprise analytics efforts your team has supported, highlighting lessons learned relevant to staffing and management.

Identify any specialized certifications or unique skill sets needed to support AI, automation, or advanced analytics components of your solution.

How do you plan to staff this project to ensure timely and effective execution? Please outline your proposed staffing model, including roles, responsibilities, and qualifications of key team members.

Artificial Intelligence, Automation, and Optimization:

Describe specific opportunities where AI, machine learning, or intelligent automation can accelerate Government workflows, reduce manual labor, improve data accuracy, or deliver early warning indicators for leadership.

Describe how your solution leverages automation for tasks such as data ingestion, cleansing, validation, enrichment, anomaly detection, and lifecycle management.

Provide examples of how you have successfully implemented AI and automation in similar projects. How do you plan to leverage AI and automation to streamline Summit’s customer onboarding and intake processes, reducing intake time from 2-6 months to under 2 weeks?

Implementation, Deployment, and Transition:

How would a notional implementation timeline, including major phases, milestones, and dependencies for deploying a functional analytics environment look like?.

Describe your recommended transition approach for onboarding users, migrating legacy processes, and supporting gradual adoption while maintaining continuity of operations.

Describe your strategy for training, user support, change management, and ensuring adoption of advanced analytics tools across diverse user roles.

Rough Order of Magnitude and Licensing:

PERFORMANCE WORK STATEMENT (PWS)

DEPARTMENT OF VETERANS AFFAIRS

Veteran Health Administration (VHA)

Office of Information and Technology (OIT) 

VA Enterprise Contact Center 

Consolidation (CCC) Support

Date: 3/2/2025

	VA-26-TBD

PWS Version Number: 2.0

Contents

1.0	BACKGROUND	3

2.0	APPLICABLE DOCUMENTS	3

ADDENDUM A – ADDITIONAL VA REQUIREMENTS, CONSOLIDATED	30

ADDENDUM B – VA INFORMATION AND INFORMATION SYSTEM SECURITY/ PRIVACY LANGUAGE	37

BACKGROUND

The Department of Veterans Affairs (VA), Veteran Health Administration (VHA), Office of Information & Technology (OIT) strives to provide high quality, effective, and efficient Information Technology (IT) services to those responsible for providing care to the Veterans at the point-of-care as well as throughout all the points of the Veterans’ health care in an effective, timely and compassionate manner.  VA depends on Information Management/Information Technology (IM/IT) systems to meet mission goals.

VA manages over 60 million calls annually through 20 decentralized and siloed Contact Centers, each operating independently. This leads to duplicated tools, limited data integration, inconsistent service delivery, and a Veteran experience marked by confusion, repeated authentication, and unnecessary handoffs. VA faces a critical imperative to modernize its legacy Contact Center infrastructure through a comprehensive, enterprise-wide consolidation and transformation effort. VA’s Contact Centers – spanning telephony, mobile applications, text messaging, email, and other communication modalities – have operated in silos, relying on outdated technologies that no longer meet the expectations of today’s Veterans, Caregivers, Families, and Survivors.

To align with modern industry standards and deliver a seamless, high-quality experience, VA is reimagining its Contact Center operations as a unified, concierge-style service. This transformation will enable VA to orchestrate and differentiate between administrative, financial, clinical and non-clinical, and virtual emergency and urgent care services, ensuring Veterans receive the right support, at the right time, through the right channel. By consolidating and modernizing its Contact Center capabilities, VA will reduce duplication, improve responsiveness, and meet the growing national demand for timely, personalized support. This initiative is not merely a technical upgrade; it is a strategic shift toward Veteran-centric service delivery.

SCOPE OF WORK

The Contractor shall deliver a comprehensive suite of program support services, drawing upon expertise in healthcare, Contact Center Consolidation (CCC), and the Genesys platform. This support will encompass project management (utilizing the Project Management Body of Knowledge [PMBOK] framework), business process standardization, contact center operational design, workforce planning and optimization, technology modernization and integration, change management, communications, policy formulation, and training.

This effort will extend to all major VA Administrations and Staff Offices, including the Veterans Health Administration (VHA), Veterans Benefits Administration (VBA), National Cemetery Administration (NCA), Office of Information Technology (OIT), Office of Management (OM), and the Veterans Experience Offices (VEO). The activities will be centered on planning, coordinating, and executing a phased consolidation of the existing VA contact centers into an enterprise structure aligned with VA's overarching governance, performance, and technology frameworks.

The consolidation of VA contact centers will be conducted through a multi-phased organizational model aimed at delivering the CCC solution across multiple, iterative and concurrent waves. This model ensures strategic oversight and management until Full Op

The Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) Office of Information and Technology (OIT) will host an in-person Industry Day to reconnect and strengthen collaboration with our partner organizations, fostering innovation and shared commitment to advancing veteran services. This event is jointly organized by the VA Technology Acquisition Center (TAC) and the VA Office of Small and Disadvantaged Business Utilization (OSDBU).

Participants will engage with OIT senior leadership, get knowledge about recent organizational improvements, obtain insights into OIT's IT acquisition strategy and digital transformation priorities, and comprehend expectations for vendor engagement and performance.

search_text:
Lean Depot Management System (LDMS) Sustainment Support

CAUTION:

The Government is conducting market research to identify potential sources that have experience with the Lean Depot Management System (LDMS) and the expertise to maintain the infrastructure to support and sustain the LDMS within existing USAF legacy systems.  This requirement is specifically for the USAF Small Arms Depot that has a requirement to sustain the LDMS within their unique depot repair operations.  The Air Force has not made a commitment to procure any of the items/services discussed, and release of this RFI should not be construed as such a commitment or as authorization to incur cost for which reimbursement would be required or sought.  Contractors/Institutions responding to this market research are placed on notice that participation in this survey may not ensure participation in future solicitations or contract awards.  The Government will not reimburse participants for any expenses associated with their participation in this survey.  All submissions become Government property and will not be returned.

Request for Information or Solicitation for Planning Purposes

(a) The Government does not intend to award a contract on the basis of this solicitation or to otherwise pay for the information solicited except as an allowable cost under other contracts as provided in Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) 31.205-18, Independent Research and Bid and Proposal Costs.

(b) Although “proposal” and “offeror” are used in this Request for Information, your response will be treated as information only.  It shall not be used as a proposal.

(c) This Request for Information is issued for the purpose of market research in an effort to overcome barriers to competition.

Purpose

This requirement is to provide the Air Force Small Arms (AFSA) Depot and the Small Arms Program Office (SAPO) with LDMS user support, accounts, help tickets and server administration (backups, security, patches, hardware, failures, etc.).  The information provided in the RFI is subject to change and is not binding on the Government.  Some of the services and support tasks are, but not limited to:

In LDMS, assist with uploads of equipment inventory, parts/kits, customer names, vendors, tooling, Illustrated Parts Breakdowns

Contractor expertise shall be required for all LDMS Functional areas

Brief Description

The AFSA Depot performs small arms and light weight depot repairs and provides weapons sustainment in support of the SAPO.  Systems include ground-fired rifles, carbines, handguns, grenade launchers, shotguns, and machine guns.  The depot also modifies and manufactures special tools and fixtures.  Weapons Repair Technicians may perform weapons inspections and repairs on all small arms and light weapons assigned to the Air Force.  They evaluate and provide technical solutions to complex small arms problems.  In addition, they assist in evaluating new and emerging technologies to be used in the small arms arena to satisfy new requirements.  Weapons Repair Technicians also support the USAF Shooting Team, National Match, Royal Air Force Atlantic Challenge, Defender Challenge and other competitions as requested and approved.  Weapons Repair Technicians provide rapid response affecting the entire Air Force, including complete weapons systems or to satisfy an urgent and compelling war fighter need.

The AFSA Depot provides a unique capability that does not exist in base intermediate shops or DoW depot-level overhaul facilities.

The LDMS sustainment allows continued visibility and traceability of all weapons/parts to meet Financial Improvement and Audit Readiness (FIAR) compliance.  This also facilitates the AFSA Depot operations and keeps it aligned with maintenance information systems already in use today by other USAF depots.

As an integral part of the AF maintenance and supply data tracking, it must maintain an interface/data exchange with current legacy and future systems to include but not limited to:

D043-Master Item Identification Control System

The contractor shall actively and responsively coordinate with designated AFSA Depot and SAPO team members participating in LDMS sustainment operations.

Expertise and Skills Sought

Program Management Support

At least 5 years of experience managing software, server administration, or database projects.

System Operational Support

At least 5 years of experience supporting and managing one or more servers within the AF network infrastructure

At least 5 years of experience supporting and managing databases within the AF network infrastructure

At least 3 years of experience working with legacy supply systems such as D043 and D035K and associated integration

At least 3 years of experience working with LDMS integration

DoD approved information assurance workforce certifications for necessary IA personnel as listed in the current version of DoDM 8140.03.  At a minimum: CompTia Security+ is required for the System Administrator (SA), Database Administrator (DBA), and Information Assurance Officer (IAO)

The contractor shall possess at a minimum the following:

Extensive knowledge and experience with LDMS and related infrastructure to ensure proper interface with legacy systems

User help desk and DD Form 2875, System Authorization Access Request, validation

LDMS help desk support for troubleshooting and report functions

Note: THIS IS A REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI) ONLY to identify sources that can provide sustainment support for the LDMS.  The information provided in the RFI is subject to change and is not binding on the Government.  The Air Force has not made a commitment to procure any of the items/services discussed, and release of this RFI should not be construed as such a commitment or as authorization to incur cost for which reimbursement would be required or sought.  All submissions become Government property and will not be returned.

Responses must be received no later than close of business 26 Mar 2026.

Please send responses to ricardo.jones@us.af.mil with a copy to jennifer.primus@us.af.mil and hayley.brown.2@us.af.mil.

CONTRACTOR CAPABILITY SURVEY

Lean Depot Management System (LDMS) Sustainment Support

Part I.  Business Information

Please provide the following business information for your company/institution and for any teaming or joint venture partners:

	Company/Institute Name:

	Address:

	Point of Contact:

	CAGE Code:

Phone Number:

E-mail Address:

Web Page URL:

	Government Proposed North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) Code: 541511	

Based on the Government proposed NAICS Code, state whether your company is:

Small Business				(Yes / No)

Woman Owned Small Business		(Yes / No)

Small Disadvantaged Business		(Yes / No)

8(a) Certified				(Yes / No)

HUBZone Certified			(Yes / No)

Veteran Owned Small Business		(Yes / No)

Service Disabled Veteran Small Business	(Yes / No)

System for Award Management (SAM)  	(Yes / No)

A statement as to whether your company is domestically or foreign owned (if foreign, please indicate the country of ownership).

Responses to the Capability Survey Part I and Part II must be received no later than close of business 23 Mar 2026.  Please ensure data is in a readable electronic format that can be received through a firewall.  Attachments shall not to exceed 10 MB per email.  Multiple emails are acceptable.  Responses must be sent via email to the following:

Ricardo Jones, ricardo.jones@us.af.mil 

Jennifer Primus, jennifer.primus@us.af.mil 

Hayley Brown hayley.brown.2@us.af.mil

Questions relative to this RFI should be addressed to the PCO, Ricardo Jones, ricardo.jones@us.af.mil.

Part II. Capability Survey Questions

General Capability Questions:

Describe the nature of the service capabilities you provide supporting and sustaining the LDMS.  

Describe your company's past experience providing services similar in complexity to this requirement.  Include contract numbers, a brief description of the work performed, period of performance, agency/organization supported, and individual point of contact (Contracting Officer or Program Manager).

What quality assurance standards / processes does your company employ?  Provide a description of your quality program (ISO 9001, AS9100, etc.).

Commerciality Questions:

Is there established catalog or market prices for our requirement?

If you offer this product and/or service to both U.S. Government and commercial sources, is the same workforce used for both the U.S. Government and general public?

Is our requirement offered to both under similar terms and conditions?  Briefly describe any differences.

search_text:
DRAFT
NAVY DSRC TASK ORDER FOR HITSU- III 
1.0 GENERAL INFORMATION 
This task order performance work statement (PWS) consists of background, scope, and 
tasks specific to the Navy DSRC.  Any PWS sections not referred to in this task order 
PWS should align with the base contract PWS. 
1
.1 INTRODUCTION: 
COMNAVMETOCCOM administers and operates the Navy DSRC, one of the five DoD 
HPC DSRCs established under the auspices of the DoD HPCMP.  Located at Stennis 
Space Center in Mississippi.  Navy DSRC is also the executive agent for the enterprise 
data archiving capabilities consistent across the five DSRCs.     
1
.2 BACKGROUND: 
The Navy DSRC maintains and provides premiere HPC capability with primary
emphasis on support of the largest, most computationally intensive HPC applications
and delivery of time critical HPC services to directly support DoD operations worldwide.
Operating as a component of Naval Meteorology and Oceanography Command 
(CNMOC), The Navy DSRC’s unique program has an operational component requiring 
the highest priority consistent operations to Fleet Numerical Meteorology and 
Oceanography Center (FNMOC).
1.3 SCOPE: 
The scope of this contracted effort includes: (1) essential Navy DSRC operations 
support including system integration and operation, system administration, network 
administration, cybersecurity/information assurance, facilities support, database 
administration, storage administration, maintenance management and execution for the 
facility, hardware, and software; (2) delivery of HPC capabilities at unclassified and 
classified levels; (3) enterprise and user support requirements, such as Navy DSRC 
Help Desk, application software support, outreach, and support for other HPCMP 
components; (4) program and project management support, such as financial data and 
reports and process management, procurement support, and inventory support. 
Operations support includes all services required for Navy DSRC routine operations 
somewhat independent of HPC capabilities being delivered or customers supported. 
The Navy DSRC operates 24 hours/day, 7 days/week, even during federal holidays. 

DRAFT
Cybersecurity is a critical requirement on this contract, where the Navy DSRC must 
perform continuous risk assessments and management, as well as sustain the 
approvals to operate. 
This contract will provide services to support the operations and management of the 
Navy DSRC.  Requirements at the Navy DSRC include work requested and funded by 
HPCMP components other than HPCMP Centers, as well as customer-funded efforts 
outside the HPCMP. 
 
2.0 Period of Performance (POP): 
The period of performance for this task order will consist of a five-year ordering period.  
Tasks will be issued during the five-year ordering period and will specify a period of 
performance for each task. 
 
3.0 GENERAL SCOPE OF Requirements: 
3.1 Center Operations: The contractor shall provide support across the Navy DSRC 
which includes IT infrastructure systems located at the Stennis Space Center bunkers 
and The National Center for Critical Information Processing and Storage (NCCIPS) as 
well as the main facility located within the Naval Oceanographic Office building 
complex. 
 Local Operations: The contractor shall provide computer operator support in 
accordance with documented procedures and processes.  This support is required to 
provide the personnel necessary to monitor and operate the Navy DSRC and provide 
around the clock (i.e., 24 hours a day/ 7 days a week) operations and IT help desk 
support at the Stennis Space Center, MS. Note: Customer Support augments the 
HPCMP Help Desk by providing support to on-site users as well as local and remote 
users of classified resources. It is staffed 0800 – 1630 Central Time and Due to direct 
support of DoD operations worldwide, all trouble calls require response or transfer 
within 30 minutes during normal duty hours or 2 hours after normal duty hours. 
 
The highest level of security clearance required for services performed under this 
contract is SECRET. The Navy DSRC operates in an open security environment and 
processes unclassified and classified data up to and including SECRET. Most 
Contractors performing work on this contract require IT Level I (critical sensitive 
information access) as defined in SECNAV M5510.30. Some Contractors require IT 
Level II (noncritical sensitive information access) as defined in SECNAV M5510.30. 
All contractors will be required hold and maintain, at a minimum, SECRET eligibility 
through a valid and current NACLC, Tier 3 (T3) or higher investigation [Tier 5 (T5), 
Single Scope Background Investigation (SSBI)] in accordance with SECNAV M5510.30. 
Contractors requiring IT Level I (critical sensitive information access) require a favorably 
adjudicated final T5/SSBI investigation.  
 

DRAFT
The contractor shall operate government vehicles as needed within Stennis Space 
Center as well as within the local area. These vehicles include any regular passenger 
vehicles. The contractor shall not operate any vehicles requiring a Commercial Driver's 
License (CDL). The contractor shall comply with the provisions of OPNAV Instruction 
5100.12J, Traffic Safety Instructions, regarding licensing of contractor personnel.  
 
3.1.1 Operations Monitoring.  The contractor shall provide support to operate and 
monitor, as directed, all HPCMP and DSRC systems and computing infrastructure 
assets within the Navy DSRC. This includes 100% of all networks (unclassified and 
classified), data storage, data archives, web services, asset management, and other 
DSRC and Enterprise systems.  It also includes the health of major HPC systems that 
are provided and maintained by a system vendor or original equipment manufacturer 
(OEM).  Further, it includes cybersecurity and other aspects of operating and 
maintaining HPC systems as identified by the government. The contractor will be 
responsible for any additional emerging requirements, including workload management, 
software virtualization containers, container orchestrators, and user support, customer 
support, or both, that are placed on the major HPC systems by the Program. The 
contractor shall develop automated systems to enhance the monitoring of operations, 
both in scope and response time. 
 
3.1.2 Facilities Engineering Support: The Contractor shall provide support to Navy 
DSRC efforts related but not limited to minor building alterations, environmental 
monitoring, electrical power and backup subsystems, and mechanical support systems.  
Services include planning through engineering designs and assessments, as well as 
other functions needed to execute facility projects. Environmental monitoring is largely 
provided via an automated system (EMMS). The Navy DSRC has been increasing the 
level of automation in recent years, and the contractor shall assist further development 
of these automated systems. 
 
3.1.3 Cybersecurity Support for DSRC Operations.  The baseline operational DREN, 
SDREN enclaves in the Navy DSRC require authorization to operate and connect to the 
wide-area networks.  The Contractor shall assist the government cybersecurity staff in 
attaining and sustaining the appropriate authorizations to operate.  DSRC cybersecurity 
requirements are detailed in section 3.7 of the overarching PWS. 
 
3.1.4 Systems Administration: Note: OEM personnel, contracted directly by the 
Government, are primarily responsible for HPC systems administration, with the 
contractor providing supplemental support. While this is current in 2025, this is subject 
to change and must be considered at the time of RFP and the nature of the contracts 
negotiated on incoming Technology Insertions (TIs).
  
For baseline Navy DSRC operations, there are several non-HPC devices such as 
workstations, servers, repositories, and virtual communication equipment. The 
Contractor shall provide system administration support in keeping these systems 
operating reliably and securely. 
 

DRAFT
3.1.5 Network Engineering and Administration: For baseline DSRC operations, the 
DREN, and SDREN enclaves require engineering and system administration support for 
the various equipment items such as routers and switches.  The Contractor shall 
provide network engineering and system administration for the network devices.  In 
addition, in accordance with DoD HPCMP direction and JFHQ-DODIN Comply-to-
Connect (C2C) Concept of Operations instructions, the Contractor shall 
administer/maintain the necessary hardware and software (currently Forescout) at the 
Navy DSRC to continue enforcing C2C compliance on the DREN and SDREN. 
 
3.1.6 Database Administration:  The Contractor shall provide local Navy database 
administration support for system utilization and change management requests.  
 
3.1.7 Software Development.  The Contractor shall develop, and support execution of 
specialized software used in Navy DSRC operations.  This includes but is not limited to 
applications to monitor and automate facility environmental controls, monitor and 
automate center operations, and automate operational and business processes. 
 
3.1.8 Maintenance support: The Contractor shall provide support for DSRC hardware, 
software, and facility maintenance agreements related to facility equipment and 
supporting infrastructure (networks, non-HPC servers, etc.) used in routine operations.            
Note: Hardware, software, and facility maintenance are performed by the contractor 
with additional support provided by Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) or third-
party maintenance.  
 
3.1.9 Inventory Management Support:  
The Contractor shall assist the government in identifying and documenting equipment 
inventory for asset management.  For Navy DSRC, assistance will be provided to 
maintaining the inventory of COMNAVMETOCCOM. This inventory in less than a tenth 
of the overall inventory.  Assistance with removal of Navy DSRC excess/retired 
hardware and removal from inventory is also performed by the contractor. 
 
3.1.10 Configuration and Change Management.  The contractor shall manage the 
Navy DSRC’s configuration and change management database; provide support by 
identifying, maintaining, reporting, and verifying configuration management items; 
provide support to the Navy DSRC by identifying, analyzing, documenting, and 
maintaining change requests IAW local process and procedure. 
 
3.1.11 Procurement Support: The Navy DSRC can purchase various items needed to 
support routine operations.  This includes, but is not limited to, office supplies and 
equipment, facility infrastructure items, and HPC infrastructure equipment, as well as 
hardware/software maintenance.  The Contractor shall provide procurement/purchasing 
support for any Navy DSRC requirements. 
 
3.1.12 Continuity of Operations: Technical support is required to reconstitute critical 
operations, defined by the government, at alternative location(s) in the event of a 
disaster that causes the shutdown of Navy DSRC operations at the primary site.  The 
Contractor shall assist in establishing continuity of operations and disaster recovery for 

DRAFT
such events and shall support any exercises/simulations. Note: The Navy DSRC has an 
operational component requiring the highest priority in restoring operations. 
3.1.13 Technical Project Support.  The Navy DSRC may execute studies and projects 
related (but not limited) to facility upgrades, infrastructure changes, HPC capacity 
increases, and HPC system installation and integration.  The Contractor shall provide 
technical support to Navy DSRC studies and projects, whether at the local or enterprise 
level.  This support shall include technology planning and assessments and engineering 
support during project execution. 
 
 
PWS 3.2 Deliver Unclassified HPC Capabilities at the Navy DSRC 
 
Delivering HPC capabilities includes requirements in direct support of installing, 
integrating, and operating HPC systems.  The baseline scope in this PWS section 
includes all unclassified HPC systems.  This PWS section also includes the HPC-
related infrastructure that ties directly to delivering unclassified HPC capabilities, such 
as data movement, data storage (mid-tier and archive), and user interfaces/portals to 
unclassified HPC systems.  The Navy DSRC generally hosts three to five large HPC 
systems as well as a varying number of smaller specialized systems.  
3.2.1 Infrastructure Support for Unclassified HPC Systems.  The Contractor shall 
provide technical support to include (but is not limited to) system engineering, system 
integration, system administration, and database administration for HPC-related 
infrastructure directly tied to delivering unclassified HPC capabilities.  This includes (but 
not limited to) HPC data movement and storage systems, portals, servers, routers, and 
switches that have direct relationships/connections to HPC systems.  This does not 
include those systems or devices that are part of the Navy DSRC baseline operations 
identified in paragraph 3.1 and associated subparagraphs. 
 
3.2.2 Unclassified HPC System Installation and Integration.  This requirement 
includes preparation of the Navy DSRC facilities for installing, integrating, accepting, 
and operating unclassified HPC systems.  The Contractor shall provide engineering 
support for connecting the unclassified HPC systems to the Navy DSRC infrastructure, 
both facilities-related and HPC-delivery-related. 
 
3.2.3 Application Management Support for Unclassified HPC.  The HPC original 
equipment manufacturers (OEMs) typically provide levels of support, maintenance, and 
warranties for hardware and software delivered with an operational HPC system.  For 
those software components that are to be installed and/or modified to support HPCMP 
workloads/use cases, the Contractor shall install, modify, and manage those 
applications for unclassified HPC system utility.  This includes (but is not limited to) job 
schedulers, reservation/dedicated partition services, data management/movement 
services, data visualizers, HPC applications, and user interfaces/portals. 
 
3.2.4 Cybersecurity Support for Unclassified HPC Systems.  This requirement is 
related to cybersecurity support for unclassified HPC needed in addition to the Navy 

DRAFT
DSRC operations cybersecurity support identified in paragraph 3.1.3.  The Contractor 
shall provide this additional cybersecurity support to include (but not limited to) HPC 
Infrastructure and HPC systems.  Cybersecurity requirements related to unclassified 
HPC systems are identified in the overarching PWS section 3.7. 
 
3.2.5 Unclassified HPC System Support: HPC systems may be managed and 
administered by OEMs, third-party entities, government personnel, or the Contractor.  
The Contractor shall provide additional unclassified HPC system administration support 
including (but not limited to) job scheduler configuration,

GENERAL INFORMATION

PLACE OF PERFORMANCE

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

DESCRIPTION

THIS REQUEST FOR INFORMATION (RFI)/SOURCES SOUGHT IS ISSUED SOLELY FOR INFORMATION AND PLANNING PURPOSES ONLY AND DOES NOT CONSTITUTE A SOLICITATION.

THE SUBMISSION OF PRICING, CAPABILITIES FOR PLANNING PURPOSES, AND OTHER MARKET INFORMATION IS HIGHLY ENCOURAGED AND ALLOWED UNDER THIS RFI IN ACCORDANCE WITH (IAW) FAR 15.201(e).

DISCLAIMER

This RFI is issued solely for information and planning purposes and does not constitute a solicitation. Neither unsolicited proposals nor any other kind of offers will be considered in response to this RFI. IAW FAR 15.201(e), responses to this notice are not offers and will not be accepted by the Government to form a binding contract. Responders are solely responsible for all expenses associated with responding to this RFI. All information received in response to this RFI that is marked Proprietary, will be handled accordingly. Responses to the RFI will not be returned. At this time, questions concerning the composition and requirements for a future RFQ will not be entertained.

SOURCES SOUGHT DESCRIPTION

This is NOT a solicitation announcement.  This is a sources sought/RFI only.  The purpose of this sources sought/RFI is to gain knowledge of potential qualified sources and their size classification relative to NAICS 541512, Computer Systems Design Services. Responses to this sources sought will be used by the Government to make appropriate acquisition decisions.  After review of the responses to this sources sought, a solicitation announcement may be published.  Responses to this sources sought synopsis are not considered adequate responses for a solicitation announcement.  

The Department of Veterans Affairs (VA), VISN 05 Network Contracting Office, is seeking sources that can complete requirements for Non-immersive virtual reality walking system that at a minimum meets the requirements listed under the description section below.  

The information identified above is intended to be descriptive, not restrictive and to indicate the quality of the supplies/services that will be satisfactory. It is the responsibility of the interested source to demonstrate to the government that the interested parties can provide the supplies/services that fulfill the required specifications. 

If you are interested and are capable of providing the sought out supplies/services, please provide the requested information as well as the information indicated below. Response to this notice should include:

(1) Please indicate the size status and representations of your business, such as but not limited to: Service-Disabled Veteran Owned Small Business (SDVOSB), Veteran Owned Small Business (VOSB), Hubzone, Woman Owned Small Business (WOSB), Large Business, etc.)? Service-Disabled Veteran Owned Small Business (SDVOSB) receive preference. 

Company name, address of company, point of contact

A through description of experience in managing support requirements of this nature.

(2) Is your company considered small under the NAICS code identified under this RFI?

(3) Your FSS GSA/NAC contract number. 

(4) General pricing of your products/solution is encouraged. Pricing will be used for the purpose of market research only. It will not be used to evaluate any type of award.

(5) Please submit your capabilities in regards to the salient characteristics being provided and any information pertaining to “equal to items” to establish capabilities for planning purposes?

(6) Please review salient characteristics/statement of work (if applicable) and provide feedback or suggestions. If none, please reply as N/A.

(7) Please provide your DUNS/UEI number.

CONTACT INFORMATION: Responses to this notice and any questions related to this RFI shall be directed to Cassie White cassie.white@va.gov. All information regarding Capabilities Statements or any other proprietary information relative to this RFI shall be submitted via email to cassie.white@va.gov. Telephone questions or responses shall not be accepted.  Responses must be received no later than 10:00 AM EST, 2/28/2026.  If a solicitation is issued it shall be announced at a later date, and all interested parties must respond to that solicitation announcement separately from the responses to this sources sought.  Responses to this sources sought notice are not a request to be added to a prospective bidders list or to receive a copy of the solicitation.

Description

The Loch Raven VA Medical Center and/or the Baltimore Annex Human Motor Performance Laboratory.

General

The Contractor shall provide equipment to the Loch Raven VA Medical Center and/or the Baltimore Annex Human Motor Performance Laboratory.  The Contractor shall provide all necessary information and characteristics of an non-immersive virtual reality walking system that can reduce fall risk in older adults. The VR walking platform shall work with our existing equipment.

Requirements

Safety Harness

Real Time Patient Monitoring 

	Tracks patients progress.

	Keeps training history

	Up to date data

Works with existing equipment

Warranty

Loch Raven VA Medical Center and/or the Baltimore Annex Human Motor Performance Laboratory
