UNCLASSIFIED  
 
 
 
LCNC Application Ecosystem RFI 
  
February 2026  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Low Code No Code (LCNC) 
Logistics and Finance 
Application Ecosystem 
 
Request for Information (RFI) 
 
 
U.S. Army Contracting Command – Rock Island (ACC-RI) 
3055 Rodman Avenue 
Rock Island, IL 61299-0001 
 
 
 
  
 


UNCLASSIFIED  
 
 
 
LCNC Application Ecosystem RFI 
 i 
February 2026  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
THIS PAGE INTENTIONALLY LEFT BLANK 
 
  

UNCLASSIFIED  
 
 
 
LCNC Application Ecosystem RFI 
 ii 
February 2026  
 
Table of Contents 
1.0 Purpose................................................................................................................. 1 
2.0 Background........................................................................................................... 1 
3.0 Objectives ............................................................................................................. 2 
4.0 Requested Information ......................................................................................... 3 
4.1 Company Profile ................................................................................................ 3 
4.2 Application Type and Data Model ..................................................................... 4 
4.3 Domain Expertise/Areas of Interest and Operational Impact ............................. 5 
4.4 Experience with the Army’s Approved Platforms ............................................... 6 
4.5 Proposing an Alternative (non-APL) Platform .................................................... 7 
4.6 Partnering for Rapid Acquisition. ....................................................................... 8 
4.7 Driving Mission Advantage through Innovative Technologies ........................... 9 
4.8 Security and Interoperability Standards ............................................................. 9 
4.9 Illustrative Cost and Schedule ......................................................................... 10 
5.0 Submission Instructions ...................................................................................... 11 
6.0 Appendices ......................................................................................................... 11 
6.1 Appendix A: Current Army ERP Systems .......................................................... 13 
6.2 Appendix B: The EBS-C Value Chains ............................................................... 14 
6.3 Appendix C: EBS-C Solution Architecture ........................................................ 15 
6.4 Appendix D: References .................................................................................. 16 
6.5 Appendix E: Approved Product List ................................................................. 17 
 

UNCLASSIFIED  
 
 
 
LCNC Application Ecosystem RFI 
 1 
February 2026  
 
 
1.0 Purpose 
The Army Contracting Command – Rock Island (ACC-RI), on behalf of PEO Enterprise 
and the Project Manager, Enterprise Business Systems – Convergence (PM EBS-C), 
requests information from industry regarding capabilities, interest, and approaches to 
develop, host, integrate, and sustain Low-Code/No-Code (LCNC) applications that 
support Army logistics and finance communities.  The Army is committed to fostering 
innovation and collaboration with industry, including non-traditional contractors, to 
develop solutions that align with modernization goals.  Vendors who may not have a 
fully developed solution but have the capability, interest, and willingness to develop 
applications tailored to Army needs are encouraged to respond.   
Vendor input will be used to inform the acquisition approach, technical requirements, 
integration strategies, and potential enterprise adoption consistent with Army LCNC 
guidance.   
Disclaimer: This RFI is issued solely for market research purposes and does not 
constitute a solicitation or a commitment to issue a solicitation in the future. Responses 
to this notice are not offers and cannot be accepted by the Government to form a 
binding contract. Any information submitted by respondents to this RFI is strictly 
voluntary. Respondents are responsible for all costs associated with response 
preparation. Responses will not be returned to the responder. Information received in 
response to this RFI shall be safeguarded adequately from unauthorized disclosure.   
2.0 Background 
The Army is undergoing a major modernization effort to establish a Common Business 
Operating Environment (CBOE) with EBS-C converging the current Army SAP ERP 
systems (Appendix A). This modernization effort includes assessing and migrating 
operational value chain processes (Appendix B) to enable:   
1. Supply Chain Simplification   
2. Process Automation   
3. Resilient and Secure Infrastructure   

UNCLASSIFIED  
 
 
 
LCNC Application Ecosystem RFI 
 2 
February 2026  
 
 
4. Agility to Support Maneuvers   
5. Scalability to Adapt to Future Demands   
6. Auditability to Maintain Transparency, Traceability, and Accountability 
The key elements of the CBOE are captured in the EBS-C architecture diagram 
(Appendix C), which includes the:   
• Transactional Core Layer: The foundation of the system using SAP S/4HANA 
and provides stable, governed, and reliable processing of all core business 
transactions (e.g., financials, procurement, logistics, HR, and manufacturing).   
• Engagement Layer: Facilitates all user interactions.   
• Agility Layer: Enables unique business processes and business workflows to 
avoid building customization of the Transactional Core.   
• Integration Layer: Ensures reliable, real-time flow and transformation of data 
between the Transactional Core, the Agility Layer, and external systems. 
A key aspect of the EBS-C architecture is the support of an application ecosystem to 
augment EBS-C capabilities at the operational level. LCNC solutions will play a critical 
role in modernizing hundreds of defense business systems that perform routine, non-
transactional functions (analytics and decision support tools) or transactional functions 
that integrate with ERP systems. These solutions will leverage APIs to authoritative data 
sources and align with the Army CIO Low-Code/No-Code Development Guidance 
(Appendix D).  Additionally, these solutions must align with the Army Unified Data 
Reference Architecture (UDRA) to emphasize interoperability and data portability across 
systems. 
3.0 Objectives 
The Army is establishing an LCNC ecosystem to accelerate delivery of business and 
mission solutions while ensuring governance, cybersecurity, and interoperability with 
Army enterprise services and data platforms.  LCNC applications will augment EBS-C by 
providing rapid user-centric solutions for mission support tasks that are not part of the 
EBS-C core.  
The Army seeks information on vendor capabilities and approaches that address the 
following high-level objectives:   

UNCLASSIFIED  
 
 
 
LCNC Application Ecosystem RFI 
 3 
February 2026  
 
 
1. Market Capabilities: Discover industry capabilities, interest, and willingness to 
support the Army’s ecosystem of applications in the areas of finance and 
logistics. Vendors may respond based on their existing solutions or their 
capability to develop applications tailored to Army requirements. Responses 
should categorize applications as transactional or non-transactional and assess 
integration needs.   
2. Development using Approved Platforms: Identify vendors, solutions, and 
development approaches using Approved Product List (APL) systems 
(Appendix E) to ensure compatibility, security, and compliance with Army 
standards.   
3. Development using non-APL Platforms: Determine the feasibility of 
adopting new platforms and the timeline for approval if not on the APL.   
4. Partnering for Rapid Acquisition: Design strategies to maximize outreach, 
encourage innovation, expand the industrial base, streamline acquisition and 
transition successful prototypes to production.   
5. Driving Mission Advantage through Innovative Technologies: Pursue 
innovative features or tools for accelerating development of the application and 
aligned with the functional communities’ needs for the application to improve 
operational readiness and enhance decision-making.  Collaboration with 
functional communities during development is required. 
6. Security and Interoperability Standards: Provide solutions that adhere to 
Army cybersecurity, interoperability, and governance standards.   
7. Cost and Schedule: Rapidly deploy and demonstrate prototype projects and 
determine development and operation costs. 
4.0 Requested Information  
4.1 Company Profile 
Please provide the following information for your company. If you are submitting as part 
of a team, please provide this information for each company on the team. Vendors who 
may not have a fully developed solution but are willing to develop applications tailored 
to Army needs should describe their capability and interest in doing so."  

UNCLASSIFIED  
 
 
 
LCNC Application Ecosystem RFI 
 4 
February 2026  
 
 
1. Company Information: Please provide the company's name, address, country 
of business, and website. 
2. Company Representative: Please provide the name, business title, phone 
number, and email address of the company representative 
3. Government Identification: Please provide the CAGE code and SAM Unique 
Entity ID.  
4. Partnering: Please describe your team's approach to partnering. 
5. Certifications: Please list any relevant certifications, such as FedRAMP, RMF, or 
ATO. 
4.2 Application Type and Data Model 
Please define your solution or proposed solution for development by selecting the 
category below that best describes its primary function and its interaction with Army 
data systems. Vendors who do not have a fully developed application but have the 
capability and willingness to develop solutions tailored to Army needs should describe 
their approach and technical expertise. If your solution or proposed solution for 
development has both transactional and non-transactional components, please describe 
each. 
1. Transactional Applications.   Definition: These applications are built to 
process data and execute transactions that require a two-way, read/write 
integration with an Army ERP system of record (Appendix A). Their core purpose 
is to create, update, or modify data within those authoritative systems. Your 
Response Must Describe: 
• Your technical approach to integration and data synchronization.   
• How you ensure data integrity, manage error handling, and maintain an 
audit trail between your application / proposed application and the Army's 
ERP. 
• Which specific Army ERP systems your solution is designed to integrate with. 
2. Non-Transactional Applications.   Definition: These applications are designed 
for analytics, decision support, visualization, or reporting. They consume data 
from authoritative Army sources in a read-only capacity and do not write new or 
modified data back to the core ERP systems. Your Response Must Describe: 

UNCLASSIFIED  
 
 
 
LCNC Application Ecosystem RFI 
 5 
February 2026  
 
 
• How your application or proposed application aggregates data from one or 
more authoritative sources to provide actionable insights. 
• Your methodology for transforming raw data into intuitive dashboards, 
predictive analytics, or other tools that enhance decision-making for the 
finance and logistics communities.   
• The primary Army data sources your application would leverage.   
4.3 Domain Expertise/Areas of Interest and Operational Impact  
Demonstrate your company's expertise and willingness to solve challenges specific to 
the Army's finance and logistics domains. Vendors who may not have a fully developed 
solution but have the capability and interest in developing applications tailored to Army 
needs should describe their approach and technical expertise. Your response should 
provide concrete evidence of your capabilities and experience. 
1. Primary Area of Expertise:  Please select the area(s) where you have proven 
experience and provide examples of your solutions or projected solutions you 
could develop.  
• For Finance: Describe a finance application you have developed or propose 
to develop based on your expertise. Explain the specific problem it solved or 
could solve (e.g., improved budget tracking, streamlined financial reporting, 
enhanced audit readiness) and the value it provided or could provide for the 
user.   
• For Logistics: Describe a logistics application you have developed or 
propose to develop based on your expertise. Explain the specific problem it 
solved or could solve (e.g., increased inventory accuracy, improved supply 
chain visibility, enhanced readiness reporting). Show how it delivered or 
could deliver measurable value to the user.    
• Describe your ability to develop logistics applications that support Army 
requirements (e.g., inventory management, readiness indicators and others). 
Provide examples of innovative logistics applications you have developed. 
2. Bridging the Finance-Logistics Divide: A critical Army requirement is 
maintaining a seamless, auditable link between logistical actions and financial 
transactions (e.g., linking a parts request to a purchase order and its final 
payment). 

UNCLASSIFIED  
 
 
 
LCNC Application Ecosystem RFI 
 6 
February 2026  
 
 
• Describe how your solution(s) or proposed solution(s) ensure or would 
ensure this data integrity.   
• Explain your approach to creating and maintaining an unbreakable audit 
trail that connects logistics and financial data across different systems. 
3. Scalability for the Global Mission: Army operations span the globe, from 
large, well-connected installations to small teams in disconnected, tactical 
environments.   
• Describe your architecture and methodology for scaling your application to 
support enterprise-wide deployment (potentially hundreds of thousands of 
users). 
• Explain how your application can effectively support users in disconnected, 
limited bandwidth, or intermittent connectivity (tactical edge) environments. 
What is your model for data synchronization when a connection is re-
established? 
4.4 Experience with the Army’s Approved Platforms 
Your ability to effectively build and deploy on our approved platforms is critical. This 
section requires you to provide evidence of your experience with the Army's specific 
technology ecosystem as defined in Appendix E.  If you only have experience in a 
commercial technology ecosystem, please relate that experience to the questions below.   
1. Platform Expertise and Project Experience:    
• Identify which of the CIO Approved Development Platforms (e.g., Vantage, 
PowerApps, ServiceNow) you have direct, hands-on experience building 
solutions on  
• For each platform you identify, provide an example of a project you have 
delivered. Describe the solution

search_text:
The 316th Contracting Squadron at Joint Base Andrews, Maryland intends to award a 
simpliﬁed acquisition on a sole source basis to Lumena Inc. in accordance with FAR 6.302-
1 Only.one.responsible.source. 
The Government has an existing MindGym hardware solution that requires continued 
support of the proprietary Mind Gym software to continue to function, remain up-to-date, 
and support. Lumena, a woman-owned small business (WOSB), is the only source capable 
of providing this proprietary software. No other vendors or contract vehicles offer or are 
authorized to distribute this product. The requirement is commercial in nature, and no 
changes to the government's technical documentation are needed prior to solicitation. No 
competitive opportunities apply due to the lack of alternative sources. The period of 
performance requested is 17 February 2026 (Tuesday). 
A determination by the Government not to compete the proposed procurement action 
based upon responses to this notice is solely within the discretion of the Government. The 
North American Industry Classiﬁcation System (NAICS) Code for this acquisition is 541511 
with a size standard of $34M. Information received will be considered solely for the 
purpose of determining whether to conduct a competitive procurement. The government 
will not be responsible for any costs incurred in preparation of any capabilities statements 
or documents prepared for this notice. 
Parties interested in this announcement shall provide detailed information of their 
capabilities and certiﬁcations to clearly meet the requirements stated above. Responses 
will be via email, to include the company's name, point of contact, address, phone number, 
UEI number, business size, and CAGE code. All responses must be received by 13 February 
2026 (Friday) by 4:30 PM EST. Responses may be emailed to the Contract Specialist, Amn 
Zwe Naing via email zwe.naing@us.af.mil. 
NOTE: If documents are sent via e-mail, the contractor will be responsible for validating 
Government receipt by contacting the Contract Specialist. No solicitation is available and 
telephone requests for information will not be taken. 
 
Approved by: 
 
 
Byron E. Howard 
Contracting Officer

POLARIS SDVOSB GWAC 
QUESTION AND RESPONSE DOCUMENT #1 
 
Thank you for your interest in the Polaris SDVOSB GWAC. 
 
Legacy questions and responses (Q&R) from the Polaris SB and Polaris WOSB Request for 
Proposal (RFP) are provided.  These Q&Rs are applicable to the Polaris SDVOSB RFP. 
 
In addressing questions, it is the Government’s position that if the solicitation’s position 
is self-evident, the response to a question may simply be that the solicitation already 
addresses the matter in the manner the Government intended for it to be addressed. 
 
While the Government may include one or more specific solicitation passages in a 
response to a question, interested parties are reminded that section L.3 of the 
solicitation states, “Offerors are instructed to read the entire solicitation document, 
including all attachments in Section J, prior to submitting questions and/or preparing a 
proposal. Omission of any information from the proposal submission requirements may 
result in rejection of the offer.” 
 
Questions were not extensively edited for grammar, punctuation or spelling. Not every 
question is shown. Only those questions, or portions of questions, that were deemed 
frequently asked and/or those that were deemed to benefit the procurement process are shown. 
 
Questions and responses are organized into topic areas shown below. Interested 
parties stand to benefit from reviewing all statements, questions, and responses. 
 
●  General 
●  Relevant Experience 
●  Past Performance 
●  Systems, Certifications and Clearances 
●  Organizational Risk Assessment 
●  Teaming 
●  Representations and Certifications 
●  Responsibility 
●  Polaris Submission Portal (PSP) 
 
This is the first Q&R document. Additional Q&R documents will be posted to SAM.gov as 
appropriate. It is the responsibility of the Offerors to periodically check the solicitation on 
SAM.gov for more information. 

General

GENERAL INFORMATION

PLACE OF PERFORMANCE

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

DESCRIPTION

1. DATE, TIME, AND PLACE:  The event takes place, Monday February 9, 2026 from 1:30 P.M. – 2:30 P.M. EST.    

2.  AGENDA:  Participants will be provided the outline of a business opportunity and related details.

3. REGISTRATION:  Visit https://www.vetbiz.va.gov/events and click the link for the “Registration Open - General Dynamics Information Technology (GDIT) Virtual Business Opportunity Session” to register. 

4.  PRIMARY NAICS CODES: Multiple North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) codes pertains to this opportunity.  Additional amendments may be issued to target other related NAICS codes specified within this announcement.

5.  DISCLAIMER: This notice is issued solely for information and planning purposes and does not constitute a solicitation. Neither unsolicited proposals nor any other kind of offers will be considered in response to this notice.

MAR
COR
SYS
COM
Condition Based Maintenance (CBM+)
Virtual Industry Day
Distribution Statement A.  Approved for public release: distribution is unlimited
.

OUR MISSION
Equip our Marines with the most capable ground 
warfare and information technology systems to 
maximize their expeditionary readiness and combat 
effectiveness in all domains.
Equipping an unrivaled, future
-
focused 
Fleet Marine Force, powered by a 
dedicated acquisition workforce.
OUR VISION

USMC CBM+ VISION
3


CBM+ CONCEPTUAL FRAMEWORK
4


USMC CBM+ IMPLEMENTATION
5


USMC CBM+ IMPLEMENTATION
6


WHAT WE ARE SEEKING FROM INDUSTRY
7
• A single contract solution for hardware, data analytics, and cyber aspects of the program. Can be 
achieved through subcontracting/partnerships
• Software/Data analytic solutions that are not proprietary.  The USMC intends to own not only the data, 
but the models and visualizations created by this contract
• Data logger hardware solution(s) that are TRL-9 and capable of WI-FI at a minimum. Multiple data 
transmission methods (Wi-Fi, Iridium, Cellular, LI-FI, etc.) is desirable
• Demonstrated experience building models for ground vehicles
• Ability and willingness to work in Government provided cloud solutions (MS Azure and Advana)
• Data visualizations that provide Marine Commanders, Staff Officers, and Maintainers a decision 
support tool that is easy to use and understand

CONDITION BASED MAINTENANCE PLUS (CBM+)  
 
 INDUSTRY DAY 
 
The Automatic Test Systems (ATS) Team, Product Manager Maintenance Support Systems 
(PdM MSS), Marine Corps Systems Command (MARCORSYSCOM) will host a Virtual 
Industry Day on 8 January 2026 from 9:00 a.m. -11:00 a.m. EST via Microsoft Teams focused 
on Condition Based Maintenance Plus (CBM+).  The Industry Day will consist of an overview 
briefing from the ATS team on the requirement, the planned acquisition approach and a one-hour 
question and answer period.  
 
MARCORSYSCOM has a requirement to operationalize CBM+ as a maintenance strategy for 
ground platforms across the USMC. CBM+ is a transformational concept that changes the 
approach of equipment management, readiness reporting, maintenance, and logistics to a data 
driven Artificial Intelligence (AI)/Machine Learning (ML) enabled process using data analytics 
as the foundation to influence action. The CBM+ process starts with collecting data that is 
transferred, stored, analyzed and ends with visualized data to support decision making. The goal 
of the program is to improve material readiness and reduce lifecycle cost of systems and 
components. It also aims to improve logistics requirements forecasting for more responsive 
support capability across the battlefield.  
 
MARCORSYSCOM is seeking a single contract solution to collect, transmit, store, analyze, and 
visualize data from ground platform Controller Area Network (CAN) Bus, Global Combat 
Support System-Marine-Corps (GCSS-MC), Expeditionary Fluid Analysis System (EFAS),  
operating data, and other sensors and data sources as they become available.  Other data sets are 
expected to be added as they become available. Solutions must protect Government data and 
information from unauthorized disclosure at every phase of the process. 
 
The following capabilities are cited to provide focus for this event but are not an all-
encompassing list of CBM+ requirements: 
 
Collect:  MARCORSYSCOM expects that Hardware will be needed to collect data from ground 
platforms. Hardware devices must be capable of interfacing with a military vehicle’s CANbus 
via a wired interface (9-pin or 6-pin Deutsch connectors (with or without minor modification) to 
record/log all information present, store no less than 32GB of data, record a minimum of two 
CANbusses simultaneously and provide a method of transmitting/ transferring the data off the 
platform. Hardware must be no larger than 7.5”x 6” x 3” inclusive of any mounting 
plates/hardware, be able to provide FIPS 140-2 compliant encryption, and be compatible with the 
Marine Corps Enterprise Network (MCEN) Wireless LAN (WLAN).  
 
Transmit/Transfer: Transmit methods of interest are Wireless Fidelity (WIFI), Light Fidelity 
(LIFI), Bluetooth, tactical military radios, and Iridium Short Burst Data. Hardware must operate 

in a passive mode and not emit any Radio Frequency (RF) signal when not in range of an 
approved and configured network. Wired/manual offload of data should be possible.  
 
Store: All collected data will be stored in Department of Defense Cloud environments accessible 
only through the Department of Defense Information Network (DoDIN). The expected cloud 
environments are the Microsoft Azure Cloud environment on the MCEN and the Navy’s Jupiter 
enclave in the Advana cloud environment. The Government will provide access to the identified 
cloud environments for qualified personnel in the performance of this work. 
 
Analyze: Data collected will be analyzed in the cloud environment(s) identified above using 
tools available in those environments along with source code and models created in the 
environment(s). Analyzing data includes applying AI/ML models to predict failures, parts 
requirements, and remaining useful life of platforms and components. Additionally, the ability to 
conduct trend analysis on fault, parts, oil life, and usage data is required.  
 
Act: The outputs of AI/ML models will be visualized in a dashboard decision support tool hosted 
in the Jupiter Advana environment and accessible via Common Access Card authentication from 
a DoDIN connected system.  The decision support tool must provide Marine maintainers, 
maintenance officers, staff officers, and commanders at all levels the ability to determine overall 
readiness “at a glance” and provide the predictive information required to make data driven 
operational and maintenance decisions. Additionally, a maintainer level dashboard that includes 
fault codes, operational data, and troubleshooting assistance will be made available to 
maintainers in the Microsoft Azure Cloud environment to enable immediate action.  
 
MARCORSYSCOM anticipates the following applies to any future contract action related to 
CBM+.  Solutions function as intended for an indefinite period, without additional cost to the 
customer (e.g., subscription fees). The government shall retain all rights to models, source code, 
and visualizations created in the provided cloud environment.  All data and information input 
into the solution by the customer remains the sole property of the customer.  The customer (or its 
qualified, authorized representative, subject to an appropriate non-disclosure agreement) will 
have the technical data necessary to validate and verify the solution’s output and is both legally 
and contractually permitted to do so.  The customer does not typically have authorization to 
reverse engineer the product for purposes inconsistent with the purchase contract terms, unless 
pursuant to law or regulation. 
 
To register for this virtual event, Email the attached registration form to Thomas Hale at 
thomas.hale@usmc.mil. Registrations must be received by the closing time of 5:00 p.m. EST 29 
December 2026. A Microsoft Teams invite will be distributed to industry partners who respond 
by this deadline.  
 
Please note: Check over your submission to ensure your email and telephone numbers are 
correct. You will NOT receive an invitation to attend if your email is incorrect. In the event of 

unforeseen technical issues, the virtual session may be cancelled, and a copy of the slide deck 
will be provided to the registered attendees following completion of the event. 
 
Please include: 
 
• Your name and contact information, including (to the extent applicable): organization 
name, name and title of point of contact, phone number, email address, SAM.gov 
Unique Entity identification number; and website URL.  
• The offeror’s business type (large business, small business, small disadvantaged 
business, 8(a)-certified small disadvantaged business, HUBZone small business, woman-
owned small business, very small business, veteran-owned small business, service-
disabled veteran-owned small business) based upon North American Industry 
Classification System (NAICS) code 541512 - Computer Systems Design Services.  
Please refer to Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) 19 for additional detailed 
information on Small Business Size Standards.  The FAR is available at 
https://www.acquisition.gov/. 
 
Event Format: This will be a 2-hour event, which includes a 1-hour question and answer session.  
• MARCORSYSCOM CBM+ Presentation 
• Closing comments 
• 10-minute break 
• Industry Q&A  
 
Initial questions about this event may be submitted to Thomas Hale via email at 
thomas.hale@usmc.mil, no later than 9:00am 6 January 2026. All questions and answers 
addressed during this event will be posted to Sam.gov.

1 
 
 
Performance of Work Statement (PWS)  
For  
Condition Based Maintenance Plus (CBM+) 
 
1.0  Scope. 
This Performance of Work Statement (PWS) sets forth the work efforts required to procure and 
integrate data loggers with Marine Corps’ ground vehicle platforms to collect Controller Area 
Network (CAN) data, transfer data over approved networks, store, catalog, and manage data in 
government cloud repositories, apply Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) models 
and other data analytic techniques to analyze the data, and visualize the data in an easy to understand 
decision support tool so Marines can act on the information All associated logistics, technical 
information and documentation for hardware and software delivered to be delivered are documented 
in this PWS. The CBM+ capability consists of the necessary hardware, software, data analytics 
(AI/ML), data visualizations (dashboards), interfaces, and all associated buses, connectors, and 
cabling. The contractor shall provide the requisite program management, engineering, test and 
evaluation and logistics support to ensure that delivery schedules, performance requirements and 
overall supportability of the CBM+ system are accomplished as set forth in the contract.  The 
Contractor is responsible for providing all materials, services, and necessary support documentation 
needed to complete the tasks identified in this PWS.

take precedence. All second tier and below references cited in mandatory compliance shall be 
considered as guidance only. Nothing in this document, however, supersedes applicable laws and

Request for Information (RFI) 

Automated Pharmacy Prescription Dispensing Kiosks – Department of Veterans Affairs 

TAC RFI Number: 36C10B26Q0129

Purpose:

The Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) is seeking industry feedback to inform planning, acquisition strategy, and solution design for a secure, automated, pharmacy prescription dispensing kiosk that enables Veterans to receive medications prescribed by a licensed provider through a pharmacist-verified dispensing workflow to improve timely access to medications while maintaining full regulatory compliance and patient safety. This Request for Information (RFI) seeks responses from Vendors interested in supporting an initial pilot implementation, with the potential to expand to a full-scale deployment, and demonstrating the ability to deliver a unified solution that supports ongoing operations, maintenance, and enterprise-wide adoption of the product.

This RFI is issued for information and planning purposes only and does not constitute a solicitation nor does it restrict the Government as to the ultimate acquisition approach. In accordance with (IAW) Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) 15.101(c), responses to this notice are not offers and cannot be accepted by the Government to form a binding contract. The purpose of this RFI is to obtain market information on capable sources of supply, industry practices, and input specific to the information provided. The Government is not responsible for any cost incurred by industry in furnishing this information. All costs associated with responding to this RFI will be solely at the interested party’s expense. Not responding to this RFI does not preclude participation in any future Request for Proposals, if any is issued. Any information submitted by respondents to this RFI is strictly voluntary. All submissions become Government property and will not be returned. Any proprietary/sensitive information must be clearly marked by the vendor. 

Background:

VA pharmacies are a key resource for Veterans, family members, caregivers, and survivors, and are often required to support overall Veteran care. The VA currently delivers pharmacy services through a combination of medical center pharmacies, outpatient clinics, mail order fulfilment, and community partners (i.e. Community Care Networks).

To improve timely access to medications and enhance the Veteran experience, the VA plans to pilot and deploy automated pharmacy prescription dispensing kiosk(s) as part of a more unified and modernized pharmacy service delivery model. This effort seeks to expand secure, pharmacist-verified dispensing capabilities in strategic locations (to be determined), leveraging standardized technology workflows, and inventory management practices. The goal is to improve convenience and continuity of care through the automated system, reduce wait times and operational burden, support secure after-hours access in centralized locations including rural communities, and deliver safe, accurate, and consistent pharmacy services for Veterans. 

Performance Objectives:

Improve Veteran Access to Medications

Enable timely, convenient access to specific non-controlled, non-hazardous prescriptions that a patient may need to start right away, such as antibiotics or urgent short-term treatments, all available through a kiosk. 

Potential to support a feature that allows Veterans access to a pharmacist in real time.

Vendor must have a plan for potential positioning of their solution outdoors and potentially separate from the point of care provided (state laws do not prohibit such positioning) to enable 24/7 access by Veterans. 

Solution must have the capability to integrate (via Application Programming Interface) with VA’s Federal Electronic Health Record (Oracle Health) and existing Veterans Health Information Systems and Technology Architecture (VistA) electronic health record system.

Vendor must provide 24-hour support to resolve information technology issues.

Support Verified Provider Prescriptions

Accept and process prescriptions from any licensed and authorized prescriber for all Veterans verified within VA systems.

Ensure all prescriptions undergo appropriate pharmacist review and verification prior to dispensing.

Ensure Patient Safety, Accuracy, and Authentication

Maintain a high standard of dispensing accuracy.

Provide pharmacist oversight to ensure appropriate dosage of medications are stocked within the automated system. 

Ensure proper replenishment of medications is available and completed promptly. 

Verify patient identity using secure, reliable authentication methods to ensure medications are dispensed only to authorized recipients.

Maintain Regulatory and Policy Compliance 

Comply with all applicable federal, state, and local pharmacy laws, VA policies, and cybersecurity and privacy requirements, including Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act and VA information security standards.

Support Data Visibility and Reporting

Provide accurate and timely reporting on utilization, inventory, and system uptime.

Support Organizational Change Management

Assist with communication, training, and deployment planning across the VA network.

Help advertise the existence among local Veteran populations.

Requested Information:

Vendors are encouraged to provide the following:

A brief capability statement, including relevant experience. Please do not include generic marketing materials.

Footprint of the device.

Specific requirements for operation (power, connectivity, etc.).

Recommended approach to achieving full scalability VA-wide utilizing the above objectives, including:

Operational Plan detailing a unified enterprise-ready solution.

Proposed approach to adhere to varying state mandates and approach to obtain waivers, if necessary. 

Proposed approach to integrating solution with VA’s Federal Electronic Health Record (Oracle Health solution) and VistA electronic health record system.

Proposed approach for monitoring and maintenance to include software updates and mechanical repair.

Examples of automated pharmacy prescription or other medical dispensing kiosk deployment.

Proposed approach to providing remote pharmacist access.

Describe system operations and maintenance, to include:

Overall inventory capacity as well as inventory at the unit-dose and bottle level, including real time counts. 

Describe how the system manages lot numbers, expiration dates, and recalls.

Proposed approach to maintaining appropriate inventory.

Proposed approach to Veteran verification, to include:

Describe security controls to prevent unauthorized access, theft, or removal of medication without a verified prescription. 

Key risks and mitigations VA should consider

High-level cost and schedule considerations

Innovative ideas or technologies VA should explore 

Other considerations the VA should consider regarding implementation of a pilot program to full scale deployment.

Submittal Information:

This is a RFI only. It is requested that all companies interested in participating in this effort note their interest and provide an indication of their respective capabilities to perform the effort described above. Marketing material will not be accepted as an indication of capability. The vendor must display that their solution is tested and has been successfully implemented in a healthcare setting. Only responses related to the Government’s requirements as listed above will be considered. 

Provide the following information on the first page/slide of the submission:

Name of Company

Address

Point(s) of Contact

Phone Number(s)

Fax Number

Email addresses

Company Business Size and Status

NAICS code(s) – If applicable, identify your company’s Small Business size status based upon the applicable NAICS code of 541512. For more information refer to http://www.sba.gov/.

Socioeconomic data

Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) Number

The vendor shall submit their response no later than February 2, 2026 at 12 PM ET.  Submissions should not exceed 15 pages. Questions shall be submitted no later than January 21, 2026 5 PM ET. Responses and questions are to be sent via email to Andrea Caltabilota at Andrea.Caltabilota@va.gov, Nicole Bestreski at Nicole.Bestreski@va.gov, and Robert Kirzow at Robert.Kirzow@va.gov. Please include " Automated Pharmacy Prescription Dispensing Kiosks " in the subject line of your response. Mark your response as "Proprietary Information" if the information is considered business sensitive. 

NO MARKETING MATERIALS ARE ALLOWED AS PART OF THIS RFI.

The Government will only review what is being requested under the “Submittal Information” section of this RFI. The Government will not review any other information or attachments included that exceed this limit.

DISCLAIMER

This RFI is issued solely for information and planning purposes only and does not constitute a solicitation. All information received in response to this RFI that is marked as proprietary will be handled accordingly. In accordance with FAR 15.101(c)(2), responses to this notice are not offers and cannot be accepted by the Government to form a binding contract. Responders are solely responsible for all expenses associated with responding to this RFI.

GENERAL INFORMATION

PLACE OF PERFORMANCE

ADDITIONAL INFORMATION

DESCRIPTION

Please see the attached Request for Information entitled, “RFI Automated Pharmacist Prescription Dispensing Kiosks.” If interested, please respond in accordance to information referenced in the RFI.

See attached document: RFI Automated Pharmacy Prescription Dispensing Kiosks.

SECTION SF 30 BLOCK 14 CONTINUATION PAGE 

        

SUMMARY OF CHANGES  

SECTION A - SOLICITATION/CONTRACT FORM 

The following have been added by full text: 

        ANSWERING VENDOR QUESTIONS

Addressing Questions and Answers for the Vendors

Q1: Section 2.0 requires all pages to be numbered with volume, paragraph and page numbers. Generally there are multiple paragraphs on a single page. Does the government wish the paragraph to be the first full paragraph or last full paragraph incorporated into the page number?

A1: If the question is understood correctly, in the sentence referred to in Section L, Part A,  2.0, is not referring to the actual page numbering of the proposal.  The Government is requesting that volume numbers, paragraph numbers, and page numbers used on all pages. 

Q2: Table G-1 does not appear to have file naming conventions as discussed in the paragraph above. Will the government provide discussed file naming conventions?

A2: The Government shall remove "The Offeror shall follow the basic structure of the file naming convention in Table G-1 (Offeror Electronic File Naming Convention) for electronic file submissions (e.g., proposal volumes, attachments, appendices), where “#” represents the sequential numbering of files. Offeror’s shall keep file names short (i.e., under 40 characters) so that the files are accessible on Government share drives. Files with longer names often become corrupt or inaccessible." Change made in this amendment.

Q3: Will the government adjust the Equivalent Categories to reflect the SCA occupational categories of 23000 - Mechanics and Maintenance and Repair Occupations, rather than the 30000 - Technical Occupations?

A3: The Diesel Engine Specialist has a SCA code of 23530.

Q4: Does the government have requirements beyond the SCA job descriptions for degrees, years of experience, etc. for the LCATS listed?

A4: The degree, years of experience, etc, are as stated in the Statement of Work Section 8.0. The contractor shall complete attachment L-3.

Q5: Attachment L-8 Vol 5 CA-1 Business Systems Status does not match the Table on page 114, section 5. Volume 5 - Contract Administration of the Solicitation. 1.	Will the government update and re-issue Attachment L-8 to reflect the information that is to be provided in Volume 5 pertaining to Business Systems?

A5: See corrected attachment in this amendment.

Q6: Will the government extend the due date for this solicitation?

A6: The Government will extend the close date to January 5, 2026 at 12PM PST.

Q7: If a past performance has no CPARS, we have to submit the L-4 form to our government contracting POCs, to then send back to you. The L-4 form is a form to acquiring CPARS. Therefore, the Prime contractor would receive CPARS for the L-4 form submission. Does this mean that bidders cannot use subcontractor Past Performance? (Subcontractors cannot receive CPARS)

If subcontractor Past Performance is allowed, can the government send a questionnaire that we can send to our Prime contractors Program Manager directly?

A7: Subcontractor Past Performance is allowed. If Attachment L-4 does not apply, Attachment L-5 can be used.

to:

        TABLE OF CONTENTS

Exhibit/Attachment Table of Contents

 

SECTION L - INSTRUCTIONS, CONDITIONS AND NOTICES TO BIDDERS 

The following have been modified: 

        SECTION L

L-1 CONTENT OF PROPOSALS (SERVICES)

PART A   GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS

1.0	GENERAL

Offerors are required to submit sufficient information concerning all evaluation factors to enable Government personnel to fully assess the capabilities of the Offeror to perform all requirements.  The proposal must be sufficient in detail and scope to enable evaluation and provide the evaluators a clear understanding of the Offeror’s approach, expertise, and capability as required by the solicitation.  Proposals must demonstrate that the Offeror has a thorough understanding of all requirements and associated risks and is able, willing, and competent to devote the resources necessary to meet all requirements.  The Offeror shall not alter or rearrange the solicitation.  The burden of proof for all substantiation within the proposal rests with the Offeror.  Offerors are advised that the Government may incorporate any part of the Offeror’s proposal deemed beneficial to the Government, into the final contract, with or without discussions.  Cost/Price information, (e.g., dollar values and/or hourly rates) shall appear only in Volume 4 (Cost/Price). 

  

In presenting material in the proposal, the Offeror is advised that quality of information is more important than quantity.  Clarity, brevity, and logical organization should be emphasized during proposal preparation.  It is the responsibility of the Offeror to present enough information to allow the Government to evaluate its proposal without discussions.  Unsubstantiated statements that the prospective Offeror understands, can or will comply with the specifications, or paraphrasing the requirements or parts thereof are considered inadequate and may render a rating of unacceptable.  

The Offeror shall include any data necessary to illustrate the adequacy of the various assumptions, approaches, and solutions to problems.  There is no need to repeat information in more than one volume.  The detailed information must be included in the most logical place and summarized and referenced in other areas if an overlap exists.  Do not provide elaborate brochures or other presentation material beyond that sufficient to present a complete and effective proposal.  

Throughout these instructions, “Offeror” is defined as the prime contractor with its CAGE code identified in Block 15A on Standard Form (SF) 33, Solicitation, Offer, and Award.  A “Joint Venture” (JV) is a partnership or teaming arrangement that is formed for the purpose of responding to this solicitation when the prime contractor consists of more than one legal entity.  “JV Team Members” are the entities that make up a Joint Venture.  The term “entity” is used to mean any or all of the following: prime contractor, JV, JV team members, any subcontractor, and corporate parent, division, subsidiary, or affiliate (e.g., any contractor with a different CAGE Code/DUNS than the Offeror).  The following entities are defined as principal entities and only principal entities will be used in the past performance evaluations: (a) the prime contractor; (b) JV Team Members; and (c) other entities that are proposed to perform at least 15% of the proposed total cost/price for the contract. Offerors are advised that in order to demonstrate work commitment, each principal entity must be identified in Volume 4 Cost/Price and associated with a commensurate dollar value.

2.0	PROPOSAL FORMAT AND SUBMISSION

Offerors shall format all proposals using a Times New Roman 12 point normal font (no reduction permitted), single-spaced.  The Offeror shall number all pages with volume, paragraph, and page numbers.  If the Offeror uses graphs to convey information, the graphs must show enough detail to understand the information described.  The Offeror shall present graphs and tables in no smaller than a 10 point font.  

Electronic Submission of Proposal

All volumes of the proposal shall be submitted electronically through the Solicitation Module of the Procurement Integrated Enterprise Environment (PIEE) at https://piee.eb.mil. No other submissions, such as mail, hand-carried, or other electronic system (e.g., DOD SAFE) are authorized by the solicitation unless specifically authorized by the Contracting Officer pursuant to the paragraphs regarding PIEE System outages below. For instructions on how to post an offer, please refer to the Posting Offer demo: https://pieetraining.eb.mil/wbt/sol/Posting_Offer.pdf.

It is the Offeror’s responsibility to follow the registration instructions found on the PIEE website. It is advised that all potential prime Offerors and their subcontractors ensure the proper company points of contact are registered in the site based on their CAGE codes and have the proper roles assigned well in advance of the solicitation closing date. Subcontractors are only required to register in PIEE if they want to submit their proprietary proposal information separate from the prime offer. Documents submitted by the subcontractor directly to the Government must have the prime contractor’s name, CAGE, and RFP number on the first page of the document.

It is also the Offeror’s responsibility to confirm receipt of proposals and all electronic communications. Screen shots of the submission should also be taken to validate a submission was accepted in the PIEE system against this solicitation. The Government is not obligated to search for incorrectly submitted proposals in PIEE.

The submission date for all Volumes shall be no later than the date and time specified in Block 9 of the SF 33 of the RFP. 

If there is an unanticipated PIEE System outage within 24 hours of the proposal due date and the outage has interrupted normal Government processes so that proposals cannot be received by the exact time specified in the solicitation, the Contractor shall immediately notify the Contracting Officer. This notification shall occur prior to the proposal submission deadline and shall be made in writing. The notification may be in conjunction with verbal notification, but verbal notification alone shall not be sufficient. The Offeror shall obtain written approval from the Contracting Officer to submit the proposal via an alternate method as shown in the paragraphs below or the Contracting Officer may advise the Offeror that the Government will follow the procedures set forth in FAR 15.208(d) for amending the solicitation closing date.

The following alternate methods may be utilized when there is a PIEE system outage and the Offeror receives authorization from the Contracting Officer:

Contractor Proposals Submitted by Carrier: The Offeror shall submit electronic CD-ROM proposals via United States Postal Service or through a commercial carrier with next day delivery using the address provided below.

Name: 	Erika Martin

Phone:	 760-793-3151

Email address:    Erika.m.martin6.civ@us.navy.mil

U.S. Postal Service Mailing Address:

		COMMANDER

		CODE D242000 (E. Martin – 760-793-3151)

		NAVAIRWARCENWPNDIV

		1 Administration Circle, MAIL STOP 1303

		CHINA LAKE, CA  93555-6108

Direct Delivery Address (UPS, FedEx, etc.):

		COMMANDER

		CODE D242000 (E. Martin)

		NAVAIRWARCENWPNDIV

		BLDG 02334, MAIL STOP 1303

		CHINA LAKE, CA 93555-6108

Solicitation Number: N6893625R0026

Hand Carried Proposals: Hand carried proposals must be delivered to the address above, attention Contracting Officer, Erin Strand, and/or Contract Specialist, Erika Martin, 760-793-3151. If a proposal or amendment is hand carried, the Contractor must have current base access for delivery of the proposal. Without base access, the Contractor may not get beyond the installation security gate to deliver its proposal. Guards are not authorized to accept proposals. Each Volume shall be on a separate CDROM. Delivery time shall be coordinated with the contracting office to ensure availability to sign for the package.

Regardless of which alternate method is used, the Offeror shall package the CD-ROMs in the most efficient manner possible grouping like volumes to the maximum extent possible. Each Volume shall be on a separate CDROM. The package shall include all proposal volumes including the digitally signed document(s) submitted as part of the Cost/Price Volume. The package shall include a packing slip detailing the contents to include the volume number, title, and copy number. Also, the package shall be stamped or marked “Controlled Unclassified Information” and “Source Selection Information – See FAR 2.101 and 3.104.”

Electronic Proposal Media

The Offeror shall submit all volumes of its proposal electronically and ensure compatibility with the operating system and applications defined in Section 2.1.2 below.  The Offeror shall keep the portions of the proposal that contain pricing information separate from the portions of the proposal that do not contain pricing information.  For this solicitation, only Volume 4 Cost/Price shall contain cost/price information and shall be delivered in separate files from the remainder of the proposal.  The Offeror is responsible for ensuring electronic proposals are virus free in accordance with Section 2.1.4.  

Operating System and Applications

The Government uses Microsoft Windows 11 Operating System to access proposals.  Offerors shall submit proposal information using the following applications: Microsoft Word 2016, Microsoft Excel 2016, and Microsoft PowerPoint 2016. However, Offerors shall submit letters and other signed documents as a fully searchable Portable Document Format (.pdf) file.  Use of other application software for submission of proposals is prohibited. 

Hyperlinks

The Offeror shall not use hyperlinks in proposal volumes. 

Virus Free Electronic Submission

The Offeror is responsible for ensuring that its electronic proposal is virus free. The Offeror shall certify, in its proposal cover letter, that all electronic proposal information delivered throughout the evaluation (e.g., original proposal submission, responses to evaluation notices, Final Proposal Revisions) are virus free. The Offeror shall specify the software, version, and virus definition used to check the data

Multimedia

In order to reduce file sizes, the Offeror shall not embed sound or video (e.g., MPEG) files within the proposal submission. The Offeror is encouraged to simplify the color palette used in creating figures; and minimize size of graphics files; and avoid scanned images. 

File Naming Conventions

Valid extensions for files using their associated applications follow: 

The Offeror shall follow the basic structure of the file naming convention in Table G-1 (Offeror Electronic File Naming Convention) for electronic file submissions (e.g., proposal volumes, attachments, appendices), where “#” represents the sequential numbering of files.  Offeror’s shall keep file names short (i.e., under 40 characters) so that the files are accessible on Government share drives.  Files with longer names often become corrupt or inaccessible.

The Offeror should provide one folder for each volume or annex, containing all electronic files requested within the volume or annex. Please submit each volume, or annex as one file if possible. Offerors should not provide each section/paragraph as separate files.

The following do not count toward page count: Cover pages, Title pages, Table of Contents pages, Resumes.

NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT
Protected Critical Infrastructure Information (PCII)
Agreement in consideration of my being granted conditional access to certain information, specified below, that is owned 
by, produced by, or in the possession of the United States Government.
(Signer will acknowledge the category or categories of information that he or she may have access to, and the signer's willingness to 
comply with the standards for protection by placing his or her initials in front of the applicable category or categories.)
Initials:
I attest that I am familiar with, and I will comply with all requirements of the PCII program set out in the Critical 
Infrastructure Information Act of 2002 (CII Act) (Title II, Subtitle B, of the Homeland Security Act of 2002, Public Law 
107-296, 196 Stat. 2135, 6 USC 101 et seq.), as amended, the implementing regulations thereto (6 CFR Part 29), as 
amended, and the applicable PCII Procedures Manual, as amended, and with any such requirements that may be 
officially communicated to me by the PCII Program Manager or the PCII Program Manager's designee.
Sensitive Security Information (SSI)
Initials:
I attest that I am familiar with, and I will comply with the standards for access, dissemination, handling, and safeguarding 
of SSI information as cited in this Agreement and in accordance with 49 CFR Part 1520, "Protection of Sensitive Security 
Information," "Policies and Procedures for Safeguarding and Control of SSI," as amended, and any supplementary 
guidance issued by an authorized official of the Department of Homeland Security.
Other Sensitive but Unclassified (SBU)
Initials:
As used in this Agreement, sensitive but unclassified information is an over-arching term that covers any information, not 
otherwise indicated above, which the loss of, misuse of, or unauthorized access to or modification of could adversely 
affect the national interest or the conduct of Federal programs, or the privacy to which individuals are entitled under 
Section 552a of Title 5, as amended, but which has not been specifically authorized under criteria established by an 
Executive Order or an Act of Congress to be kept secret in the interest of national defense or foreign policy. This includes 
information categorized by DHS or other government agencies as: For Official Use Only (FOUO); Official Use Only 
(OUO); Sensitive Homeland Security Information (SHSI); Limited Official Use (LOU); Law Enforcement Sensitive (LES); 
Safeguarding Information (SGI); Unclassified Controlled Nuclear Information (UCNI); and any other identifier used by 
other government agencies to categorize information as sensitive but unclassified.
I attest that I am familiar with, and I will comply with the standards for access, dissemination, handling, and safeguarding 
of the information to which I am granted access as cited in this Agreement and in accordance with the guidance provided 
to me relative to the specific category of information.
I understand and agree to the following terms and conditions of my access to the information indicated above:
3. I attest that I understand my responsibilities and that I am familiar with and will comply with the standards for protecting 
such information that I may have access to in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and the laws, regulations, and/
or directives applicable to the specific categories of information to which I am granted access. I understand that the United 
States Government may conduct inspections, at any time or place, for the purpose of ensuring compliance with the 
conditions for access, dissemination, handling and safeguarding information under this Agreement.

1. I hereby acknowledge that I have received a security indoctrination concerning the nature and protection of information

8. If I violate the terms and conditions of this Agreement, such violation may result in the cancellation of my conditional

4. I will not disclose or release any information provided to me pursuant to this Agreement without proper authority or

5. (a) For PCII - (1) Upon the completion of my engagement as an employee, consultant, or subcontractor under the

(2) If the Authorized Entity is a United States Government contractor performing services in support of the PCII Program, I

(b) For SSI and SBU - I hereby agree that material which I have in my possession and containing information covered by 
this Agreement, will be handled and safeguarded in a manner that affords sufficient protection to prevent the unauthorized

6. I hereby agree that I will not alter or remove markings, which indicate a category of information or require specific

category of information or, in the case of PCII, unless such alteration or removal is authorized by the PCII Program 
Manager or the PCII Program Manager's designee. I agree that if I use information from a sensitive document or other 
medium, I will carry forward any markings or other required restrictions to derivative products, and will protect them in the

7. I hereby agree that I shall promptly report to the appropriate official, in accordance with the guidance issued for the 
applicable category of information, any loss, theft, misuse, misplacement, unauthorized disclosure, or other security 
violation, I have knowledge of and whether or not I am personally involved. I also understand that my anonymity will be

9. (a) With respect to SSI and SBU, I hereby assign to the United States Government all royalties, remunerations, and 
emoluments that have resulted, will result, or may result from any disclosure, publication, or revelation of the information 
not consistent with the terms of this Agreement.
(b) With respect to PCII I hereby assign to the entity owning the PCII and the United States Government, all royalties, 
remunerations, and emoluments that have resulted, will result, or may result from any disclosure, publication, or revelation

10. This Agreement is made and intended for the benefit of the United States Government and may be enforced by the

the category of information involved and neither the United States Government nor the Authorized Entity have waived any

proceeding to protect any sensitive information to which I have been given conditional access under the terms of this 
Agreement.

permissible for the particular category of information), I understand that all conditions and obligations imposed upon me 
by this Agreement apply during the time that I am granted conditional access, and at all times thereafter.
12. Each provision of this Agreement is severable. If a court should find any provision of this Agreement to be 
unenforceable, all other provisions shall remain in full force and effect.
13. My execution of this Agreement shall not nullify or affect in any manner any other secrecy or non-disclosure

14. These provisions are consistent with and do not supersede, conflict with, or otherwise alter the employee obligations,

TSA PRE-SOLICITATION NOTIFICATION

The Transportation Security Administration (TSA), a component of the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), is responsible for protecting our Nation’s transportation systems to ensure freedom of movement for people and commerce. These statutory responsibilities encompass providing the most effective transportation security in the most efficient way possible as a high-performing counterterrorism organization. To enhance mission performance and achieve its shared goals, TSA is committed to promoting a culture founded on integrity, innovation, and team spirit.

In support of TSA’s mission, the Information Technology (IT) office provides world-class information technology services. Field Operations & CUstomer Support (FOCUS) will establish a new Task Order to provide the TSA enterprise-wide Single Point of Contact Information Technology help desk and headquarters/field Information Technology Specialist support.

Purpose

TSA is announcing the intention to make available to interested GSA Multiple Award Schedule (GSA MAS) vendors a Virtual Reading Room (VRR), which will contain documents pertinent to the upcoming solicitation for FOCUS. The VRR will likely contain Sensitive Security Information (SSI). Therefore, access to the VRR will be restricted to vendors and individuals that have been approved by TSA. The purpose of this notice is to announce the VRR and rules regarding access to the VRR.

Requirements Background and Overview

TSA’s IT infrastructure and engineering are currently supported by the IMPACT II task order. The IMPACT II task order provides the skill sets necessary to operate and maintain the TSA IT Enterprise to include supporting engineering and development. TSA determines all development and engineering included in the enterprise to support its end-users. The TSA IT Enterprise supports approximately 85,000 employees and contractors at various locations, including TSA Headquarters (HQ) currently located in Springfield, VA; approximately 440 airports and Federal Security Director (FSD) offices; international support sites; and numerous other mission critical centers and locations. Approximately 85 percent of TSA users consist of a combination of Transportation Security Officers (TSOs) and Federal Law Enforcement Officers (LEOs), while other TSA staff includes TSA HQ employees and field security operations management and administrative su

Performance Work Statement (PWS) 
Enterprise Network and Information Security Enhancement (ENISE) 
1.   Background 
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) administers America’s tax laws and collects the revenues 
that fund most government operations and public services.  The IRS and its employees represent 
the face of government to more U.S. citizens than any other agency. 
 
IRS provides services to millions of taxpayers to help them understand and meet their tax 
obligations.  We also deter people inclined to evade their responsibilities and vigorously pursue 
those who violate tax laws.  Service and enforcement are equally important priorities for the IRS. 
We are committed both to providing excellent service to taxpayers and enforcing America’s tax 
laws in a balanced manner. 
 
Beginning in mid-2006, the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) released several 
memorandums to address protecting the vast quantities of information managed by the 
Government. OMB’s Memorandum 06-16 speaks directly to the ability to manage Sensitive but 
Unclassified (SBU)/Personally Identifiable Information (PII) extracts for legitimate use, and 
OMB M-07-16 which emphasizes protection and management of SBU/PII as a federal 
requirement. 
2.   Objective  
 
The IRS has identified cloud technology as a key support enabler: hosting and managing big data 
and enabling IT operational excellence through use of complex applications and programs. 
Cloud computing will allow the IRS to efficiently manage, operate and run a very large-scale 
computing infrastructure that delivers the capacity, availability and performance that will permit 
nearly 90,000 end users to focus on mission accomplishment while accessing more than 500 
applications and programs. 
This effort supports the Cybersecurity Information Protection Enhancement Controls (CIPEC) 
program’s mission to enhance and standardize data protection controls across the enterprise, 
safeguarding sensitive taxpayer data, and ensuring the integrity and security of the IRS systems. 
2.1   Purpose 
Given the services provided to the Taxpayers of the United States of America, the volume and 
sensitivity of Taxpayer data (e.g., IRS Employee data, IRS Affiliate Organizational Entities - 
Public, Private or Non-profit) has grown exponentially.  Associated with this reality is the 
mission-critical need to protect this PII and SBU Information and guard against the increased 
occurrence of Enterprise Data (or Privacy) breaches and identity theft. 
 

  
 
2 
 
Privacy breach1 and identity theft are among the most prevalent and publicized cyber-crimes in 
the news today. According to the Open Security Foundation, which hosts “DataLossDB” a 
database which records unauthorized Personally Identifiable Information (PII) incidents.  
 
Despite increased awareness, data privacy legislation, federal guidance, as well as the 
implementation of Agency-specific policies and security awareness training programs to help 
guard against the unauthorized disclosure of and access to Agency SBU information and PII, the 
inadvertent disclosure of this data continues day to day by well-meaning and dedicated 
employees. 
2.2   Agency Mission  
Considering these trends over the past quarter of a century, the U.S. Department of the Treasury 
and the IRS recognized the need for the implementation of a comprehensive system to: 
 
 Accurately discover or prevent SBU/PII data leakage. 
 Maintain the confidentiality of SBU/PII being stored and used by the IRS. 
 Ensure public trust in conducting business with the IRS electronically (e.g., IRS 
Modernized eFile (MEF)system). 
 Prevent unauthorized disclosure of SBU/PII being stored and used by the IRS. 
 
As an element of the Treasury Department’s ongoing efforts to improvement its Cybersecurity 
program, the Office of the Chief Information Officer (CIO) provided additional controls, in its 
memorandum “TCIO-M-09-04”, related to the management of Treasury’s Cybersecurity 
environment. These new controls focus particularly on the storage of data on removable media 
and the unauthorized transmission of information outside the Department.  
3.   Scope of Work 
The purpose of the ENISE Project is to augment the IRS CIPEC Program’s DLP Technical team 
with experienced Cloud Technical, Federal Information Security Management Act (FISMA) 
Compliance, and Program support Subject Matter Experts (SMEs), who have successfully 
supported the implementation/integration of an “on prem” Enterprise DLP Commercial-Off-The-
Shelf (COTS) Solution within an Enterprise Agency’s Hybrid Cloud Ecosystem – primarily 
utilizing the COTS Cloud product offerings of Microsoft, Skyhigh, Broadcom, Palo Alto etc.  
 
The primary goal of this acquisition is to ensure the continued provision of trained and certified 
Information Technology (IT) professionals with extensive experience in developing and 
implementing Cloud Migration projects. This effort supports the CIPEC program’s mission to 
enhance and standardize data protection controls across the enterprise, safeguarding sensitive 
taxpayer data, and ensuring the integrity and security of the IRS systems. Additionally, the 
government seeks to future-proof its technical environment by integrating emerging technologies 
and solutions. It also aims to explore innovative mechanisms to incorporate artificial intelligence 
 
1 The term “breach” is also defined by OMB memoranda M-07-16,: “Safeguarding Against and Responding to the Breach 
of Personally Identifiable Information”, May 22, 2007, as: “For the purposes of this policy, the term “breach” is 
used to include the loss of control, compromise, unauthorized disclosure, unauthorized acquisition, unauthorized 
access, or any similar term referring to situations where persons other than authorized users and for an other than authorized 
purpose have access or potential access to personally identifiable information, whether physical or electronic.” 

  
 
3 
 
and machine learning (AI/ML) technologies to further enhance operational capabilities and 
efficiencies. 
 
The ENISE Project will be implemented as a multi-year multiple-release effort. Although the “on 
prem” and “Cloud” solutions are intended to be functionally equivalent, the Architecture, 
Infrastructure, Configuration, Security Compliance model and Implementation approach required 
for the successful implementation of the IRS DLP in the Cloud solution is to be fundamentally 
different.   
 
The IRS Business Units and IT organizations rely on the CIPEC to provide Subject Matter 
Expertise relating to the implementation and operational support of Broadcom, Inc’s, Symantec 
DLP Suite COTS solution and Cloud Computing, related to the specific solutions the IRS has 
already acquired and have implemented.  This requisite expertise that the DCM ENISE Project 
must continue to provide, is associated with related competencies including: solution 
architecture/engineering, requirements elicitation, project management techniques and 
approaches, cloud solution FISMA security & privacy compliance, Cloud DLP systems and 
database administration, data leakage event troubleshooting, solution DLP detection rule 
development, detection rule triggers & configurable exceptions, systems upgrade work for 
solutions of various vendors, event management and triaging workflow development, role based 
access management and control.  In addition to protecting the IRS from data exfiltration the 
ENISE project will also be configuring Palo Alto Cloud Hosted Security Stack to enhance the 
organizational network secure posture. To support the DCM ENISE project needs, while 
maintaining a cost-effective price structure, the PWS will include Firm Fixed Price (FFP) tasks.

3.1   Current Information Technology (IT)/Network Environment  
In 2013, the SPIIDE Project was initiated and the IRS Cybersecurity A&I’s DLP Program 
Management Office (PMO) was stood-up to execute the implementation of a Commercial-Off-
The-Shelf DLP solution.   
 
The IRS DLP solution was implemented in two separate software releases: 
 Release 1.0 focused on implementing the first of three Functional Uses Case components: 
Data-In-Motion (DIM).  The Data-In-Motion (DIM) component, implemented in the IRS 
Production Operational environment, monitors the presence unencrypted PII or SBU data 
in outbound email messages and posted to public-facing IRS Web Sites.  Using this IRS 
DLP COTS component, certain instances of unencrypted PII and SBU data is detected, 
their transmission outside of the IRS Network perimeter is blocked and the responsible 
IRS employee is notified by an auto-generated email response.   
 Release 2.0 focused on implementing the final two of three Functional Uses Case 
components: Data-At-Rest (DAR) and Data-In-Use (DIU).  Data-At-Rest (DAR) 
component specifically scans fileshares, SAN/NAS storage, SharePoint, Websites, 
Databases, etc. for files containing sensitive content (such as PII, PCI, HIPAA, etc.) 
and/or other content/context including age of the file, file types, encrypted files, etc. The 
deployment scope of the DIU component, via use of endpoint agents locally installed on 
enterprise workstations (Laptops) and on servers, monitors and responds to the presence 
of PII & SBU Data (currently configured as DLP policies). 
4.   Tasks 
Outlined in this PWS is a detailed description of the professional services that the selected 
Contractor shall provide to the ENISE Project, to support the continued instantiation of the IRS 
enterprise DLP solution in the emerging IRS Cloud ecosystem. This effort also includes the 
deployment and configuration of Palo Alto Next Gen Firewall (NGFW) technologies, as well as 
related activities (e.g., authoring required OneSDLC documentation, participation in established 
Integrated Project Teams (IPTs) and/or establishing a new IPT for the DLP in the Cloud effort, 
support for IRS FISMA Annual Security Compliance Assessments (ASCA). 
 
The following tasks are the high-level summary of the scope for this Performance Work 
Statement: 
 
1. CLIN 0001 
 Task 01 – Solution Delivery Support

Implementation & Operations Support  
 Task 03 - On Prem” DLP Detection Rules Integration 
 Task 04 - Critical Gap Analysis Between the On-Prem and Cloud Hosted DLP 
Solutions for Implementation  
2. CLIN 0002 
 Task 01 - Palo Alto NGFW Proxy Configuration Implementation 
 Task 02 - Web Application and API Security Deployment 
 Task 03 - Threat Detection and Cloud Detection & Response Implementation 
 Task 04 - Data Security Posture Management (DSPM) Development 
 Task 05 - Prisma Access Browser Architecture and Deployment 
 Task 06 - Enterprise DLP Configuration with IRS Policies 
 Task 07 - AI/ML Augmentation and Enhancement in E-DLP 
 Task 08 - Strata Cloud Manager Configuration

4.1 CLIN 0001 
4.1.1 TASK 1 –SOLUTION DELIVERY SUPPORT 
4.1.1.1   SUBTASK 1 – ORIENTATION /PROJECT KICK -OFF BRIEFING 
Within two weeks of Task Order award, the contractor shall conduct an orientation briefing for 
the Government. The Government does not desire an elaborate orientation briefing, nor does it 
expect the contractor to expend significant resources in preparation for this briefing. Rather, the 
intent of the briefing is to initiate the communication process between the Government and 
contractor by introducing key Task Order participants, explaining their roles, reviewing 
communication ground rules, and assuring a common understanding of Task Order requirements 
and objectives. 
 
The orientation briefing shall be held virtually via Microsoft Teams and both parties shall 
mutually agree upon the date and time. The completion of this briefing shall result in the 
following: 
 The contractor and Government personnel who will perform work under this Task Order 
will be introduced. 
 The Government will show the applicable facilities to the contractor if the contractor will 
be performing work on the Government's site. 
 The Government may provide any Government Furnished Property (GFP) to the 
contractor at this time. 
 Any issues concerning the contractor clearances for contractor personnel will be 
discussed.   
 Government will provide instructions for their personnel on how to obtain Government 
Furnished Equipment (GFE) laptops with Enterprise Remote Access Project (ERAP) 

  
 
6 
 
access capabilities to allow contractor personnel to work at alternate locations during 
inclement weather or other authorized periods, if needed. 
 The contractor shall demonstrate confirmation of their understanding of the work to be 
accomplished under this PWS. 
 
4.1.1.2   SUBTASK 2 –COORDINATION OF SOLUTION DELIVERY ACTIVITIES AND GOVERNANCE 
PROCESSES 
The contractor shall provide support for the planning strategizing for project initiatives and 
project engagement. The contractor shall provide documentation, reporting, and briefing support 
as required. The contractor shall coordinate project and program activities and governance 
processes. The contractor shall coordinate, facilitate, monitor, and support interfaces with other 
IRS projects, initiatives, and programs to ensure cohesiveness between all IRS security 
initiatives. The contractor will provide acquisition support for supplemental solution planning 
and integration to address risks, limitations, and gaps. The contractor will support FISMA, OMB, 
Treasury Inspector General for Tax Administration (TIGTA), and Government Accountability 
Office (GAO) requests or requirements. The contractor will coordinate with Cyber and Privacy 
stakeholders, as needed, to ensure that all required tasks are met.
